One hour ago I launched my book, The Trade."
"Ten minutes ago, it became an Amazon #1 Bestseller."
""
"Thank you to Stan McChrystal, founder of the McChrystal Group, and author of three NY Times Bestsellers, for the incredible support of my book, The Trade. Everything about you has been world-class. Everything. "
""
"Thank you Ryan Moran, founder of Capitalism. com an Best Selling author of 12 Months to $1 Million, for your support, your endorsement, and for writing the incredibly kind forward to my book."
""
"This is the best work of my lifetime. "
"I did not write it to get something out of me. "
"I wrote it to finally get something out of you."
""
"You are here because you are ready. "
"I'm here to prove it to you."
""
"My goal is to be a NY Times Bestseller. A ridiculous goal for a self-published book. But that's no reason not to try. "
""
"Buy The Trade on Amazon, today."
"Live The Trade starting tomorrow."
""
"Welcome to the Normal 40."
"It starts today. "
""
"#thetrade"

I am exhausted, but I owe you an update."
""
"Our little book, the one without a major label or a marketing budget, is kicking ass. Currently, we are #1 in EVERY category we compete in—sort of amazing for a book on day one, with zero reviews. "
""
"We're #8 in Amazon Business Decision Making to make it even more interesting. This is where the million-dollar labels and the people with 40,000 Amazon reviews hang out. But there's The Trade at # 8, with zero ad spend and zero reviews. "
""
"I can't thank you enough for all of your help with #thetrade. I'll update you again tomorrow. "
""
"Well, I think we have their attention. "
"Welcome to #thetrade. "
"This is the #normal40.

Warning — this is not my usual post. You’ve been warned."
""
"I was alone in my study, drinking. My kids and wife were in bed. I was numbing."
""
"It was June 2021, and I was lost. Stuck between outward success and something inward I couldn’t name. I was about to close the deal of a lifetime, but deep down, I knew—my life’s biggest deal was not my life’s best work."
""
"And then, out of nowhere, she appeared."
""
"A young woman I had never met walked into my living room. Thin. Fragile. But unshaken."
""
"She stood in front of the world, facing something most of us spend our lives trying to avoid—the shortness of life. That dreams don’t wait. That no one is promised tomorrow. And that waiting for it to be easy isn’t an option. And that for things to change, sometimes you have to muster the courage to change it."
""
"I sat in my study fighting back tears—tears of seeing someone refuse to wait, push through pain, and live without fear of regret."
""
"That was the moment Nightbirde (Jane) reminded me that it’s okay to have a dream, but it’s not OK to let it die with you."
""
"This was the day I started—the day I started dreaming again, the day I started posting, connecting, and building, the day I permitted myself to start."
""
"During rambles, I often ask, “What makes you cry?” Most don’t like to answer, but that answer holds more truth about who you are than anything else."
""
"Sometimes, they ask me back—what makes me cry…"
""
"I will tell them about Nightbirde and share this video (below)."
""
"A few months ago, I sent a note to Nightbirde’s brother, Mitch Marczewski, simply to express my thanks. I didn’t expect a reply, but I got one. Then we rambled. Then we dreamed. Since then, we’ve become friends."
""
"Mitch runs The Nightbirde Foundation, which gives hope to those on their darkest days—hope for life, hope for the chance to chase a dream, hope that it’s not too late to do their lifetime’s best work."
""
"And now, we’re teaming up on something—and you are invited."
""
"We’re capturing the incredible stories of a few women impacted by The Nightbirde Foundation—stories of life in its rawest and purest form—stories that need to be told and deserve to be heard. Stories with ripples larger than any of us."
""
"We need help. We will record live, in person, on site."
""
"If you have the equipment, the curiosity, and the heart to help us tell these stories, this is your invitation. It will pay better than anything you’ve ever done, and it will have nothing to do with money. "
""
"We have no idea where this will go, but we’re certain we won’t regret the journey. "
""
"This is what a lifetime up to something looks like."
""
"I can’t wait to see who Nightbirde impacts next."
""

This is on our family farm and ranch at 5:21 AM. "
""
"This land has been in our family my entire life, and most of my dads and his dad’s before him.  "
""
"Last night, for the first time ever, I pulled a camper out onto the prairie and I spent the night.  "
""
"This video was of the sun raise, and it’s beautiful.  But the sound of the prairie waking up will blow your mind.  It is something everyone needs to take in. "
""
"#normal40

Day 4 — The Normal 40 Ranch Retreat. "
""
"I have no words; you wouldn't believe me if I did.  "
""
"This event at the ranch was the first. My first.  I poured a lot into it.  But in the end, it was a test of me, my process, and, well, the prairie.  "
""
"I hoped that it would work. "
"I hoped that lives would be touched."
"I hoped that the prairie would open up and let something in.  "
""
"And, well, it did.  "
""
"Fifteen people got to see the ranch from a whole new angle, walk the paths, and connect with something inside that had been dormant for years."
""
"But most of all, fifteen people have created friendships they will never forget. "
""
"Fifteen people who have a new appreciation for what it means to “be human.”"
""
"I'm on my way home today. I am exhausted—physically and emotionally. Like every other retreat participant, my life has been changed, and my mission sharpened. "
""
"And I can't wait to do it again. "
""
"Be up to something. "
""
"#normal40 #executivecoach #human

In a few hours, I start a 24-hour trek to Cusco, Peru. Where, by design, or maybe foolishness, no one is waiting for me. Other than a ticket into Machu Picchu, and a return flight next week, there is no agenda."
""
"This week marks two years since I made The Trade."
""
"My old life, for more than a decade, lived well but did not flow. I would leave home as one person, but in an instant, would transform into who I needed to be at work. Then, 10 hours later, I would transition back. Or so I thought."
""
"As I climbed, the lines blurred, and so did the nature of who I was and what I tolerated."
""
"Eventually, I had a work life, and it was good. And I had a home life, and it, too, was good. But the only relation between the two was me. Living two lives felt tangled, out of flow, and nothing was pure—always wondering how one would impact the other."
""
"In 2022, I set out in search of something pure that also pays the bills. That decision, and the year of preparation leading up to it, has led me here."
""
"Look, feeling unsatisfied about where you’re going is the worst feeling in the world. Mostly because you can’t tell anyone, but also because this is the path you chose 20 years ago. After all, who will feel sorry for you—someone who has exactly what you chased?"
""
"Well, me. Nope, actually, us—The Normal 40 Insider Community. I see the box you are in, and I know it is lonely in there. I am here to do something about it. "
""
"First, I have some bad news:"
" — You are in control of how you feel today."
""
"Sure, you can blame others. But the fact is, this one is on you."
""
"Ironically, that’s what makes it hard. If you would just get fired with a severance, at least then you could play the victim card. But leaving on your own accord, who does that?"
""
"Well, me. And thousands of others. With zero regrets. "
""
"To be clear, I’m not urging you to quit — I am encouraging you to start. To Just F*ing Do Something (#JFDS). To change something. To join something. And holy crap, maybe even reach out and talk to someone about what you feel."
""
"If you are still reading, then I’m writing this to you. It’s the message you hoped to read when you hoped to receive it. This is a note from your future self asking you to finally start, to finally see who you are capable of being, next."
""
"I started a few years ago, and I have no regrets."
""
"I’m not rich. I’m not retired. I’m not washed up. "
"I am on mission, and my work is the best of my lifetime."
"My work is pure."
""
"And in the moment you start, yours will be, too."
""
"Oh yeah, back to Peru: I will be writing from the Inca Trail. And, if my emotion right now is any indication, as I hug my family goodbye and set off, it will be some intimate writing. It won’t be for everyone; it will be for you."
""
"The best chapter of your life is here, and I invite you to find it."
""
"Join The Insider Mastermind, the Vintage One Waitlist (April launch), or the Live Event waitlist."
""
"The Box is coming down."
"Let’s be up to something."
""

I was never in an honor's program. Not in high school and certainly not in college. "
""
"In fact, on my high school graduation thank-you notes, I spelled college wrong. True story. I wrote to everyone who gave me a few bucks: "Thank you for the money. I will use it for collage.""
""
"Ugh."
""
"So, you can imagine my surprise when I was contacted by The Dean of the Honors College at South Dakota State University, Dr. Rebecca Bott. I was even more surprised when she asked me if I would be their keynote speaker at their annual convocation ceremony. But I followed the omens and immediately said yes."
""
"Look, dude, I've also done the math here."
""
"This event was on the Honor's College schedule for more than six months. So giving me six days to prepare means one thing: I was a long way down their list of desired speakers."
""
"But that's how this world works ... so I said yes, and then I showed up."
""
"The event was last week, and this is the transcript the University shared. Now I'm sharing it with you. "
""
"The theme: Advice I wish I would have had when I was a senior in college. "
""
"I was told by one of the Honors students afterward: "I've been in honors for three years, and this is the first time I've heard someone use the word "bullshit" at one of our events.""
""
"You'll have to read it to appreciate the delivery. But I stand firmly behind the application."
""
"Enjoy the #normal40 highway. And don't forget to show up. "
""
"No matter where your name is on the list, show up for the call.  "
""
"Today: Show up, then own it. "
""
"#normal40"

"In a moment, what I was, I was not.""
""With that statement, he ended the direction of my life ... I was 56.""
""
"Last week I spent some time with former general Stan McChrystal. It was one of the most interesting hours of my lifetime."
""
"We discussed many things, but military strategy was not one of them. "
""
"We talked about careers, challenges, and spouses. "
"We talked about first days and last days. "
"We spoke about resigning and starting over. "
"We talked about what it was like when failure finds you. "
"And we discussed how The Trade begs you to move forward. "
""
"Listen to the rest of this incredible conversation on Normal 40: The Podcast."
""
"Part 2 of this podcast will be out soon -- but this one is a must-listen."
""
"You are not alone. "
"These are your best days."
"This is your time."
"Welcome to the #normal40."
"Welcome to #thetrade. "
"#podcast #motivation #ownit McChrystal Group

Dear N.40 Highway - I have something to share."
""
"Today I will start writing the book. It wil be:"
""
" - A book I never thought I would be willing, able or qualified to write. "
""
" - A book that will be emotional, personal and sometimes scarry to write and to share."
""
" - A book about who we are, who we become and who we are capable of being - and how it changes over time."
""
" - A book about wins and losses, life on the farm, life in the boardroom and the life trades we face to get to where we are."
""
" - A book about the miles traveled, the sacrifces made and the million ways we all arrive into some stage of our life to wonder “Is this it?” "
""
" - A book about the families we love, the firends we used to have, the shortness of life and the best years of our life we have to create all it is we will leave to the world."
""
" - A book about you."
""
"I don’t yet have a title, but I have the chapters mapped and the themes created.  "
""
"From time to time I will come here to ask for help, and I would apprecaite getting it from you.  I would love to hear from you if there are questions you hope the book answers…or let me know if there’s a title you would like to suggest."
""
"Join me in the process. That’s how you too can help 1000 dudes. "
""
"This is a good day. "
""
"And I own it."

The Diagnosis:  I’m an author. But today, I’ve stared at this screen for two hours."
""
"Not because of writer’s block. Because sometimes, the words I write for others are the ones I need for myself."
""
"I saw an old friend yesterday. Actually, a young friend."
"Tim Schut is just 48 years old. He is a friend I’ve known for 30 years. A friend I feel lucky to know and was fortunate to see."
""
"Until a few years ago, life for Tim looked a lot like life for many of us: school, marriage, kids, career, goals, and success."
""
"He chased it. He caught it. He lived it. And he shared it."
""
"But Tim was always different. Always gave a little more. Always doing what was right and needed to be done."
""
"Tim had an orbit few could replicate. None of it was for show or profit."
""
"This was the only way Tim knew how to be Tim."
""
"Then came the curveball."
""
"On December 23rd, 2021, I had lunch with Tim. That’s when he told me:"
""
"“Lon, I’ve got a disease that is going to kill me slowly.”"
""
"His diagnosis was Primary Progressive Apraxia of Speech (PPAOS). His Prognosis—a long fight."
""
"PPAOS is a disease so rare it barely exists in textbooks."
"A disease that presents itself by taking his ability to speak, then balance, then…"
""
"What do you say in a situation like that?"
""
"I asked if I could ask him some hard questions—the kind you’re not supposed to ask. He was thrilled."
""
"I asked him if he was mad."
""
"He said no; being mad wouldn’t help. So, he set that emotion aside."
""
"After a few more questions, I asked what scared him most. He hesitated, took a breath, and then, through his tears, said:"
""
"“It’s my girls. I probably won’t get to see them get married. And I’ll never get to meet my grandkids.”"
""
"The air between us changed."
""
"After a few tears of my own, I told Tim this:"
""
"“We can’t control whether you get to know your grandkids. "
"But we can make damn sure they know you.”"
""
"I asked if we could document his journey—not just record it, but capture it, all of it."
""
"He said yes."
""
"A single call to SDPB (South Dakota Public Broadcasting), and they agreed to create a documentary—including travel to Mayo Clinic."
""
"We started in January 2022–the last month of my corporate job."
""
"The documentary aired. It’s incredible…I’ll put a link in the notes."
""
"But what matters most is this:"
""
"I saw Tim yesterday, and it was a gift."
""
"His voice is gone. His gate is slow. And his endurance is light."
"But his hugs are full, his energy is good, and his heart still commands an orbit."
""
"His eyes still know me. And mine still know him."
""
"Tim didn’t share his story to get famous. He wanted all of us to re-evaluation our time…becuase it’s not guaranteed."
""
"It’s a reminder: we don’t control the clock."
"We control how we live with the time we have."
""
"Tim’s light hasn’t dimmed."
"His leadership hasn’t faded."
"His impact hasn’t disappeared."
""
"He’s still teaching. Still leading. Still inspiring. Still being Tim."
""
"Turns out, he never needed his words for that."
""
"Thanks for being a guide, old friend."
""
"You are still #uptosomething."
""
"#normal40 #thetrade #jfds

"Follow the omens." Great advice to give, hard advice to follow."
" -- But here I go."
""
"There's a book I read every spring. This will be my seventh reading. "
""
"It's a book that, without question, has done more to shape my thinking than any other writing. "
""
"It's not a book for everyone--but that's why I love it."
"It's a book for someone -- someone who is ready."
"If you're here, it's a book for you."
""
"The book is The Alchemist by Paulo Coelho. And, by the way, if you listen to books, this is the best Audible recording I've ever heard."
""
"The omens, frustrating enough, are telling me to do more. They are telling me I'm ready to lead more, to be more, and to act more. They are telling me to grow."
""
"The omens are not telling me to make more money...that would be nice. "
"No, they are telling me d more to inspire the next person who needs it."
""
"And they are telling me to do it with video and audio: One to many."
""
"So, after reading a few chapters of The Alchemist this morning, I decided there was no time like the present to start stepping into something that feels uncomfortable and unnecessary."
""
"I could have waited until tomorrow. "
"I could have done a second take with less facial glare."
"I could have written a script and made this more smooth."
""
"I chose to hit record and share my story, as it was happening."
"I decided this was my time."
"I decided to #JFDS."
""
"You can judge my work for yourself. "
"Because I mustered the courage to ship it."
""
"So, what are the omens telling you? "
""
"What is the thing you know you should try but are avoiding? "
"What do you wish you had done yesterday but believe will be easier tomorrow?"
"What if you quit waiting and just did something."
""
"You're going to see more video from me."
"Not because I love it, but because I'll regret it if I don't."
"And because I feel this is the right next step for our journey."
""
"I will still be writing...but now I'll also be recording."
""
"This is a lifetime ... up to something."
"And you are invited. "
""
"#normal40 #thetrade #personaldevelopment

Tomorrow, it will be two years since I left my corporate job. "
""
"When I resigned, people thought it was foolish and probably irresponsible. And on some days, it felt like it. "
""
"I left without a resume, a list of clients, without a clear business plan, and I was not financially set."
""
"Who does that? Well, me."
"And I don’t feel like a fool."
""
"I didn’t leave because I was mad, unhappy, and cast aside."
"I didn’t leave because I couldn’t take the stress or workload."
"And I didn’t leave because my best years were behind me."
""
"I left because it was my time, and I knew it."
"I left because my future wasn’t there, and I knew it."
"I left because money was no longer a driver, and I felt it."
"I left because my life’s best work was upon me, and I knew it."
"I left because the grind was stealing my life, and I knew it."
"I left because I would regret it if I had never tried, and I knew it."
""
"No, I didn’t leave because my best days were behind me."
""
"I left because the best days of my life were upon me, and it was my time to live them."
""
"The work I will do today, the work I will do with someone exactly like you, will be the best work of my lifetime, and it didn’t exist two years ago."
""
"Two years ago, my scorecard was my paycheck."
""
"Today, my scorecard is the notes of thanks—the incredible notes of thanks from all the men and women who have found a home at Normal 40, and then made a change in their life."
""
"This week has been full of notes, calls, and emails from people this little movement has inspired. "
""
"I traded my year-end bonus and stock options for these notes of thanks. And it’s the best trade of a lifetime."
""
"My mission is to inspire the change in a thousand lives."
"My metric of success is one thousand notes of thanks."
""
"So …"
""
"If it is your time to start, and you know it;"
"If your future isn’t where your feet are, and you know it;"
"If money is no longer your motivator, and you know it;"
"If your best work is still inside of you, and you know it;"
"If you would regret never trying it, and you know it;"
"If you are ready to explore what’s next, and you know it;"
"Then maybe, just maybe, reading this isn’t an accident."
""
"This post isn’t an algorithm."
"This post is the sign you’ve been praying for"
""
"One year, 11 months, and 30 days ago, I left who I had become for a chance to build something from my gifts. For the rest of my life, it will be the best bet I ever placed."
""
"If you’re still reading this, it’s for a reason. You needed it."
""
"What if you were one of the one thousand?"
"What if your note goes on my wall?"
"What if this is the day you start?"
"What if we do it together?"
""
"I am inviting you to book a ramble with me. I book out several weeks, but book one anyway."
""
"Then do me a favor: If Normal 40 has been a Lantern to your path or a Guide to your journey, I would be honored if you would take your place as one of the one thousand. It can be a post, a DM, or an email. "
""
"If you are feeling saucy, a letter:"
""
"Normal40 Ranch"
"37315 143rd St"
"Mansfield SD, 57460"

Dear Texas ........ Umm, okay. "

""

""

Six months ago, I released my book, The Trade, into the world."
""
"It was the best work of my lifetime. It was written from the heart, through my experiences, and with the benefit of thousands of hours of research with people who felt just like me."
""
"When I launched, I had hopes—no, visions, of it selling tens of thousands of copies."
""
"I had dreams of it being on airport bookshelves and carried by Barnes & Noble, Inc. and in the little nook bookstores in Anytown, USA."
""
"I even fell into the author’s delusion of wondering … what if someone with incredible influence finds it and it impacts them?"
""
"By those early metrics, The Trade missed the mark."
""
"It didn’t get picked up by Hudson Booksellers. And Oprah Winfrey and Ryan Holiday haven’t worked it into her morning routine (yet)."
""
"But…"
""
"The Trade, a 170-page, self-published book with zero advertising budget, has found its way into thousands of homes and has impacted lives in ways I never imagined."
""
"It has connected people around the world who are in the same lonely battle. And it has started conversations in living rooms, bedrooms, and boardrooms around the world."
""
"It has inspired people to go for the promotion, and it has prepared others to finally chase a dream."
""
"It has given people permission to accept how they feel and the courage to finally do something about it."
""
"It has altered the direction of countless lives. Including my own."
""
"But now, more than ever, The Trade is just getting started. Sales are going up, and momentum is building."
""
"More than ever, leaders are seeking a reason to pause, think, dream, and start—to take back the control they’ve let slip away."
""
"More than ever, people are asking themself the single question that might change their life: “Is my work here done?” And if it is, “What am I willing to do about it?”"
""
"One more thing in my ramble on with you:"
""
"Since launching The Trade, I have relied on a single marketing plan for impact. The plan went against the grain to see if we could pull it off."
""
"I took a chance on what others around me thought was short-sighted, small ball, crazy and a sure failure."
""
"I bet on the people who read it, needed it, and were inspired by it to be the Lanterns who would promote it, share it, gift it, and impact others."
""
"I bet on each of you to do it with me."
"And it worked. "
"And we’re just getting started."
""
"So—"
""
"If you are able to help us relentlessly find the next person who needs it — Do it."
""
"Post about it."
"Review it."
"Share a photo of you holding it."
"But do something."
""
"Then see what finds you in return."
""
"Finally, if you are one call away from someone who can give The Trade an incredible lift, you are all I have."
""
"And, until now, you are all we’ve needed. Make the call."
""
"The Trade was never intended to make us rich—as measured by dollars."
""
"The Trade was written to make each of us feel rich—as measued by your notes of thanks. And I am inviting you to join the Normal 40 movement with us."
""
"Let’s be up to something."
""
"#normal40 #thetrade #bestsellingauthor #jfds

It just keeps calling me …"

"Every time I come back to the farm, I feel it."
""
"There’s something here. Something tugging. Something pulling and something … connecting."
""
"While I don’t have it all mapped out, and it’s hard for me to imagine exactly how it will work, I can feel a nudge every time I am here. And the nudge is clear: “Bring them here.”"

This is me, with no guarantees or safety net, building something more significant. This is me, just a dude on the prairie, showing you what it’s like when you take back your calendar. "

Son,"
""
"This weekend, your time playing football has most likely drawn to a close. You had a fantastic season, made some incredible plays, and, without question, forged some unforgettable memories that will find you, whenever needed, for the rest of your lifetime."
""
"Ten years ago, it would be hard to believe there was a bigger eight-year-old Bronco fan than you."
""
"Like it was yesterday, I remember you gearing up for every game simply so we could watch them together at home. You would sleep in your jersey on nights before games. Then, on game day, you wore that uniform everywhere. Well, everywhere we would let you; the grocery store, the hardware store, and restaurants were fine. We drew the line at wearing a helmet to church."
""
"I can hardly believe that ‘yesterday’ was ten years ago."
""
"But, somehow, here we are."
""
"No longer at the beginning of a chapter, but at the end. And no longer the little boy with a plastic helmet in our basement, but a young man with an authentic helmet on the field."
""
"Son, two years ago, you taught me an incredible lesson I will never forget. One morning, before school, you told me: “Dad, I’m going to go out for football next year. I know I might never play, but I just want to be on the team.”"
""
"At the time, you were a kid who had never played an organized down of football. A kid who was, at best, an underdog to make the team. But you knew you had a dream, and you knew you wanted to chase it."
""
"“Dad, I just want to be on the team.”"
""
"Wow."
""
"You don’t know it, but that declaration — the decision to start to become something you were not yet, was … powerful. And it was, and is, inspiring."
""
"Son, the hardest thing you will come to wrestle with in your life is to know exactly who you are — and then muster the courage to stand in it. Most of us can’t let go of who we have already become to permit ourselves to chase who we want to be next."
""
"So, we never start. "
""
"Instead, we come to know who we are not, and we tolerate staying there."
""
"But you showed the courage to start. Even when it feels late."
""
"Son, we never know where the world is going to take us, and that’s part of the adventure — for you, and for me. But no matter where this life takes you, know that your mom and I will be in the stands — rain or shine, wind or calm, snow or sleet, win or loss, we will be there — cheering and learning."
""
"I admire your courage to start, Dawson. For the rest of your lifetime, you will be glad you did. And for the rest of your lifetime, know that starting is always an option."
""
"On this weekend, the scoreboard didn’t show a win. "
""
"But because you chose to start, we all got to be part of a team; your team. And that’s a win."
""
"It’s hard to believe how swiftly time moves. And it’s impossible to know where we will be in ten more years. But along the way, let’s never be afraid to start — from wherever we are."
""
"Let’s forever be glad we tried. It’s the only insurance we are offered against future regret."
""
"From the stands,"
""
" — Dad"
""

I’m a bit reflective today. It’s been quite a month. But … "
""
"Yesterday, I learned the news of a friend who lost his battle. A brilliant Normal 40 guy with a young family, quick wit, a flashy smile, and a big heart. The guy we all admire, even if only during the occasional encounter."
""
"Eight months from diagnosis to death."
""
"Eight months. "
""
"To say goodbye. To do the things. To prepare the family. To leave it better…"
""
"Eight f*ing months."
""
"We talked a year ago. We talked about my journey and his. My family and his. My dreams and his. "
""
"We shared a similar story—and we were in similar health. "
""
"Four months after that ramble—a diagnosis."
""
"Eight months after that …. this."
""
"Today, take some time to give thanks for everything around you. Take a break from the quest for more and settle into the blessings of now."
""
"Today, see the world for what it is, and what it is not. "
""
"Today, know that what you do and do not get done this weekend or this week won’t matter in 90 days. So ditch the stress."
""
"Today, give thanks for all you have already done, seen and experienced."
""
"Today, give thanks for the ride you’re on: for those who get to take it with you and those who choose to do it with you."
""
"Today, give thanks to your mental health and your physical strength. Give thanks for reading this post, going for a walk, calling a friend, and giving a hug. "
""
"Today, give thanks for all of the things your money can’t buy. Because for the things that matter most, money is laughable."
""
"There’s a razor-thin line between striving to have more and striving to be more."
""
"The difference comes down to the self-awareness of what can ultimately be bought and what you would never sell—and which of those gets your energy."
""
"Those of you who are here are not here wondering what it is you want to buy next. You’re here searching for where you can have your lifetime’s most significant impact. The stuff that matters if ever you’re faced with only eight months to live."
""
"From here, I’m going for a walk. "
""
"I’m going to think about my friend Greg, and I’m going to get a little pissed off about how unfair the world can be. "
""
"But I will also use it."
""
"I will use it all as a reminder to think more about what matters most, and what will get my energy."
""
"What matters if you have only eight months left?"
""
"After all, there’s no guarantee any of us have even that much time."
""
"On my walk, I’ll take an inventory of my life, the gifts I have been given, and the blessings I sometimes allow to go unseen. I will thank my family, my home, my friends…but I will also thank my health and my ability to take each step before me."
""
"I will also remember that doing it again tomorrow is never a guarantee."
""
"I will miss my friend Greg and pray for his family, whom I don’t even know. "
""
"Today, take some time to give some thanks to the things in life that matter most. "
""
"The things in life money can’t buy."
""
"Do more than think about them; show them your thanks."
""
"You’ll be glad you did."
""
"#normal40 #thetrade #leadership #JFDS

Leaving is never perfect, and should never be easy."
""
"It is, in fact, always imperfect. And if done on time, it should never be easy."
""
"Leaving is not the conflict, but the result of one you are settling. "
""
"But leaving has ripples, most of which you will never see."
""
"This week, I have been wrestling with the imperfectness of leaving. Negotiating the excitement of the chase with the reality of what is left behind—I’ve been thinking about the ripples."
""
"Leaving isn't just an act; it's an echo through a lifetime, a ripple in the pond of our existence. It bounces off of the people we know, the bonds we have built, the ideas we have launched, and the purposes we have stood for. And these have ripples."
""
"Leaving is more than an act; it’s a bet on a new chapter in a new place. Leaving is neither the start nor the end; it’s the transition in between. It’s the place where we leave behind a little of who and where we were, for what we might become. And it leaves ripples."
""
"Leaving is about setting in motion the unforeseen and the vast unfoldings that come from a single decision. Leaving is not an ending of a ripple—those who live in. But leaving is the beginning of a new ripple in a new pond. Ripples that are cast from a new you. Ripples that are pure, ripples with an impact you can’t possibly yet imagine."
""
"When you leave, be it a job, a place, a relationship, or a longstanding belief, you're not just moving away from something; you are moving towards an unknown that beckons with promise and challenge. A place that offers no guarantees, but does offer adventure—a place in search of your ripple."
""
"Ripples will touch corners of a world you never knew existed."
""
"Leaving casts a new stone into the still waters of our lives, and its ripples become a testament to our courage to seek, question, and redefine and to claim our path forward."
""
"Leaving lives as a single moment of risk, and then unfolds as a lifetime of newness. It’s the chance to find a pond in search of a ripple with a stone that’s worthy of one. "
""
"Leaving is never an ending—it’s a new beginning. A chance to make a new ripple in a world that needs it."
""
"No, leaving is never perfect. But the ripples usually are."
""
"Maybe it’s time to make a new ripple."
""
"This is your invitation."
"I hope you take it."
""

In 2015, we took the best family road trip of our life. We had ten days, and it was a Friday …."
""
"As we prepared to leave for the trip, the kids were up early. Mindi (wife) was a bit nervous, and I was beside myself with curiosity."
""
"We were going to take a 10-day road trip with three kids under 12, and the morning we left, we had no idea where we were going."
""
"None. Zero. Zilch."
""
"We simply hit the road and started driving. "
""
"Our goal was simply to follow our instincts and find adventure. We all had equal votes and equal say. "
""
"I have a thousand stories of that trip. And so do Mindi and my kids. We said yes to anything that seemed like fun…and everything we agreed to do as a family."
""
"I set up a GoPro in the front of the vehicle, pointing back into the car to capture random moments of our family being a family. "
""
"Of the thousands of hours of video I have of my family together, these are some of the most cherished. The clip below is us discovering the world’s largest rocking chair along Route 66 in Missouri. It was just after I was pulled over and just before I purchased a corn cob pipe."
""
"Think back to the best trip of your life."
""
"The one you can’t stop talking about. The one your kids bring up on every holiday. The one where the memories stack so high it’s hard to believe it was just one trip. The trip that left you different because you went."
""
"Now, how much did that trip cost? "
"How damaging was it to your career?"
"What was going on at work while you were out?"
""
"Chances are, you don’t remember. "
""
"Neither do I."
""
"You thought about these costs before you went. You probably stressed over the numbers and worried about what you might miss. Maybe you even fretted over these things when you were there. And so did I."
""
"But here we are—recalling a memory that it would be impossible to put a price on. And we only have it because we went ahead and did it."
""
"Everyone is saving for something. A retirement. A house. A trip. A change. The right time to bet on you. But here’s the truth: Saving for it doesn’t make it happen. Doing it does. "
""
"The year 2025 is coming, and you have a choice: invest in something that can change your life or save it for 2026."
""
"This is the day you’ve been saving for. This is that point in your life when waiting does you no more good. This is the point where saving it for later has a diminishing return. "
""
"Quit saving it for later — later isn’t guaranteed."
""
"My Vintage 4 cohort starts in January. "
"This is what you’ve been saving it for."
""
"Let’s be up to something."
""
"#normal40 #coaching #thetrade"

"I created a 35-minute movie of the trip … and it might be worthy of its own Netflix special."
""
"Here’s a few minutes for you."
"I’m glad I didn’t wait…"
"You will be too.

This post is a hard-core, no shit, in your face; what are you waiting for; you’re not getting any younger; this is your time; you’ve given enough; take it back; what if you did it; what if you never try; here’s how you start the rest of your life marketing pitch."
""
"To be very clear, I am marketing to You."
""
"Not the You that brings to you here. Nope."
"The You that keeps you there."
""
"Not the You begging you to try—to take a chance."
"The You begging you to stay—to run out the clock."
""
"I am talking to You—the You always playing defense. The You who works on your off days, answers texts at all hours, carves out time on vacation to “check in” at work, puts your marriage third, lives most of your home life annoyed, has lost most of your meaningful friendships, has forgotten what an adventure feels like and thinks that in just a few more years you’ll be ready."
""
"You. Defensive You. I’m talking to You. And I want You to listen. Not for my sake, I’ll be just fine—but for yours."
""
"Look, Defensive You, I know You have done incredible things. Things nobody thought possible. Heck, things you never thought possible. Things most people would trade for in a heartbeat. Ironically, that doesn’t make what you’re going through easier. It makes it harder."
""
"But what you’re feeling—the pressure to stay and the guilt for wanting something different—is normal. "
""
"But another part of You is ready for more."
""
"Offensive You wants the ball back. It’s growing annoyed with the waiting game."
""
"Offensive You is tired of tolerating, and it wants to take another shot, make another play, impact another life, and build something more."
""
"Remember, Offensive You delivered you here—to the incredible life you have built. It was your offense that did that. And it wants to do it again."
""
"Offensive You wants to start. Offensive You wants to explore, test, talk, and create—to do something pure that will outlast your days. Offensive You wants to use your skills, talents, experiences, story, incredible gifts, decency, and generosity to be who you are being called to be next."
""
"Offensive You is ready."
""
"So, Defensive You, I want to help. And, for the record, You want someone’s help; that’s why you’re here."
""
"That’s why I’m here, too."
""
"I wrote a book that tells my story. The story of my move from offense, to defense, and then (finally) back to offense. Every day, the book creates a ripple in the world. The book is called The Trade, and you can read it or listen to it today. It has changed thousands of lives."
""
"More?"
""
"I host the single-greatest-valued mastermind on the planet (as judged by me). It’s called The Insider, and for $25, it will put you face-to-face with people just like you. And it will put you face-to-face with me. It is incredible."
""
"Today, Defensive You, I want you to do something. Something for you."
""
"Buy The Trade, book a ramble, talk to a friend, or join The Insider. It just might be the move of a lifetime. "
""
"It’s your ball…"
"Pass or shoot."

Emotion.  "
""
"Do it with emotion. "
""
"Today is my last day writing.  Tomorrow evening, I return home to my family. I’m ready. "
""
"Yesterday, over the course of a few hours, the end of the book found me. It was, and has been, an oddly emotional introduction."
""
"Going into this week I had no idea how I was going to deliver an ending that mattered the way I wanted it to matter. I wanted it to land so … directly, that you throw the book down and ask for another chapter.  "
""
"My mission is to inspire change - but how could words alone do something so grand?"
""
"Then the end found me, and words alone will. "
""
"I was going to read one paragraph of that chapter for you here. That was my intent. Twice. "
""
"But something stopped me. "
""
"Emotion."
""
"For some reason, I simply couldn’t get through it. It’s too close and it’s too real. I still haven’t read it without emotion tagging along. "
""
"I’ll take another run at it later. But for now, especially as I look at the worn-out look in my exhausted eyes - from hours of emotion, I’m going to take a break and go for a jog."
""
"The ending is, well, fantastic. And it will thoroughly annoy you to read it. And the words - they will be enough. Using them will be up to you. "
""
"Thanks to all of you for the notes and DMs this week. I’ve read them all and responded to almost none. They truly have been a blessing. "
""
"I’m on a mission to inspire change in the lives of a thousand dudes, and I’m taking all of you with me as we do it. It’s not an idea, it’s a movement. "
""
"And we have some work to do. "
""
"Until then, "
""
"I’ll see you on the next page.  "

Go where it takes you …"
""
"Most mornings I walk. Most mornings, it’s the same path. Most mornings, I listen to a book, a previous ramble, or a podcast."
""
"This wasn’t most mornings. "
""
"I started walking towards the sunrise. I had no agenda, no plan, just walking.  "
""
"Not far from me is a new road…that dead ends at a major highway. "
""
"It might be years before anything is there. Today, I was there."
""
"And for nearly two miles, I was alone. Until, there was Randy."
""
"I noticed him early, long before he saw me. I had no idea who he was or why he was standing at the end of a dead-end road, staring at traffic."
""
"My initial instinct was to turn around. You know, just avoid any awkward interaction. After all, we were at the end of a dead-end road."
""
"But I kept walking. I negotiated the impulse to turn around about six times but listened to my instinct to keep going. "
""
"As I got closer, I noticed a uniform, then a badge, and then the urge to waive and turn."
""
"I listened to my instinct."
""
"I waved and said, “Good morning.” He said, “Good morning. I’m waiting for my wife.”"
""
"Given where we were, the time of day (6:57 AM), and what he appeared to be wearing, nothing about that made sense."
""
"Then, all of a sudden, I heard the honking.  Many honkes.  Like ten fast honks from a car passing by the busy street."
""
"And Randy? He was waving and loving it."
""
"He turned to me, and I was still 40 yards away, and he yelled, “That was my wife. She’s on her way to work.”"
""
"Then he showed me the sign.  "
""
"Damn, Randy. Well done."
""
"Randy is new to the area. He works early morning security at a nearby ER facility and leaves before anyone in his house is up. "
""
"So, each morning, he makes his way to the end of that road, with sign in hand, wades into the dewy grass simply to make sure his wife starts her day right. "
""
"I need to be a little more like Randy. Heck, most of us do."
""
"Tomorrow, change up your routine. Take out the AirPods. Go for a walk rather than the gym. Be present with what you hear, smell, and see. Don’t be there for the physical stimulation, but for the mental and spiritual stimulation. "
""
"Then, listen to your instinct. Not your impulse to check email or get an update…your instinct. It’s trying to take you somewhere, and it’s begging you to hear it, and to trust it."
""
"Today, my presence and my instinct led me to Randy."
"And I’m already a fan."
""
"Today, listen to the quiet voice of instinct."
"Then, be up to something."
""
"This is the #normal40 highway."
"We have room for more."
"We’ll make room for you."
"We’re taking Randy with."

This is me … dreaming with you."
""
"Twenty months ago, I left my corporate job. Since leaving, I’ve hosted +400 free rambles, 50 N40 podcasts, written a best-selling book, launched two masterminds (sold out in a day), created The Insider community for people to coach one another through their next chapter, and built a free community on LinkedIn with more than 3000 leaders who show up regularly."
""
"Twenty months ago, none of this was in my wildest dreams. "
""
"Because I had forgotten how to dream."
""
"My guess is, you’ve forgotten too."
""
"Dreaming has a sequence. If you’re not careful, dreaming slips into wishing, and wishing is dangerous. For people who have dedicated their lives to something, dreaming is hard. "
""
"For you, before you can dream again, dream like you used to dream, you have to clear the following three hurdles:"
""
"1. Accept what you feel isn’t leaving on its own. You're stuck, something is out of place, and nobody will fix this for you. Sure, it was supposed to feel better and last longer. It didn’t, and it’s not coming back."
""
" — If you can accept this, move to step 2"
""
"2. Brutal Assessment: Imagine three more years of traveling further down the same road. Same desk, same routines, same deadlines, same result. Same you—only older. If that future doesn't excite you today, a change is coming for you tomorrow. A change you control, or one that controls you."
""
" — If you want to control your next chapter, move to step 3"
""
"3. Consent to start: Permission to start won’t come from outside; it must come from within you. This is not about quitting anything; it’s about starting something. You will either permit yourself to start exploring, or you will one day tolerate wondering why you didn’t."
""
"If you are ready to start, move to step 4"
""
"4. Dream again: You forgot how. It was wrung out of you by deadlines set by others, and by mandatory meetings and shifting priorities. Your dreams were traded for month-end, quarter-end, and year goals that were converted into a bonus to buy the lifestyle you can’t afford to leave. Now, dreaming is dangerous and irresponsible, and you are here … lifeless."
""
"My life’s mission is simple: Inspire you to act."
""
"Look, everyone who finds me is stuck in one of these four buckets. Being stuck makes you normal. So normal that I named the entire movement Normal 40."
""
"I know this is your journey, and it was supposed to feel better. But you don't have to do this alone. After a few thousand hours, I’ve become pretty good at sizing up who you are capable of being,helping you commit to starting and reminding you how to dream. This is what I do."
""
"My next dream:"
""
"I want to do an in-person event with a small group of people who feels stuck, have forgot how to dream, are inspired to do more and to help others. "
""
"I don’t have the details ironed out, but THIS IS ME DREAMING."
""
"If I hosted eight of us at a destination venue over four days, would you be interested in joining?"
""
"If so, email me: Lon@normal40.com"
""

This week, I’m working long hours in the field."
"I’m not doing it for the money; I’m not being paid. "
"I’m doing it for the simple joy of connecting with the land."
"A joy that can’t be bought, only experienced."
""
"This is my new life. I make all the ends meet, and I make all my obligations work. But I no longer live by my calendar — my calendar lives by me."
""
"The path I took was not easy, or obvious. I’m not rich, and what I walked away from came with a cost. Getting here was not free; it was a trade. The same will be true for you: It won’t be easy, it won’t be obvious, and it won’t be free. It will be a trade."
""
"Some make it, others don’t. "
"That is a fact."
""
"But making this trade — where my calendar works for me — is impossible to regret."
""
"This is me today … but 18 months ago:"
""
"I quit my corporate job."
"Before I quit, I had a client."
"Before I had a client, I became a coach. "
"Before I became a coach, I got clear on who I was."
"Before that, I wrote down what I would no longer tolerate."
"Before that, I accepted my future wasn’t where my feet were."
"Before that, I felt my gut begging me to finally bet on myself."
"Before that, my life’s calendar was built around quarter end."
"Before that, I had to pretend I felt better than I really was."
"Before that, I felt like you."
""
"Here’s my best advice: Start."
""
"The most intelligent people I know never start because they can’t see the end. They start by asking all of the wrong questions, like “Does it scale?” and “How much can I charge?” and “How will I stand out in this crowded field?”"
""
"Why? Because these questions are impossible to answer before you actually start. These ‘smart’ questions are your excuse to wait another quarter or year to really start."
""
"These intelligent questions are simply stall tactics; excuses you need to justify why you’re still there. And, there you are. See how smart you are."
""
"The answers you seek are found where you least want to look. And where you don’t want to look is in the vulnerable place of actually taking an action to start. "
""
"You don’t have to quit anything — nothing — to start."
"But if you ever want to make a bet on yourself, to live out your best days, to take back your calendar, you need to start so that you can one day quit. "
""
"Go back to my list and work your way from the bottom up. "
""
"Where are you on that timeline? "
""
"What are you willing to do about it? Nothing? Fine with me."
""
"But if you want to finally bet on yourself, if your future isn’t where your feet are, if you know what you no longer wish to tolerate and you don’t know what to do about it …."
""
"You’re in luck — this is the Normal 40, and this is what we do. If you want to do something to start, join 3100 in our private, closed, and free Normal community. Or download “The 14 Questions That Changed My Life.” from my website or book a free ramble with me."
""
"In this moment, you will or you will not. "
"In the next moment, you own the result."
""
"See you when you get here. "
"Don’t be late."
"I’ll be here."
""
"#normal40 #thetrade #coaching #jfds

On the Stroschein farm and ranch in South Dakota, you can still find the remnants of the original homesteads where the prairie remains untouched."
""
"No plows. No fences. Just land as it was when the first settler arrived."
""
"What’s left of their dream is a hole in the ground; the place where they lived when they first claimed the land, the place where they started."
""
"We don’t know if they came alone, with a family, or with a team. We don’t know if they “proved up” the quarter and made it theirs or if they were defeated by blistering summers and punishing winters."
""
"All we know is that they came. They gave it a shot. And if you look closely enough, you can still find the proof, and the proof still inspires me."
""
"The Homestead Act of 1862 promised 160 acres of land to anyone bold enough to try. Immigrants, soldiers, freed slaves, and the restless were encouraged to come west with one condition: they had to make the land theirs. To “prove up,” they had to live there, cultivate the soil, and survive five years."
""
"It wasn’t just a transaction—it was a test of grit."
""
"They arrived to a vast, untouched prairie that stretched farther than the eye could see, breathtaking and overwhelming. But the prairie was unforgiving. Summers scorched the earth, and winters could freeze a man in his tracks."
""
"They were hours from the nearest doctor, days from supplies, and completely at the mercy of the land. The soil was tough. The wind unrelenting. The seasons harsh. For every story of success, there’s another of failure and loss—proof that the dream didn’t come easy and don’t always work out. This is why some do, and most never will."
""
"Yet for those who came, success wasn’t always measured in acreage or crops. It was measured in the attempt. A hole in the ground where they first slept. A hole in the ground and a few boards from a home long fallen. These were the signs that someone dared to start, dared to try, dared to chase something bigger. Proof of courage. Proof of resilience. Proof they didn’t wait for the perfect time to begin."
""
"Their journey is the Normal 40 story. It’s not about guarantees; it’s about the shot. The prairie asks the same question life does: Will you show up when it’s your time? Will you leave proof behind that you gave it everything you had?"
""
"The vastness of the prairie is still there, just like the vastness of opportunity in your own life."
""
"The question is, will we claim it? Will we leave the proof that we tried?"
""
"This is a question only you can answer."
""
"I have a tug in my gut to one day bring people here to show you this simple place I call home, to show you the big sky, the vast range, and the beauty of what others call “flyover.”"
""
"The prairie isn’t for everyone. But for me, sharing the Normal 40 Ranch will create bonds between us and others that will last our lifetime."
""
"For me, that will be proof enough that I was here."
""
"Today, be up to something."
"Prove you were here."
"Be relentless."
""

""
"What if you did the same with your life?"

"Not the losers that take your money—worse."

"The losers that take your energy. "

"The commitments, habits, routines, meetings, or relationships that steal your joy and drain your momentum. The things that once worked but now give you nothing in return. The losers you’ve been tolerating."

"It’s time to loss-harvest your energy."
""
"Quit investing in what’s not working. Redeploy your energy into things that make you feel alive. Things that move you forward. Things that make you proud to say, “I’m finally doing it.”"

"When you cut the losers, it feels … fantastic."

Eighteen months ago, I launched Normal 40 to zero fanfare."
""
"At the time, I had about 1800 followers on LinkedIn."
""
"I didn’t start with a blueprint for growth, and I was careful not to ascribe any meaningless benchmarks for what success might be. Not that benchmarks are wrong, but I didn’t yet have a bench."
""
"I didn’t have a voice or message shaped by hundreds of hours of face-to-face conversation. I didn’t know if anyone had been feeling what I once felt, and I didn’t know if I could inspire them to show up for themselves if they did."
""
"Normal 40 did not start with a revenue model. I didn’t stall out on spreadsheet analysis for how long it might take me to build a following or how I convert followers to income. I didn’t waste any energy wondering how something I’ve not even started would one-day scale. And I didn’t stop when I discovered that 95% of the people who read this will be unable to comment, share, or like this message for fear of retribution or discovery."
""
"Yep, on paper, Normal 40 is a horrendous business model that nobody in their right mind would ever spend time building."
""
"So, thankfully, it’s not my business."
"Thankfully, it is my mission."
""
"There are a thousand reasons never to start. But I’m not convinced any of them are real, or even in the proper order. The reasons to wait tend to be things we manufacture out of proportion to permit ourselves not to act."
""
"If you’ve convinced yourself that this is the wrong time to start chasing a dream, you’re already losing the fight. In your lifetime, there has never been a bad day to start chasing your dream. And you’ve already let enough great days pass by."
""
"In the last 18 months, it’s hard to believe what’s happened to Normal 40. My invisible audience has grown, and that’s nice. But the real story isn’t mine, it’s yours."
""
"Normal 40 is not a business; it’s a movement."
""
"It’s a tribe of people like us, who feel like us, who dream like us, who value relationships like us, and who do hard things — like us."
""
"For those of you who show up each day and don’t feel you can post or share — I see you. I hear you. And I talk to you."
""
"This is your tribe as much as it is mine. And one day, I know I will meet you in the opening pages of a new chapter."
""
"My mission is to bring people out of the shadows of their own business success, and meet them along their journey to their life’s greatest chapter."
""
"I often get asked, “What’s next?” For a while, I wasn’t sure, or I was afraid to say it. But what’s next is becoming more apparent — on two fronts. And while it doesn’t yet have a business model, it has a mission: To get people from the Normal 40 Tribe into a community, in person, to inspire change."
""
"When people like us do things like this, we have all we need to be a tribe."
""
"If you don’t have a tribe, you’re welcome in ours. "
""
"When you find yourself hunting for change, hunt first for a tribe who will do it with you. "
""
"If that’s you, welcome to the Normal 40."
""

"Timestamp: 38:10"
""
"Lon: One of the things that I've learned is that so many of us, at what is the height of our career, we get this feeling—we get this calling, this urge to really challenge ourselves."
""
"It’s challenging to answer the question: “Is this the best work of my life?”Is this really how I want to live the next chapter of my life?"
""
"Do I, and here's my choice … I can continue to build upon what I've created over the last 20 years of my life … Or I can really take a step back, take a step outside of who I've become, step away from the title, step away from the office, step away from, in your case, the ER, step away from the degree, and I can really challenge myself to think of myself differently, as if there's no sunk cost — as if you have a zero clock and that there’s no value in living on yesterday’s decisions. A place where just because you did it yesterday, doesn’t mean it makes sense to do it again tomorrow…"
""
"And if you allow yourself to separate from that, you can arrive at this place where you can envision different possibilities for your future, but in that, as you go through that, you go through this really weird place where you try to avoid thinking about that, you try to avoid feeling it, you try to avoid showing it, because it feels irresponsible. You certainly don't talk to your friends about it; you probably don't even talk to your wife about it."
""
"Did that find you?"
""
"Keith Pochick, MD  — Guest: 41:24"

""
"I've spent how much of my life getting to this point? How much time, energy, blood, sweat and, tears, and training and, education, and dollars to be here?"
""

"That's another question that's rolling through your head. And then probably the last one is, well … will I be successful? Maybe I fall flat on my face and it's a complete disaster, and then I have to come back with my tail between my legs and beg for a job."

This Real Story is different. It's mine...and I never intended to share it. Ever."
""
"It was December 13, 2019. I was on a flight home from Washington, DC, and the angst between the voice in my head and the spirit in my gut needed the fight. "
""
"So it did."
""
"On that flight, this was penned as a note to myself, about myself, for myself."
""
"It was a conversation between mind and gut. It was penned to do exactly what it eventually did: move me from "intent" into "action." To get back on offense and off defense."
""
"It's been more than five years since this flight. "
""
"Five years? "
"Huh. "
"Five years."
""
"This morning was the first time I returned to this page since leaving my company. "
""
"Re-reading it was both awesome and eerie. "
""
"Something in my life was shifting the day I wrote this. A shift that launched my journey."
""
"And if you're reading this, something is shifting in your life, too. "
""
"Just own that."
""
"Only then can your December 13 moment happen."
""
"Here's the raw writing, two years before I resigned."

“There’s a fire in me that I often struggle to describe. That fire comes from just challenges from birth. God gave me an uphill battle that often people wonder how you face it — and it’s minuscule in the grand scheme of what others have faced. And that fire is stronger than ever, and it’s manifesting itself in a way that I hadn’t pictured when I looked at my future — until today.”"
"_________________________________________________"
""
"That’s how my guest Brandon Meyer describes the fire that’s been with him since birth—a fire sparked by cerebral palsy, 24 surgeries, and a childhood full of challenges. But he’s never asked for pity. He’s asked for purpose, and in this conversation, he begins to uncover it."
""
"This story hit hard for me, and I can’t articulate why."
""
"Maybe it’s because I was ashamed to know so little about Cerebral Palsy (CP). "
"Maybe it’s because he had such grace in his presence. Or… Maybe it’s because it caused me to rethink my potential and where I’m settling."
""
"To be clear, this is not a story about CP. "
"This is a story of mindset. "
"This is a story of a dream and the decision to chase it."
""
"It’s about what you do when life hands you a narrative — and you choose to write a different one."
""
"It’s about refusing to be defined by diagnoses, labels, or limits."
"It’s about finding your way — regardless of how it looks to others."
"It’s about getting back up and breaking the effing braces."
""
"At the end of the pod, we grabbed our guitars…because kids with CP can’t play.  "
""
"I found out that, too, was a myth."
""
"This is Part 1 of a 2-part series."
""
"And if you’ve ever felt a whisper inside saying, “There’s more,” this one’s for you."
""
"Today on the Normal 40 Podcast, meet my friend Brandon."
""
"He is up to something, and…"
"I am a fan."
""

The Farm: Day 4 — Ask for It."
""
"A human being isn’t built to do the same thing for 20 years."
""
"We’re not meant to take orders from someone we never see."
""
"We’re not wired to live by someone else’s metrics, obey a calendar we didn’t create, need permission to be with family or settle for something that no longer builds our future. "
""
"We are wired for more."
""
"You are wired for more."
""
"You are wired to inspire the best in others as it inspires the best in you."
""
"You are wired to wake up each day ready to try something new, set your own schedule, and live without apology or permission for whatever it is that makes you feel pure."
""
"But to get there, you have to ask for it."
""
"Ask for the promotion. The raise. The transfer. The remote work. The new life…"
""
"Asking isn’t about words—it’s about clarity."
""
"The clarity of knowing when your work there is done. The clarity of your potential. The clarity of what’s possible. The clarity of knowing who it is you need to be next."
""
"When was the last time you were clear enough on what you wanted to actually ask for it? "
""
"Once you were clear, asking for it became easy. So …"
""
"I’m asking you—Be up to something."
""
"Life lives better when you are."

One day, the novelty of building someone else’s dream—will run its course."
""
"One day it dawns on you: “I have no product of my own.” "
""
"Business success is beautiful, but it has a shelf life that most of us don’t see—but we can feel, usually in our gut…on Monday."
""
"We climb the ladder, telling ourselves that one day, probably after the next bonus, we will pursue what matters—investing in our own product and our own dream."
""
"But you told yourself the same thing last year. And the year before."
""
"So here you are—feeling like you felt yesterday, only older. "
""
"Know this: The first product you need to work on is not a widget; it’s you—the same “you” that you are selling to your company. "
""
"The best product you will ever have to offer to anyone is…you."
""
"And when was the last time you worked on … you? "
"When was the last time you spent money on…you?"
"When was the last time you did something to improve…you?"
"When was the last time you dreamed about…you?"
""
"The first product you need to work on is the product of you."
"Some will make the investment, others won’t."
"Which one are you?"
""
"The you I know is a one-of-a-kind. It’s full of wisdom. It’s got the know-how from wins and losses, and it knows bullshit when it sees it. It’s got its own language, its own operating system, and its own voice—the voice of you."
""
"One day, you’ll wake up and want to work on you. "
""
"When that day comes, find me. Or find someone."
"But build the product of you."
"The world needs it."
"So do you."
""
"Let’s be up to something."
"That’s what I do."

Is the dream walking in, or walking out?"
""
"If you cannot walk into your office this morning and say with conviction, “I am doing exactly what I am meant to do in this moment.” Then maybe you should find a new office, and a new walk."
""
"What’s next for you won’t start with a physical move; it will begin with a smoldering pit in your gut that won’t go away. It will start with the confusion and a desire for something other than what you have. It will start with guilt for wanting it."
""
"It will start as something you think is a phase, but it will persist. It will start with you staring at your self-made “Do I have enough to walk away” spreadsheet. The one you work on during work. The one you try not to think about when your family is in bed while you sip on your second drink, alone."
""
"What’s next for you won’t show up and offer itself to you; it has to be found. It has to be created. It has to be built."
""
"What’s next for you won’t be a gift. What’s next for you won’t be obvious. What’s next for you won’t be free. What’s next for you will be a trade."
""
"What’s next for you can’t be seen on your financial spreadsheet, or on anyone’s map."
""
"What’s next for you won’t come fast, but it will last forever if you do it right."
""
"What’s next for you starts the moment you accept your future isn’t where your feet are; that nobody is going to do this for you, and you acknowledge this is the youngest you will ever again be."
""
"It starts the moment you permit yourself to feel excited to go walking in, and then go walking out. When you decide to do it now and avoid the regret of never trying."
""
"When the work you do loses its meaning, so too do the undertones of your life. That’s your pit."
""
"If walking in today doesn’t feel like it used to, think about walking out. Think about starting. Think about betting on you—on your terms. On your timeline. And doing it with all that you are."
""
"Think about trading the grind of the next decade for work you will get to do for the next 40 years of your life."
""
"Think about trading your work for a chance to create your lifetime’s best works."
""
"Think about that You."
""
"The new You."
""
"The You who is here hoping you’re still reading. The You that’s wondering why this is resonating. The You who knows this is as young as you will ever be.  The You who knows you are ready—the You who wants to take the shot."
""
"That’s the You I’m talking to."
""
"When that You is ready, let’s be up to something."
""
"The best bet you will ever make is the bet on yourself."
""
"The best way to start is to join me in my Vintage One cohort launching in April. An exclusive and intensive journey back to you."
""
"My mission is to inspire change in a thousand lives. "
"My metric is a thousand notes of thanks."
"I’m on my way …"
""
"When you’re ready to build what’s next, Vintage One will change your life. I guarantee it. And I only take six."
""
"When you’re ready …"
""
"Let’s be up to something."
""
"Something bold, selfish, and unreasonable."
""
"You’ll be glad you did it."

Are you at the beginning, or are you at the start?"
""
"If you are here, maybe you’ve started."
"But most likely, you’re only beginning."
""
"Beginning is good. It’s part of the process. It’s ahead of wishing and wanting, and it feels good."
""
"But being at the beginning can be a false sense of progress. It can be the place where you stall. Worse, it can be a place where you hide."
""
"Take a running race, for example. You can be at the beginning of the race before anyone else. You can warm up, stretch, meet some fun people, size up the other participants, plast some numbers on your back, and ensure you have the Normal 40 podcast flowing through your earbuds. You can do some high knees, retie your shoes, and strap on your new sunglasses. Great. You look the part and, for a while, you feel the part."
""
"But you’ve not yet started anything. You are simply at the beginning of something. You are on the hook for nothing…yet."
""
"Being at the beginning is fun. It looks like you’re in the race, and it feels like you’re in the race. But you are really only standing."
""
"Starting doesn’t happen until you do something. Starting is when you leave the beginning. Starting is when you’re willing to risk the safety of the beginning to start moving towards something."
""
"Starting is different than beginning. Starting puts you on the hook. Starting has proof that you tried. Starting is action. "
""
"If you’re here, you are at the beginning. But if you also want to start, I would love to hear your story. "
""
"Maybe, just maybe, we should run together. After all, I like the podcast you’re listening to."
""
"I’ll see you at the starting line.  "
""

61% of CEOs say loneliness is impairing their performance."
"43% of physicians say the same thing."
""
"Harvard Business Review published that number years ago. But nobody is taking this on. "
""
"Until now. And let me prove it."
""
"Since 2022, I've hosted more than a thousand calls with C-suite leaders, executives, physicians, and attorneys."
""
"Here's what I think HBR missed:"
""
"This loneliness isn't about having fewer friends or contacts."
""
"These people have thousands of contacts, full calendars, people milling around them all day, and families waiting at home--hoping they don't have to spend the night working. "
""
"They're not isolated. They're surrounded...by expectation."
""
"But they have no one they can tell the truth to. Nobody feels safe."
""
"Not their spouse — too much is at stake."
"Not their colleagues — career suicide."
"Not their friends — most are contacts, not confidants."
"Not a therapist — who's never run a department or missed a kid's game because of a board call."
""
"Because they have everything they chased, and having a new dream isn't an option. So, they avoid it. "
""
"In thousands of conversations over the past three years, I've sat across from incredibly decent people who've built exactly this kind of life. The loneliness isn't about being alone; it's about being seen."
""
"It's about what they're carrying, and who in that room actually knows the struggle just beneath the surface. Who actually knows the pit of despair before a shift or the sleepless night before "hard message to the company"?"
""
"Well, I do."
""
"There's a difference between who you see and who they are. "
"Most of them feel the pit every day. "
"Almost none of them tell anyone."
""
"But they tell me."
""
"Several times each week, they will say it to me. And I can assure you, things change when they do."
""
"If ever there's a need to ramble, this is your invitation. "
""
"The best decade of your life is in front of you, but..."
"It may not be lived where you're standing."
""
"This work isn't being shared to get something out of me."
"It's being shared to get something out of you."
""
"Whenever you're ready,"
"Find me here."

When he was twelve, Parker Hays watched his dad go from pitching wiffle balls in the backyard to barely being able to walk."

"Rheumatoid arthritis took hold fast. His dad’s career crumbled. The family changed. The needs changed. And Parker—just a kid—quietly stepped in to fill the gaps."
""
"He did the laundry. The grocery shopping. The repairs."
"Because when something broke, Parker fixed it."
""
"That moment didn’t just shape his adolescence."
"It shaped his life."
""
"He became a physician. An ER doctor. A trauma surgeon. And he gave his life to it."
""
"In the ER, things break fast, and Dr. E. Parker Hays Jr. MD FACEP knew how to show up fast."
""
"For 33 years, he did precisely that. "
"For 33 years, to the day."
""
"He was the calm in the chaos. "
""
"A decorated, trusted leader in emergency medicine."
""
"The kind of doctor you’d want at your bedside when everything went sideways. The kind of doctor you would want operating on you if you were sideways."
""
"Then, a change…"
""
"One day, at lunch with his wife, an ambulance screamed past."
""
"And something was different."
""
"“I’ve spent three decades knowing what to do when someone comes off that ambulance,” he said."
""
"“Now, I want to spend the second half of my life helping people stay off it.”"
""
"That was his turning point—his Normal 40 Moment."
""
"It was time to make The Trade."
" — And he didn’t make it alone."
""
"Together with his wife, Dr. Laura Martelly Hays MD, FACEP — a physician, yoga instructor, and mindfulness expert and badass, launched Lasting Impact Wellness with one powerful mission: "
""
"To help leaders and their teams achieve sustainable well-being and build resilience to be more effective in real-time, durable for the long term, and purpose-driven in their roles."
""
"Because Parker and Laura know what it’s like to succeed on paper… while burning out behind the scenes."
""
"They’ve seen the cost of high performance."
"They’ve lived the tension of “Should I stay or should I go?”"
"They’ve stood at the intersection of success and dissatisfaction—and chosen to build something different."
""
"Parker used to run toward the sirens."
"Now, he listens for the quiet inner call that most of us try to silence."
""
"He gave himself permission to change."
"To stop fixing others long enough to heal himself."
"To learn again. To lead differently. To live fully."
""
"This is what it looks like to repot a life."
"This is life on the Upper Curve™"

What I have learned about The Normal 40 Paradox."
""
"N40 Paradox: The relentless desire for change and control without loss of established security, comfort, and status—leading to a state of indecision, deep frustration, and emotional turmoil."
""
"N40P is the negotiation between aspirations and security, with the new-found appreciation of your mortality."
""
"The Normal 40 Paradox is not found along a single axis; it’s a marketplace of decisions where the economics are not financial but emotional, and the currencies trade between courage and the time you have left."
""
"Symptoms include chronic dissatisfaction with short streaks of joy, loss of self, powerlessness against the calendar, an inability to say “no” without fear of professional fallout, mood fluctuations, a pronounced sense of stagnation, and a worry about never mustering the courage to change before it’s too late."
""
"The N40 Paradox is real, and it sucks. It creates enormous stress because addressing it and avoiding it seems equally reasonable and unreasonable. "
""
"The price of inaction generates an unhealthy amount of personal stress—often kept from family and friends because of guilt associated with obvious outward success and having what you chased. Then, slowly, this turns into emotional guilt for having so much and feeling so little."
""
"This stress and guilt often lead to self-medication through the use of alcohol and other ingestibles, isolationism, and, ironically, more work as a distraction."
""
"Options for The Normal 40 Paradox:"
""
"1.) Accept that I am talking to you, and that you are not alone."
""
"2.) Give yourself permission to finally do something about how you feel."
""
"3.) Share what you feel with someone you trust or who has been there. "
""
"4.) Level up your peer group. This won’t be solved over a round of golf and some drinks. Find a community of people like you doing things like this."
""
"I have dedicated the last two years of my life to understanding what we navigate in the prime of our lives. That journey has put me face-to-face with thousands of people, and it has led me here. It’s led me to know this is normal, and that you shouldn’t do it alone. "
""
"If this is you, you’re not alone. If this is you, we should connect. If this is you, and I’m not your jam, talk to someone else."
""
"Buy my book ‘The Trade,’ listen to the Normal 40 Podcast, download The 14 Questions That Changed My Life, schedule a #ramble with me, or Join The Insider … but just f*ing do something (JFDS)."
""
"Nobody will show up to do this one for you."
"But you don’t have to do it alone."
""
"It’s your ball, boss."
"Pass or shoot."
""

""
"What proof do you have when deciding something is not achievable and should be put on a shelf?"
""
"What process do you go through to know that “something more” isn’t possible for you?"
""
"What BS questions do you conjure up in your mind about why you can’t do it, you shouldn’t do it, or that it’s too late to do it? "
""
"When it’s gnawing at you like it is right now, what do you actually do about it? Something? Nothing? Hope? Wish it away?"
""
"Where do you draw the line between the possible and the impossible?"
""
"This is the line that shapes your future and sets your limits. It makes something possible, or it guarantees it never will be. "
""
"Where you draw this line matters. And you’re holding the pen."
""
"For the rest of your life, you will never be younger than you are today. You will never have more time to start than you have today."
""
"Maybe this is the day you redraw your line. "

"It won’t happen over night. But - "
"You have no proof it won’t happen."
""

"Draw it up and test it. "
"Or let it slowly die."
"It’s your pen."
