My house had a bookend yesterday, and it played out exactly how it was supposed to. Our youngest, the one who keeps us young, did his “Fifth Grade Walkout.” "
""
"We were all there. My wife, my daughter, my older son, and me. We showed up to see him walk out for the last time. We saw the accomplishment on his face, we heard the kids scream his name, and we could feel his readiness to chase what’s next."
""
"The little guy who has followed his siblings his whole life finally had the stage. His last day as an elementary student — a bookend to celebrate. "
""
"And my last day as a dad with an elementary-aged child — a bookend to contemplate."
""
"A bookend for both of us in one event."
""
"People often ask why I left my corporate life and why I would risk it. Why would I leave something I liked to do. Something I was good at? I know the answer …"
""
"Six years ago, we sent a shy boy who loved wearing blue boots to elementary school for the first time while I was on a flight to Brazil. Yesterday he walked out of that building for the last time, and I was there to see it, damn it."
""
"A reminder that with every first in your life, there will be a last. A reminder that life isn’t lived in firsts and lasts, but by all of the moments in between. "
""
"A reminder that time isn’t life’s income statement. "
"Time is life’s Balance Sheet. "
"And everything in life eventually balances."
""
"Since kindergarten, we’ve shared six first days of school."
"After today, we now share six last days, too."
"We are in balance. The chapter is closed. I no longer have any ability to impact the moments in between. And time will march on."
""
"We tend to show up in each other's life at the bookends. "
"I made my trade — I lived my own Walkout — to live more of the moments in between. That decision wasn’t free; it was a trade. A trade that will be impossible for me to regret. A Walkout that captured a photo of my son … walking out for the last time."
""
"Dude, six years ago will be here before you know it. Another bookend will find you. It won’t be the candles on the cake that eat at you, it will be how you lived between the bookends."
""
"I will never again live a day as the dad of an elementary-aged child. As of today, that part of my life is a memory. But I have this day, and the next. And I will own them — all because of a Walkout. "
""
"For everything in life there is a last, and so too is there a first."
""
"And firsts always start with a proper Walkout. "
""
"When you’re ready, you’ll know."
""
"When you’re ready, follow me."
""

Farm Kid Friday - What shaped you?"
""
"Dear Farm,"
""
"Thank you for the lessons you taught me so early in life. "
""
"Thank you for teaching me:"
" - A toy truck and dirt hill was enough."
" - A stock water tank could make a good pool."
" - Work was not an option; it was how we got by."
" - New tractor day was a big deal."
" - Rain on the prairie is always a gift. "
" - Banks really take stuff away, even when the rain doesn't come."
" - I can teach an 1100-pound calf to lead by his nose."
" - The smell of fresh-cut alfalfa is the best smell in the world."
" - You can build a connection to the land that will last a lifetime."
" - The term "overtime" is an opinion."
" - A neighbor will show up when needed."
" - You will show up for a neighbor when a neighbor needs it."
" - You don't attend your neighbor's farm sale."
" - Little league baseball games take priority overall."
" - It's a bad decision to wear your Sunday shoes into the feedlot."
" - A poorly tended fence leads to a lot of rework."
" - You should never put yourself between a calf and her mama."
" - Ten years old is old enough to drive, shoot and cook. "
" - Riding a bike on the lonely country roads after dark drums up some crazy things in your mind. "
" - Listening to Paul Harvey is mandatory."
" - An overnight with a buddy was the best day of the year."
" - A 45-minute bus ride is something you get used to."
" - The county Fair IS the annual vacation. And …"
" - To live there is to have something worth dying for."
""
"Photo: Me and my siblings on June 30, 1982, at the funeral of our hamster, Geraldine.  I can walk you to the exact GPS coordinates today, blindfolded."
""
"This is a fraction of the inheritance I received just by being born into a farming family."
""
"These are the ingredients that make me, me."
"This is my inheritance. And it is a gift."
" "
"Farm Kid Friday."
"If you know, you know."
""
"#normal40 "
"#thetrade"
"#ramble"

I bet I know you:"
"The relentless feeling of your time being wasted."
""
"The growing awareness of your dreams that go unchased."
""
"The burden of upholding the image of all you have become."
""
"The frustration of creating a lifestyle that now holds you in place."
""
"The guilt of having so much, but wanting something else. "
""
"The certainty of a greater legacy, and the uncertainty about how to build it. "
""
"The pressure to accumulate as much as possible from where you are, at the expense of time with family, where they are."
""
"The fear of believing you may never chase what’s in you, and…"
"The annoyance you feel for not knowing how to define it."
""
"The wish you carry for the courage to face it."
"The desire you have to one day live it."
""
"The shame you feel for holding it in."
"The hope you have to one day start."
"The loneliness of doing it alone."
"The sign you seek to start."
""
"If you are here, it’s not an accident. "
""
"It’s not an algorithmic miracle, and it’s not a mistake. You are here because you are searching. You are here, and you are not alone. You are here because I have been there."
""
"I’ve dedicated the last year of my life to your journey, and how to understand it. I show up here and give your feelings a home. And I have become a teacher for how to navigate it."
""
"When you are ready to finally start, you will know. It happens at the exact moment the fear of never starting over takes the fear of ever quitting."
""
"Dude, you do not have to quit who you have become to finally start. But you do have to finally start if you ever hope to quit."
""

How you experience freedom will change. It changes slowly, and you will feel the change in your gut long before you can articulate what you feel."
""
"I call this “The Turn.”"
""
"Before The Turn, your freedom is felt through an ability to say yes to whatever you want. It is your ability to buy, travel and accumulate that allows you to feel free."
"— The scorecard: Your garage."
""
"After The Turn, your freedom is felt through your ability to say no to whatever you don’t want. And to say no without fear of consequence to your image, title, trajectory, or income."
"  — The scorecard: Your calendar."
""
"Navigating The Turn is not easy. "
"Dude, it’s probably why you’re here."
""
"But when The Turn finds you, find me. "
"Welcome to the Normal 40."
"Nobody said it was easy."
"Let’s ramble. "

This is the last mile of a four-mile lap I’ve run hundreds of times."
""
"In this photo, you can see the farm where I grew up."
""
"My family built the fence, tilled the soil, and has worked this land for generations."
""
"Even the road itself, after being vacated by the township, is now under the care of our family. After all, and according to the township, it doesn’t really lead you anywhere."
""
"On this mile, the last mile, I chose to run with my head up."
""
"In the miles before, I could be distracted by uneven ground, thoughts of fatigue, or by a nearby sound."
""
"But on this mile, the last mile, I run above the pain as I reel in the distance between where I am, and the place that built me."
""
"The four-mile trek around the home section has come to represent a lot in life:"
"- The aggressive start down the road as we leave home."
"- Entering the second mile; uncharted territory as we move further from our roots."
"- Then, in the far distance, in our self-made world, somewhere in mile three, the outline of home in the distance starts its gentle tug."
""
"As I move closer, step by step, the endowed memories of a thousand laughs and a thousand struggles romance in my mind."
""
"In the final mile, I give thanks for each step behind me and all the lessons each step has taught me."
""
"Finally, I give thanks to the mile ahead, the one that’s vacated but takes me back to where I started."
""
"Home isn’t the place you leave. It’s the place you return to, physically or mentally, when you need it."
""
"This is my place. This is the place that grounds me. This is the place that connects my past to my future as I live in the present."
""
"Where is your place? Maybe it’s time to pay it a visit."
""
"Welcome to the Normal 40 highway.  Dude, I’m glad you’re here."
""
"If you need a place, maybe someday you can come to mine."
""

Scars: They tell us who is playing the game, and who is watching it."
""
"A well-lived life leaves scars. Scars of bets made, chances taken, opportunities pursued, and shots missed—scars from life in the game, not in the stands."
""
"Scars do more than make an appearance; they have a story. They start as missteps or misreads. They travel through bouts of personal betrayal, wreckless processes, and politics. Scars, while still wounds, will cause you to wonder where all the support went, will cause you to rethink how and if you’ll ever renter the game, and will leave you feeling alone and damaged. "
""
"Earning the scars of life can suck. But your scars are proof that you were in the game."
""
"If you live brave enough, events that feel like failure will find you, and it will suck."
""
"But I don’t feel sorry for you. I never will. Instead …."
""
"I feel sorry for those who get in your way after you’ve reckoned with the loss and have learned how to use it."
""
"I feel sorry for those who don’t yet have the battle experience you do, and I feel sorry for those too weak to take a position bold enough ever to risk it."
""
"I feel sorry for those who bear the wound of regret for never taking the shot and wishing they had, and for those who want to start but never do."
""
"Dude, there is nothing more powerful than someone who has reckoned with a loss and has shaped it into a weapon."
""
"Leaders are built through their scars earned on the field; they are not destroyed by them."
""
"Onlookers are destroyed by their wounds that fester while watching from the sidelines. Wounds that never find their scar."
""
"When you want off the sidelines and into the game, I want you to hear this: You are ready. That’s why you are here. "
""
"Now, let’s close the wound and put your scars to work."
""
"You’ll never regret the view from the field."
""
"It’s your ball, boss."
"Own it."

Where will you be in six months from today?"
""
"From June 1999 to February 2022, I was on the climb."
"Then, I walked away. No backup. No guarantees. All me."
""
"I traded certainty of great pay at a world-class company and…"
"I traded the big title and an executive office. "
""
"I traded the stock options and the bonus potential, and …"
"I traded the image that took me fifteen years to create."
""
"It wasn’t easy, and it wasn’t free. It was, in fact, a trade."
""
"I wasn’t running from anything. I loved my company, and I was good at my work. I was well compensated, was in all significant decisions, and worked with some of the best;  we did big things."
""
"So, why did I leave?"
""
"Simply, my work there was done, and I knew it. I felt it. "
""
"Resigning took a day. "
"Preparing to leave took years."
""
"By the time I separated, I was ready. "
"And I was made ready by following a process."
""
"If you’re still reading this, something is burning in you. You have two choices:"
""
"1.) Work to define it, or "
"2.) Tolerate never finding out. "
""
"Six months from today, one of you will have explored a trade. The other will still be where you are today, only older."

"Which of these are you?"

“Join Meeting.” You get to hear it all on this one. "
""
"On paper, they have nothing in common."
"In reality, they all share the same story.  "
""
"My job: Give them a home, and"
"Help them discover what lives next."
""
"People wait weeks for a Normal 40 ramble; it’s worth the wait. Most leave the call with more than they thought possible. Not bad for a free ramble."
""
"How do I know? You tell me. You send notes, and you change one small thing about your reality. And I see it in your face and hear it in your voice. And you? Well, you feel it inside."
""
"Ramblers leave believing that maybe, just maybe, this was the first day of their next chapter. And they leave knowing they’ve got someone who is in their corner. "
""
"I never know who I will pick up along the Normal 40 Highway when I hit “Join Meeting.” But after hundreds of calls, I’ve never been disappointed. "
""
"If I could play all rambles live — every ramble — you wouldn’t believe the stories this dude from the internet gets to be a part of. But you would also discover that we’re not all that different inside. We all want the same basic things, and we all struggle to accept it."
""
"On this week's episode, you get to listen to a ramble with Andrew Zanet — and you get to hear it at “Join Meeting.”"
""
"It will take you less than a few minutes to decide this is a dude to know."
""
"In 2022, Andrew made The Trade. Professionally, he had it all — more than he thought possible. Then, between Covid and kids, his priorities shifted.  "
""
"Andrew and I ramble from his farm in New South Wales, Australia. And this will not be the last time he and I have a chance to ramble. "
""
"Find Normal 40: The Podcast everywhere you listen."

Readiness: A word that has stifled a million dreams. "
""
"We all want it. We work towards it. We move with it. You buy things that help you achieve it. You coach others on it. And when you feel like you finally have it for yourself, you will finally act."
""
"The quest for readiness is a temptress; a thief. But it’s also why you are here."
""
"Readiness is a state of mind. There isn’t a checklist, a master key, or a spreadsheet that will give you the calm of readiness. It’s a word we use to mask what it really is: fear of getting it wrong."
""
"If you’ve ever wondered if you’re ready — you are."
"If you’re wondering if you’re ready right now — you are."
""
"There will be a split second when it happens — the moment you know, in your gut, that you are, in fact, f***ing ready. "
""
"At that moment, you have a choice to make:"
" - Extend your past, ot"
" - Build your legacy."
""
"Dude, eventually, you will act, but you will never feel ready. Get over it, get under it, get around it, or run your ass through it. But quit waiting for it."
""
"When it’s your time, don’t consult your readiness. Consult your sturdiness and your courage to finally start. "
""
"When it’s your time, find someone who has done it and use them as a guide. That will be all you need. "
""
"Listen to me: You are ready. Now … "
""
"Will you extend your past, or"
"build your legacy?"
""
"You own it."
""

"#thetrade"

"Remember your aspirations? You would share them at the bar and corporate mixers. Your aspirations fueled you and gave a sense of direction to your decisions—all of them. "
""
"Most likely, what you are doing today was once an aspiration—the dream of what you will one day be."
""
"The first date with your wife, having a family, getting the house, buying the car, and landing the job — all the result of the aspirations you organized your life around."
""
"But what about now? You never talk of aspirations anymore."
""
"It’s a more complicated question now than it used to be. Unlike when you were 30, you have something to lose…or so we think. Slowly, over a decade, your aspiration gets ground down into the heavy dust of toleration."
""
"Now, here you are. Reading a note from some dude on the internet. A dude who has been exactly where you are."
""
"If you are here, you are searching (yes, you are). You are here secretly searching for the same essential thing: Clarity for what’s next, and the courage to chase it."
""
"But here’s the hard truth: Clarity will not be found using the earth-moving tools of an architect. Clarity will be found using the sand brushes of an archeologist."
""
"Somewhere under the decads of grinding, you will find your beat-down and long-abandoned aspirations. They will be hard to excavate, and they will be delicate, but they are there."
""
"The aspirations of who you are capable of being rarely change. Those aspirations are in you."
""
"The anxiety you feel? The Sunday put you feel? The discontent you feel? The thing that brings you here? — These are your aspirations trying to claw their way out."
""
"Your aspirations still dream of who it is you will one day be. And they still have hope. "
""
"The most important work of your life is to get clear on who you aspire to be, and then create a plan to get there."
""

What’s the deal with Easter eggs, and what is an Easter bunny?"
""
"Well, if you’re also curious, this is what I found. "
""
"Eggs have been a symbol of fertility and rebirth in many cultures for thousands of years. Early Christians used eggs to symbolize parts of the Easter story."
""
"In early churches, eggs were blessed by the priests and distributed to the congregants at the end of the Paschal vigil, which is the Saturday before Easter known as Holy Saturday. During the Paschal vigil, Scripture is read, candles are lit, and baptisms occur. During the service, “Easter eggs” were handed out to symbolize Jesus’ resurrection: The hard shell represents the sealed tomb, and the cracking of the egg represents Jesus’ resurrection from the dead."
""
"But there’s more…"
"Early Christians also abstained from eating eggs and meat during Lent, a period of 40 days where Christians fast, repent, and pray to prepare for Easter. Therefore, Easter was the first time Christians could eat eggs after 40 days of fasting."
""
"Okay, but why do we dye Easter eggs?"
""
"Originally, Christians dyed eggs red to represent the blood that was shed on the cross (this was lost on my last night while dyeing eggs last night with my family). Over time, this tradition spread throughout Eastern churches and eventually reached European churches."
""
"Later, according to Good Housekeeping, early Christian missionaries dyed the eggs in different colors to represent different aspects of the Easter story. They used yellow to represent the resurrection, blue to express love, and red to represent the blood of Christ."
""
"And hiding the eggs; what’s that about?"
"Early Christian missionaries hid Easter eggs painted with biblical scenes for children to find. The children would find the Easter eggs and tell the story associated with the paintings. Therefore, early Easter egg hunts helped children learn about the significance of Easter."
""
"One of the earliest Easter egg hunts most resembling the modern Easter egg hunt can be traced to Martin Luther. During this time, men would hide eggs for women and children to find. The joy experienced as they found eggs are said to have emulated the joy the women felt when they found Jesus’ empty tomb."
""
"And the bunny?"
""
"According to Time Magazine, the Easter bunny first arrived in America in the 1700s with German immigrants who settled in Pennsylvania and transported their tradition of an egg-laying hare called “Osterhase.” "
""
"Children made nests in which this creature - a bunny - could lay its colored eggs. Eventually, the custom spread across the U.S., and the fabled rabbit’s Easter morning deliveries expanded to include chocolate and other candy and gifts, while decorated baskets replaced nests. "
""
"Or, in my house, the Easter basket has sort of become a trial run for Christmas."
""
"Now you know. "
"And so do I."
""
"Now, where’s my basket?"
""

""
"Then COVID hit, and now I’m sort of around. Shit, not sort of around; I am home—all day. I don’t know how to describe it, but I felt like I was, well, like the world I knew had disappeared."
""
"One day I woke up, and I asked myself, what have I been missing for 20 years? My young boys were almost teenagers, my wife barely tolerating me, and I felt no freedom or control in my life. It had all been sold."
""
"I couldn’t put what I was feeling into words, and I’m the f***ing attorney. Then you showed up. I don’t know how, but there you were—calling me out and telling my story. Making me own it. Making me think about the next 40 years. So, dude, that’s why I’m here; I want to work on my next 40. "
""
"Lon: Tell me about the moment you knew."
""
"Dude: It actually started before Covid: It was February 1, 2019. I know that because that’s bonus day at the office - and mine had just hit the bank."
""
"It was 6:12 AM, and I was in the office by myself. I logged in to see my bank balance. It was in there, and it was big. Then, for some reason, I went back to see what I made in 2009, simply to prove some progress, I guess. Shit, I don’t know. But that’s what I did."
""
"When I looked, my 2009 pay was a fraction of what I just had deposited. My check in 2019 was four and a half times larger. Four and a half times! I was grateful and happy, but that lasted maybe, maybe a few days. Then, nothing. Emptyness. And I didn’t know why. Something was wrong with me."
""
"I tried a lot of things to figure myself out. I made a hobby of trying things to fix mem but nothing works. "

""

“How do you know when it is REALLY time to make The Trade?”"
""
"Since leaving my corporate life 14 months ago, this question has come up a lot. It’s the central question of why I am here."
""
"Let’s be honest; it’s the central question of why you are here, too. This is interesting…"
""
"Yesterday, Jean Smith, who is pretty awesome, asked this question - but with a slight adder. She used the word “REALLY” (in all caps) to emphasize the question."
""
"It changed everything."
""
"Let’s talk about the “REALLY.”"
"It’s what we’re all looking for, really."
""
"Look, our minds are fantastic at connecting other people’s dots. We can look at someone else's situation and our minds will build a story of them that works best for us."
""
"Yes, you do that."
""
"Look at my story. Look at what I did. Look at what I am doing - and judge it. For a moment, we can both pretend you haven’t already judged it. It will be fun."
""
"You have connected the dots about me. You have connected dots that may not be there — about how my story was possible FOR ME. You’ve decided that it was somehow easier, I was somehow wealthier, or I was somehow better gifted to leave a job I liked for a life I wanted more."
""
"You connect dots in my life - dots in my story - dots that allow my trade to look easier than yours. Dots that make it okay for me to do it but also make it okay for you to wait. Dots that make my decision look obvious, and yours look harder."
""
"Look, I did that for years before I left. I can give you the names of people I would say it about. It doesn’t make me bad; it makes me normal. It makes you normal, too."
""
"But when you start using the word REALLY, you accidentally change the owner—from someone else, to you."
""
"So, how do you REALLY know?"
""
"When you can’t quit thinking about it."
"When you can’t quit dreaming about it."
"When you find yourself frustrated about it."
"When you find yourself getting pissed off about it."
"When you look for friends who are also going through it."
"When you feel like you have nothing more to give to your job."
"When you feel like you have nothing more to learn from your job."
""
"When you know your best days are in front of you."
"When you know your best gifts have more to give."
""
"When you want to take a chance on yourself."
"When you want to test who it is you’re capable of being."
""
"How do you REALLY know?"
""
"When the fear of never trying becomes scarier than the thought of leaving — you are ready."
""
"REALLY."
""
"Warning - You will know before you feel ready."
""
"And when you know, you add the word “REALLY” to your search. That makes it about you."
""
"It’s your brain's way of looking for some dots to connect. It’s your brain seeking the sign, the plan, the answers, and the outcome - so that the decision is obvious; it never is."
""
"It’s the sign we all want before we trade, but it’s something we never get."
""
"When you use words like REALLY, it’s a sign that you're ready. Now you’re not asking for a friend. You’re asking for you."
""
"When that day finds you, find me. "
""
"REALLY."
""

Last night I found it hard to sleep. That doesn’t happen much anymore. But it found me after several dudes connected with me this weekend to share their symptoms."
""
"Some people have come to call this Sunday Scaries. For some reason, that falls short. That’s too cute."
""
"The scaries are something a first grader has. This is not that."
""
"This is different. This is a pit. This is a hole in the chest you can feel and a mind that races so hard it can’t be stopped. This is not cute."
""
"This is the uncontrollable churn in the gut. This is the racing thoughts of an impossible workload and the inability to find even an hour of reasonable sleep."
""
"Scary? No."
""
"This is more than scary. This is the pressure of life. This is the stress of a job that doesn’t allow you to leave, and a boss with a knack for stealing your calm. Scary is a threat; this is real."
""
"This is the stress of a faltering project, a report that isn’t done, or a problem that isn’t yet fixed."
""
"This is the stress of the call you’ve not returned and the guilt of the help you offered but haven’t delivered on. This is the pressure of knowing you could have done more, believing others will think you should have done more, and wishing you would have done more. "
""
"This is the anxiety for the emails that don’t quit, the text messages that are piling up, a calendar that others have filled beyond belief, and the stack of updates on your desk."
""
"This is not scary. This is the frustration."
""
"The frustration over the lack of control over your time, your priorities, and your life. It’s the fear of believing you may never catch up."
""
"Scarry? No. This is more than that."
""
"This is the pressure of providing enough to pay the medical bills, to make enough to cover the mortgage, and to afford the lifestyle."
""
"This is the pressure of saving for retirement, paying for the day, and making damn sure that nobody else in your house needs to worry about it as you do."
""
"And all of this before you even have a chance to be a good husband, dad, or neighbor."
""
"Scarry? I wish. This is … life. Your life."
""
"The one you have built — and the one that has built you. "
""
"What you are feeling is not scary."
"What you’re feeling is frustration. "
""
"The utter, complete, and all-consuming frustration over your lack of control in the life you have built—the frustration of having no control over your time, priorities, and energy."
""
"The frustration of knowing you have all that you’ve chased but that you’ve traded away your freedom to get it."
""
"Frustration that you can’t enjoy dinner with your spouse, a trip with your family, or time with your friends without feeling equal pressure from your work."
""
"That isn’t success. That is prison."
""
"And that is scary. "
""
"When it’s finally your turn to deal with this, we should ramble."
""
"You literally have nothing to lose - but more of your time."
""

The story of your life builds slowly, but lives swiftly — without reverse."
""
"The story of your life comes down to the decisions you make—decisions in moments of conflict, tolerance, confusion, and temptation—and how you carry yourself through the results."

"In the book of your life. Last week’s story has been written. It was authored through your moments of conflict, confusion, temptation, and tolerance. "
""
"In the book of your life, next week’s story is not written. But it will have moments of conflict, confusion, and temptation. "
""
"You may choose to tolerate the week as it finds you and allow that to be your story."
""
"— Or — "
""
"You may choose to shape the week in front of you and build the narrative of this chapter of your life. "
""
"The story of your life will be written. You will write it, or you will allow others to write it for you. But the pages are being filled, even as you read. "
""
"This is true for me, and it is true for you."
""
"The story of your life doesn’t write easily. The decisions you will make along the way are not obvious. The pressure you feel to keep one foot in the past while trying to put one into the future is real. The decision to simply tolerate another day is normal."

"When the day comes when you are done tolerating, when you are ready to do the work, when you are ready to start planning your next chapter, when you are ready to take the pen, and you are ready to write your own story …"
""
" … When that time finds you — Own it."

"Let that be your story. "
""
"The story that owns the fact that you didn’t run. You refused to hide. You would not tolerate. You didn’t acquiesce. You didn’t settle. "
""
"Let your story show you traded for it. Let your story be that you bet on it. Let your story be that you went for it. Let your story be that you didn’t stay because it was easy, but you chased a dream because it was hard."
""
"Your story starts with a decision - a decision you are in control of."
""
"Today, you are writing that story. Your story. Your actions are filling the pages. What will the pages say about this day? "
""
"You are writing that story today."

"I’ve launched a community for people ready to own their pages. It’s filled with a few people just like you. A place where you don’t have to do this alone, and a place where your title is useless. "

Success is a gift, but it carries a cruel and ironic curse."
"The Gift: All you have endured has prepared you for what is next."
""
"The Curse: All you have accumulated now prevents you from chasing it."
""
"So you wait. You wish. You wonder. You tolerate another yesterday. Then, slowly, you simply hope to maintain. Before you know it, your the paranoid old guy you used to talk about."
""
"Success has a price - and we pay that in the grind of the climb."
""
"It’s paid in 70-hour weeks, missed birthdays, regrettable moods with kids, shortness of time with spouse, abandonment of friendships, airport meals, extra weight, alcohol wrinkles, poor health, and airline miles, and shit stories you tell to impress people whose names you only recall after a glance at their business card when you arrive home and throw them on your desk."
""
"But here’s what really sucks: Success also has a residual tax. A tax we pay every day through our inability to leave who we’ve become — in our minds and with our stuff."
""
"Look, dude, success has already stolen parts of your past, but at least it left you with stuff. The success tax is working right now and is here to steal pieces of your future - and it intends to leave you with regret."
""
"That feeling you feel in your gut as you read this — that’s not the fear of doing something new. That’s the fear of knowing you will have to give up something old."
""
"Success is not free; it is a trade. "
"Doing what’s next won’t be free; it will be a trade."
""
"A trade you will make, or one you will not. "
""
"Today, that feeling is simply you paying your taxes."
""
"Success tends to leave us with some really cool stuff to see on the outside, but with a churning regret of tolerance smoldering on the inside."
""
"My mission is to end that tax, and to inspire you to act."
"It won’t happen overnight. "
"But it will happen."
"Pay the tax, or"
"Live the life."
"Own it."

"Every day I talk to people just like you. They look like you, talk like you, feel like you, and struggle to make the trade — like you. "
""
"You are wrestling with what will be one of the greatest burdens - or opportunities - of your lifetime: To ride out what you have built or to chase what you know is in you?"
""
"You are hoping for an obvious answer, but it’s not there. So, you sit alone feeling the discontent of a ticking clock, the bills that never shrink, and the stress of a lifetime that no longer fulfills you."
""
"This month, this quarter, this year, and for the rest of this decade, it will become the central question of your life: Will you chase it, or will you let it pass?"
""

WARNING: The people you show up here to read, follow, and engage with will establish the baseline for what you believe is possible — not for them, but for you."
""
"Before you fall in love with someone’s content, story, or craft, make them prove it. Do your due diligence. Make sure they are worth your two most significant assets - your time and admiration."
""
"Dude, this content doesn’t find you by accident. It is not fed to you randomly. And these words - right here - aren’t part of an algorithm or an advertisement you didn’t expect. They have found you through your search for more or different — knowingly or not."
""
"You probably wonder why you’re here."
""
"You wonder what keeps you coming back. What about this random dude from the internet who keeps showing up in your feed? And how can you both crave it and be entirely annoyed by it simultaneously? What is the guilty curiosity that stops you from scrolling?"
""
"If you don’t know, I do."
""
"You see yourself in the words."
"You hear your voice in the tone."
"You feel the pressure growing in the lines."
"You can see the life you hope to chase, and you can feel the crushing pain of knowing that you might never try."
""
"You are here because — "
"You see the ingredients of a life you want to chase."
"You see a dude who took a risk without any guarantees."
" - A dude who traded a successful life to chase a new one."
" - A dude who is doing what you’ve dreamed of."
" - A dude with the ingredients of a life you want live."
" - A dude who didn’t have the answers but did it anyway."
" - A dude responded to what you feel brewing inside of you."
" - A dude who maybe isn’t all that different from … you."
""
"You see a dude who dared to finally emerge from the shadows — the shadows you’re currently living in—the shadow of your former self."
""
"I get it. I was you. But I quit tolerating yesterday. I decided to step out of the shadows for me — and for others who haven’t yet."
""
"When it’s time for you to peek outside the shadows, hit me up for a ramble."
""
"Dude, seriously,  they are free."
""
"I don’t give away thousands of hours of my life so that you can get something out of me."
""
"I give up thousands of hours of my life so that maybe — just maybe — we can finally get something out of you."
""
"Dude, I’ll show up for the maybe - and I hope you do, too."
""
"After all, that’s not my shadow you’re standing in; it’s yours."
""
"What’s next?"
""
"1.) Grab this week's podcast: The Awkward Conversation. "
""
"2.) Sign up for The Normal 40 Rambler: Notes from me to you."
""
"3.) Join my private & off LinkedIn community, “The Insider.”"
""
"4.) Join the waitlist for my small-group mastermind"
" - Vintage One is sold out, but you can be part of Vintage Two."
""
"5.) Receive a pre-publication copy of my book “The Trade.”"
""
"6.) Do nothing. Keep wondering. Keep avoiding. "
" "
"You will do at least one of the above; choose wisely."
""
"All are available on the normal40 website."
"Most of it is free. All are for you."

When I hit “Join Meeting” I never know who I am about to meet."
""
"Six times yesterday, I hit “Join Meeting.” Six times I was placed in front of one of the most inspiring, engaging, articulate, wise, and decent people the world has to offer."
""
"They arrive to the call through a lifetime of unique decisions, walking a path that nobody before them has dared to walk."
""
"They arrive to the call not knowing what to expect, not able to articulate their feeling, and not sure what a dude from the internet could possibly do to help them find the control, the calm, and the perspective they once had."
""
"They show up anyway."
""
"They arrive to the call believing their best days are in front of them, but unsure how to live them. They arrive battle worn from years of wonder - is this all there will ever be?"
""
"I have seen this face, this path, this journey - this loneliness, 400 times in the last year. I dedicated a year of my life to exploring it."
""
"I have heard the stories and I have seen the tears. I have felt the anguish and I have rambled along the precarious road of regret."
""
"I’ve heard stories of financial growth, great offices, big titles, wild trips, diamond status, and big deals. And I’ve listened to the accounts of spouses who become roommates, of kids who they never get to know, and the miserable, embarrassing, life-stealing obligation they feel to hold it all together - becase that is the price of success."
""
"I’ve shared tears with those who simply want to feel like they did when they buttoned up their full dress uniform, and those who don’t remember what life was like before the diagnosis."
""
"I’ve learned there are a million ways into a feeling we share, but I’ve only come to discover one way out."
""
"My mission is to give you a home. My mission is to give you a chance. To help you separate who you are for others from who you want to be for yourself."
""
"My mission is to inspire change in a thousand lives and to insure your life against future regret. I will know success when I have a thousand notes of thanks from a thousand people who have made The Trade."
""
"I didn’t set out to make this my mission. I’m simply one of the one-million paths that led to this place. The only difference is that I talk about it, and I help others navigate it."
""
"This is a note I woke up to this morning. This was one of six unbelievable rambles. It’s from a dude I’ve never talked to before, but am certain to talk to again. "
""
"If you’re here, I see you. I hear you, and I write to you. "
""
"I am not here in hopes that you get something out of me."
""
"I’m here because I believe that maybe, just maybe, today may finally be the day I get something out of you."
""
"This week I am opening a private off LinkedIn community called The Insider. It won’t give you any answers; you have those. But it will give you the questions, and offer a home where I share the heartfelt rambles with you. A place where you can safely connect with others, just like you."
""
"Join on my website."
""

This note is from Normal 40 group member #1583."
""
"Matt and I connected in August after he caught an episode of the Normal 40 Podcast. We rambled for the first time in October, and a handful of times since."
""
"We discovered quickly that - his work there was done."
""
"Matt didn’t leave a job he didn’t love. He didn’t leave a boss, a schedule, or a life he couldn’t tolerate living. "
""
"He made The Trade because he refused to accept the regret of never discovering what else life might hold for him. He refused to accept another yesterday while believing it would change his tomorrow."
""
"Matt may not yet appreciate it, but the words etched into the note below will forever be the opening lines of his second-half story—a story he has yet to write on the pages he is free to fill."
""
"These will be the words his kids will someday come to remember most. And these will be the words that will become impossible to regret."
""
"One day I hope Matt joins us on the podcast. He is, without a doubt, a dude to know. "
""
"My life’s mission is to inspire change in the lives of a thousand dudes. And I will know my mission has been fulfilled when we have a thousand notes of thanks from a thousand people who have made The Trade. "
""
"Notes just like this."
""
"Notes that I will read for a lifetime."
""
"Maybe your name is on one of those notes."
""
"If it is … "
""
"Book time to ramble."
"Download the podcast. "
"Get on “The Rambler” email list."
"Get early access to my book: The Trade."
""
"Dude, your life’s direction is impacted most by what you choose to tolerate and what you refuse to tolerate. "
""
"Changing what you tolerate gets awkward."
"Changing what you tolerate changes direction."
""
"When you’re ready: Lon@normal40.com."

A funny thing happens when you stare back at a lifetime of work — It starts to stare back."
""
"It starts to point out all the areas you rushed, and it points out all the work you have yet to do — work that seems less important now than it did years ago."
""
"You have built your life—all of it."
""
"It wasn’t handed to you; it was created through your work."
"It wasn’t easy; it was hard."
"It wasn’t free; it was a trade."
""
"A trade of your time in meetings, calls, airports, hotels, evening dinners, crisis management, presentations, pitches, plans, memos, and sleepless nights in preparation for the next morning."
""
"The seat you occupy right now wasn’t a gift, and it wasn’t an accident. It was by design."
""
"For twenty years, you have been the architect of your life - this life. It was your plan, your blueprint, your design, and your sheer will that got you here."
""
"You are the architect of your life."
""
"Along the way, people showed up with their tools, skills, and connections, and they helped you — just like you did for them."
""
"The sum of every architectural decision in your life has delivered you here, to this exact moment."
""
"Every moment of your life has led you to these lines. "
""
"This is the life you know."
"This is the life you’re good at."
"This is your masterpiece."
""
"But the masterpiece is starting to consume you. Wrose, it chides you for being so creative near the bottom when you started, and so predictable near the top, where you have been for years. And it demands more of your time to make it right - or it will be taken."
""
"Then -"
""
"A distant voice begs you to look around the footings, at the tools and the passions that remain unused, untouched and ignored. To see the dreams and skills you thought would be used in your masterpiece, but sit idle."
""
"As you kick around the sand, you see more—lost passions, friendships, intimacy, energy, and memories of who it is you used to be, and how it is you used to find joy."
""
"You slowly dig into the sand and you find more. More of who you used to be, more of who you once wanted to be, and more of who you are wired to be. And energy comes back."
""
"So you dig."
""
"Soon your archeologists' crane is traded for a hand shovel, your riveting gun traded for sand brushes, and your safety harness is traded for the kneepads of a researcher finding what’s been covered by decades of tolerance."
""
"Slowly, you become more inspired by the masterpiece you have yet to uncover for yourself to see, than the one you spent your life building for the world to see."
""
"The pit in your gut is not because your architecture didn’t reach high enough; it’s because the archeologist in you is begging you to play in the dirt."
""
"The pit reminds you that the answers you seek won’t be found by building out; they will be found by digging in."
""
"Eventually, you learn the life you want isn’t in a harness high above others; it’s on the ground, with your family, unearthing the gems already around you."
""
"When you are ready to dig — Lon@normal40.com."
""

"#thetrade"

“More freedom.” That’s why you’re here. I know that because you have told me that—243 times this year."
""
"But that’s not the entire story. It never is."
""
"“More freedom” is the answer we all give when asked, “What do you want?” "
""
"It’s also the answer you give when you are not clear on what you really want. Freedom is the easy answer. It’s the answer that can’t be wrong. It’s the answer that feels right. Who doesn’t want more freedom?"
""
"But freedom isn’t the answer - freedom is the outcome — The outcome of an experience you have yet to define."
""
"Freedom can be coveted by someone working 70 hours a week, and simultaneously by a spouse living in the same house, who was recently laid off."
""
"Each desperately wants what the other has a chance to hate. While both have the same answer to “What do you want?” Freedom."
""
"Freedom is not the outcome of your decisions. Your decisions are the outcome of your freedom - the freedom you already have - or had … before you let it melt away in your grind for more."
""
"What if you decided, right now, that anger was not an emotion you would use today? What if you freed yourself of that emotion?"
""
"What if you decided, right now, that guilt and anxiety were not feelings you would tolerate today? What if you freed yourself from the burden of feeling the pressure that doesn’t deserve to be in your life?"
""
"What if you decided, right now, that assuming the stress of your boss or coworker was not something you would tolerate today? To help them, yes. To be stressed for them, hell no."
""
"What if, right now, you permitted yourself to reject the last-minute meeting that will keep you from your family this evening?"
""
"What if you permitted yourself to avoid taking work home over the weekend? "
""
"What if you did all of these - today? "
""
"Dude, that is freedom. And you have it."
"What you don’t have is control. "
""
"Today … Let’s take it back. "
""
"What if you promised yourself, right now, that you would have a lighter mood all day? What freedom would that give you?"
""
"What if, right now, you committed to using the word “fantastic” five times in conversation today? What freedom would that give others?"
""
"What if you permitted yourself to leave on time today, to be home on time, and to be with your family for dinner? "
""
"If you accept that control - if you take it back from whoever you allowed to snatch it from you, how freeing might this day feel? "
""
"Sure, maybe you can’t do all of these today. "
""
"But you certainly can find a way to use the word “fantastic” today to compliment others. "
""
"Start with fantastic and see where freedom’s ripple takes you."
""
"Freedom you have. It’s your control over it that you need to take back."
""
"As you take it back, feel good knowing your Normal 40 sent you, and your Next 40 will thank you."
""
"That is what freedom feels like. "
"And I can already feel the ripples."
"And that, my friend, is fantastic."
""

Something you found interesting 20 years ago has delivered you to where you are today. If you think hard enough, you can recall the exact moment that someone said, did, or arranged something that has led you to the chair you are sitting in now."
""
"Look, we all start out pursuing what is interesting. Med school, law school, business school, drama, journalism, the arts, welding, sales, engineering, mechanics, accounting and finance, computer and technology … It is - or was - interesting to you."
""
"If you’re lucky, it remains interesting for the rest of your professional life."
""
"If you’re normal, it does not."
""
"Over time, your interest has leveled off. The passion for it has dwindled, and you have started to stagnate. You still perform, you just don’t find the work to be … interesting."
""
"Thankfully, you’ve become good at hiding all you are tolerating."
""
"There’s another problem - a bigger one. In fact, it has become the real problem. Now you are important."
""
"You have the title. The office. The team. The reputation. The lifestyle. The published articles. The clients … You have what you chased. You have what you thought you wanted when your kids were in preschool."
""
"To make it worse, almost everyone in your company would trade seats with you - and you know it. That used to be something you wanted, but now it’s just another wall of the box you find yourself in."
""
"Well, you’re not alone. I talk to people every day who are where you are. Most see it as a failure - because it feels like one. But this isn’t the result of failure, it is the result of your success. Only success can deliver you here. And it usually does. BUT - "
""
"The failure is in never facing how you are coming to feel. The failure is learning to tolerate how you feel."
""
"Here’s the deal: It’s okay to lose passion for the work that used to excite you. It is okay to get 20 years in and want a change. It’s okay to want something more or something different. It’s okay that you no longer wish to be the person you set out to become years ago."
""
"It is not okay to think this will heal itself. It’s not okay to settle for the life you have because of the image you’ve manufactured. It’s not okay to feel like your best days are in front of you and to tolerate never living them."
""
"Dude, life presents us with a lot of challenges. Most of them require us to react to them: We react to a death, a diagnosis, a disaster, a downsizing - because we have to. "
""
"This challenge is different — it haunts you. It stalks you. It lurks around in your mind as you wish it away. You tell yourself it will go away, or that tomorrow it will be easier. "
""
"For as long as you tolerate it, the feeling never goes away."
""
"This is The Trade. It will be one of the hardest things you do - by choice - in your lifetime. Or, in another twenty years, it will be one of the things that haunt you. Either way, this is on you."
""
"You will get what you tolerate. "
"Quit tolerating yesterday."
""
"See comments for more."

In life, there is a time to wage war. It will be on you to wage it."
""
"You don’t wage a war to avoid defeat."
""
"You don’t wage a war believing victory is certain or quickly won."
""
"You wage war because you know you must fight, and that the fight is worth the cost."
""
"You wage war knowing and accepting there will be a cost, and the cost will finally be worth The Trade. "
""
"If there’s a war stirring in your gut, face it."
"If there’s a war brewing in you, name it. Then, go to war."
""
"You have only two options — no more, no less:"
"You will fight, or you will tolerate. "
""
"Which you chose is not anyone else's fault. It is yours."
""
"You are in control. And, ironically, that can be part of the problem. Dude, take it back - your courage, strength, and drive to be more. Take back who you are here to be and go to war for it."
""
"Do not blame a bad boss, a poor marriage, a dishonest partner, a parent who never helped, or a bad work culture. When you give these things your blame, you give them your power. It steals your fight. It convinces you to tolerate it. "
""
"You are not here because you’re bad at waging war. Nope. "
""
"You are here because you’ve become frustratingly good at tolerating what you no longer want. "
""
"Dude, you are where you are today because of exactly who you were yesterday. You are the result of your decisions."
""
"Three years from this day, you will be who you are because of who you decided to be today. And you will become the result of your decisions."
""
"I know what keeps you coming back, and so do you. "
"I can feel it in you as you read this, and so can you."
""
"Some of you will do something about it; maybe that’s you."
"I will be here when you’re finally ready. That’s my mission. "
""
"This is your life. This is your clock. These are your days. The rest of your life is waiting for you to fight for it. To wage war for it. To make The Trade for it. "
""
"If this is your day to wage war, I will wage it alongside you."
""
"My mission is to inspire the change in a thousand lives."
""
"Maybe, just maybe, your life will be one of them."
""
"If it is, well then, maybe it’s time you do something."
""
"Let’s ramble - hit me here: Lon@normal40.com."
""
"Or join my closed and private LinkedIn community (see comments). Or sign up for The Normal 40 Ramble on my website. "
""
"Or, do nothing. "
""
"Dude, I literally can’t make it any easier than this."
""
"You will or you won’t. "
"Either way, you own it."
"Go to war. For you."
""

"#thetrade"

"#onethousanddudes"
"#motivation #inspiration

"This podcast topic is on mission. "

Dear Company Executives,"
""
"I’ve dedicated the last year of my life to engagning with people I’ve never met, in industries I’ve never studied, about the dreams they hope to one day chase."
""
"This is not my typical message, but you should hear this."
""
"After thousands of hours of intimate 1:1 conversations, most people have one thing in common: They all want to leave corporate America."
""
"Sometimes it is not because of you, but sometimes it is."
""
"Regardless of the reason, they want to be done with you, and they want to finally take a bet on themself — AND they want to finally feel like like they have some control over their lives"
""
"Control that they feel you have taken away, slowly, over time."
""
"Every company talks about culture. You should; it matters."
""
"And let’s face it, some cultures are far better than others."
""
"But after thousands of hours of conversations, I will give you my unsolicited advice about how to really improve culture."
""
"Rather than limiting your engagment to only the employees …"
""
"Spend your time also engaging their spouse and kids."
""
"If you want the truth about your culture, employees cannot level with you. You have too much power over them. They will want to share, but they won’t feel that it is “safe.” Again, that’s a culture problem."
""
"If you want the truth about where you’re culture is working or failing, talk to the person who calls your employee “mom,” “dad,” and “honey.”  Talk to the person who shares a roof, a bed, a mortgage and a lifetime with your employees."
""
"You only need to ask them a single question:"
" - What’s the mood like at the dinner table during the week?"
""
"The first test will determine if your culture enables them to be home for dinner. Your second test will be the mood. The third test will be their presence of mind in the conversation. "
""
"You probably don’t know it, but this is the essence of your culture - how your employee feels about you when they are NOT there. "
""
"This is culture."
""
"Culture is more than the benefits, the work ethic, and the wellness programs. It’s way bigger than that. I know because I’ve talked to them—a lot. "
""
"And, oddly, I doubt you’ve ever bothered to ask. And not so oddly, leaders continue to leave or plan to leave."
""
"Look, the most significant power you have over your company culture is not predicated on your ability to set budgets and give raises. It is your ability to influence the mood around the dinner table for everyone who works with you, and their family."
""
"That is the ultimate power. And you tend to abuse it."
""
"Engagning family is hard to do. I know because I’ve tried. But I asked. It was some of the best work of my lifetime."
""
"The answeres weren’t always kind. But it was the fastest way to the root of a problem. "
""
"Culture - As defined by the mood around the dinner table. "
""
"If you set your entire metric system for culture around improving this one thing, you will be unstoppable."
""
"Want to try it? Email me and we can ramble."
""
"Lon@normal40.com"
""

Dear Craven Corner,"
""
"The irony of 51 years."
""
"It may be a little late, but I want to thank you for the armature baseball sponsorship in 1967. It sort of changed my life."
""
"These two photos were not a recreation - but sheer coincidence."
""
"Fifty-six years ago, a roadside supper club called “Craven Corner” sponsored an armature baseball team. My dad played on that team for a few seasons. According to my dad, they were pretty good. "
""
"The photo on the left is of my mom and dad on game day; it was 1967. It was most certainly a Sunday afternoon - because that was game day."
""
"Six years ago, I found the old uniform tucked in a drawer in the basement of our old farmhouse. I tried it on and asked my wife to take a photo with me on my phone. According to me, I thought I looked good in. it. This is the photo on the right."
""
"When my wife and I took the photo, I had never seen the photo of my parents; it was buried in an old photo album ... then ..."
""
"Two years ago, while looking at old photos at the farm, I discovered the photo of my mom and dad from fifty-six years earlier. "
""
"I quickly pulled out my phone and made the Craven Corner connection. "
""
"It’s interesting that we each married 5’ 4” blond-haired teachers who like the color pink. It’s also worthy of note that the uniform only needed to survive one move, about 150 feet, from an old farmhouse to a new one. And Craven Corner was the site of my parent's first date."
""
"Craven Corner, you may not know it, and even though you’ve been closed for decades, your sponsorship continues to leave a legacy."
""
"Thank you, Craven Corner!"
""
"Lon -"
""

story."
""
"Normal 40: The Podcast"
"Episode 14: The Anxiety"
""
"Anxiety and depression isn’t something you order up; it shows up. Uninvited. Unwanted. It shows up mad, and it doesn’t discriminate. It doesn’t care who you are, how much you make, how great your life is, how big your office is, who you lead, or what you do. It doesn’t care. "
""
"This episode shares one dude's story on how it came for him - while at the top of his game - and how it upended his world. "
""
"All of it."
""
"It took his marriage, his money, his job, his laughter, and many of his dreams. "
""
"It did not take his courage, his resolve, his integrity, or his second-half story. "
""
"Why is this dude sharing his story? "
""
"To show that it’s normal. "
""
"To help you understand that it’s real and can bring anyone to their knees. He is doing it to help you understand it might be happening to you, and to help you identify - and support - others who might be going through it. "
""
"He wants you to be a “Manny” for others."
" - This will make sense when you listen to The Pod."
""
"If this episode lands with you, please share it with others. Share it with someone you care about and are worried about. Use THIS episode as a tool to start the conversation. A conversation that might save a life. "
""
"Listen to this episode anywhere you listen to podcasts."
""
"And, if you’re feeling generous, please leave a review. "
"It helps."
""

Aspirations don’t sit still. Aspirations move, migrate, motivate, investigate, and infiltrate your mind. Aspirations provide your mind with the power of hope, but aspirations possess the poison tip of regret. One of which will play out over time - your lifetime. "
""
"Aspiration with action will fill you."
"Aspiration without action will sink you."
""
"Action with aspiration will burn you into something you love."
"Action without aspiration will burn you out of something you, at best, learn to tolerate."
""
"So, let me ask you: Are these still your aspirations?"
""
"Is all you have built all you want? "
"Is all you have become all you hope to be?"
"Is all this job will deliver all you hope to achieve? "
""
"Is more of this what you want for you? "
"Is more of this what your wife wants for you?"
"Is more of this what your kids need from you?"
""
"Aspirations don’t change overnight."
""
"For most, they get ground down and remolded into tolerance over a decade of grinding - for something you’re no longer passionate about."
""
"But for the lucky, or maybe those who stop and do the work occasionally, before tolerance becomes the only safe answer, some make room for other aspirations to take root, and they nurture them."
""
"Some invest in new aspirations. Some challenge their old ambitions. Some take time to themself to really study their aspirations. "
""
"Some tolerate yesterdays. "
""
"Look, dude, nobody wants to be sanded down into tolerance, protectionism, and living with the anxiety of depending on a job more than it may depend on you."
""
"But yet, here you are. You are still reading a post from some dude on the internet. A dude, I will remind you, who knows you, sees you, and has been precisely where you are "
""
"So, again, are these still your aspirations?"
"Is more of this really what you want? "
""
"Dude, I’m asking for a friend."
""
"I’m asking for you."
""
"I keep showing up here for you, boss. Someday, I would suggest you show up for yourself. We both know that’s what we’re waiting for. "
""
"My mission is to get to you. My mission is to finally break you out of the box you’ve worked yourself into and get you to accept that maybe, just maybe, this is the time to act."
""
"My mission is to inspire you and 999 others. And I will know my life has had an impact when I have 1000 thank you notes from dudes who have been inspired to act. "
""
"I want you to be one of those one thousand dudes. I want you to be one of the one thousand notes of thanks that I will cherish for the rest of my life."
""
"I know my aspirations. And you are in them."
""
"Aspirations don’t have to be huge, but they do have to be yours."
""
"If this is finally your day to become part of my aspiration, find me here:"
""
"Lon@normal40.com"
""
"It’s your ball, boss."
""
"#normal40"
"#thetrade"
"#inspiration "
"#next40"
"#onethousanddudes"

What if you believed you could - and you did?"
"What if you believed you could - but you never tried?"
""
"What if you wondered what is in you, and you find out?"
"What if you wondered what is in you, and you waited?"
""
"What if you make the plan and test it?"
"What if you thought about a plan and ignored it?"
""
"What if you invested money to test your dream?"
"What if you saved your money and never found out?"
""
"What if every experience has prepared you for this moment?"
"What if every moment keeps begging for an experience?"
""
"What if you finally lean in and give it your time?"
"What if you never lean in, and it gives you regret?"
""
"What if you made the trade, and it wasn’t what you wanted?"
"What if you never traded and always wondered if it was?"
""
"What if your boss discovers your dreams - and asks you?"
"What if your boss discovers your dreams - and shares his?"
""
"What if you leave and decide to go back?"
"What if you stay and get let go?"
""
"What if you chase a dream that isn’t yet clear?"
"What if you are clear on what your dream is not, and you settle?"
""
"What if you’re here to finally get something out of me?"
"What if I’m here to finally get something out of you?"
""
"What if you book a ramble, and it doesn’t change anything?"
"What if you book a ramble, and it changes everything? "
""
"What if you believed you could do it, and you did?"
"What if you chased the life that you wanted?"
"What if you accepted that this is your time?"
"What if you invest in who you might be?"
"What if you make this the day to start?"
"What if you change your future today?"
""
"Where will you be in a year if you do?"
"Where will you be in a year when you don’t?"
""
"I’m here to get something out of you, boss."
"Oddly, you’re here for the same reason."
"Maybe, just maybe, this is your day."
"If it is, this is where you find me."
""
"From this point in your life forward, nobody shows up to do it for you."
""
"It’s your ball, boss. "
"You can pass, or "
"You can shoot."
""
"The post below — Came from a shooter."
"Which are you? "
""
"Welcome to the #normal40 highway."

LANGUAGE WARNING AHEAD:"
"Ugh, Monday. The day you are reunited with the inbox of work you bravely left behind on Friday. The work you thought you might work on over the weekend, but didn’t."
""
"You are already building your mental to-do list, and the hours in your day are already entirely spent, and so is your emotional energy."
""
"As your energy goes down, your anxiety goes up. "
""
"It’s the perfect emotional-deflation combination."
""
"And it all started setting in about 12 hours ago. Which, of course, is why you slept like crap and skipped your workout. "
""
"No energy. All anxiety. "
""
"And I can’t blame you. After all …"
""
"Not only is your inbox growing, but the meeting with your boss about the overdue project is also happening in a few hours."
""
"You also have a few insurance bills you haven’t yet paid. You know, the bills left for you on the kitchen counter. Seriously, how do you not see them?"
""
"Don’t forget that your child isn’t yet registered for camp and that always fills up. She will be crushed if she can’t do that camp."
""
"And when are you going to return those calls from last week? "
""
"Your friends want to take a trip with you, but you haven’t answered them about the dates. When are you going to figure that out? "
""
"Your tax appointment is coming up. "
""
"While we’re at it, will you ever find someone to do that home repair project? It’s been a problem for six months, and it is getting worse."
""
"……STOP ……"
""
"Dude, just stop for a moment. "
""
"Just stop for yourself - for your sanity. "
""
"Just give yourself a moment here. All of that, and I mean all of it, will be worked through. And none of it is worth the cost of the damage it is doing to your life right now. "
""
"Look, I’m just a dude - one who has had thoughts like this, and one you have stumbled across, somehow. But I know this: "
""
"This is the shit that is killing you. "
"This is the shit that is killing your marriage."
"This is the shit that takes your sleep and replaces it with stress."
"This is the shit that causes you to drink alone, on your couch, on a Tuesday night when your kids are in bed and your wife is in your room."
""
"This is the shit that ages your body, steals joy from your family, and slowly, steadily, surgically, and regrettably steals the best years of your life and replaces it with all the shit you “should” do."
""
"You have one of two choices:"
" - Keep living like you are, or"
" - Decide you want to change."
""
"It won’t be easy. This shit never is."
"But according to your list above, it also isn’t any harder."
"Worst of all, onle one person can fix it: You."
""
"Maybe it’s finally time to talk to someone. "
""
"If it is, you know where to find me."
""
"I’m a dude in your corner."
""
"Lon@normal40.com"

A little shout out to the generations before us that made it possible. "
""
"It’s a grit and courage I simply can’t imagine."
""
"#normal40

Dear Territory Pioneers: "
""
"You must have left everything behind to get here - everything."
""
"Maybe you arrived with a family, or perhaps you came alone."
""
"You put it all on the line - everything - for a shot to make a mark on the world. Your shot at building a legacy for your family, using little more than your grit and courage."
""
"I don’t know what you left behind to get here, but you traded it all to start a new life, in a hole you hand-dug into the south side of a prairie hill—a hill and a hole that still exists, today."
""
"You tilled the sod by hand, ox, or horse. You endured sweltering summer days, the typical summer drought, the occasional prairie fire, and the inevitable prairie blizzard."
""
"Some of you survived, and some of you did not. Most of your family survived, but some did not. Yet, somehow, you made the trade."
""
"I am personally not familiar with that level of courage. It is both astounding and confounding, and it has my respect."
""
"Mr. & Mrs. Pioneer, I wanted you to know that in some places, albeit fewer every year, the proof of your grit and courage survives on the prairie where I grew up. And these places still bring me wonder."
""
"The tracks in this photo are yours. You put them here. And they remain as proof of what is possible when you see what’s hard, and you do it anyway."
""
"These are the tracks that you forged deep into the #southdakota prairie as you, well, pioneered the untamed and unforgiving lands."
""
"The path you wore with your horse and wagon still lives here. In the spring of the year, before the grasses equalize the prairie, the rawness of your presence can be seen and appreciated."
""
"When I see them each spring, it fills me with wonder and curiosity that never feels satisfied. For it’s your courage that I find so hard to comprehend. It is truly incredible, and it gives me wonder."
""
"I don’t know where these tracks start or where they end. I don’t know if these tracks were traveled in good times or bad, with happy thoughts or sad, or with a family or alone."
""
"But the tracks live on and give me wonder."
""
"I want you to know, several generations later, we give thanks for all you did to tame the prairie. Thank you for risking it all to make it possible for others. Thank you for taking the chance. Thank you for showing up and showing the way."
""

""
"Oh, and one more thing. Thank you for reminding me that risks are worth taking. And if you do it right, the mark you can leave will last a lifetime - or more. Thanks for doing something bigger than you. I admire your courage and your trade."

I still have hard days."
""
"I have days when I’m rocking, and days when I’m triggered into rethinking elements of my world."
""
"These days are not “take me off mission” hard, but they still offer the deflating feeling of, “well, now what?” You know, the feeling you get when you don’t get the break you wanted, and it feels like starting over—that one."
""
"This month, I set a goal to take Normal 40 to the stage. "
" - But I hit a snag."
""
"I applied to do a TED talk. My home town was taking applications, and I was confident that my message, research, and the real-world content from 200 rambles from C-suite leaders worldwide would be worthy."
""
"I was convinced that sharing what I learned from other leaders - on the top of their game - and that we all have one thing in common - would be enough."
""
"I thought that offering a voice to what others wouldn’t, giving a face and feelings to what others couldn’t, and giving hope to those who feel trapped - but shouldn’t … I thought that would be enough."
""
"It wasn’t."
""
"As it turns out, I still have hard days. But what brings forward has evolved."
""
"This hard day wasn’t about me. It is not about my image, ego, or position in the corporate pecking order. It’s only me here."
""
"This hard day wasn’t about me. This hard day was about you - the one who is here but feels invisible."
""
"This hard day was not about me; it was about you. The dude who shows up here wondering if you’re alone. The one who feels the weight of the world, feels like he’s slipping downward - for the first time ever — but has nobody to turn to. The person who misses his family and feels guilty that he can’t simply feel joy for all he has in life. The dude who is finally doing the math on how much his success has cost him—the person who doesn’t yet know that all of this is .. normal."
""
"It was a hard day for you, too."
""
"I still have hard days, But they’re no longer dictated to me by a boss, an assignment, a colleague, a team member, or a quarter-end."
""
"My bad days happen when I see a chance to reach the next person who needs to feel change is possible, but I’m unable to reach them."
""
"I still have hard days. But today, I will give myself permission to move to Plan B. And I will also give myself permission not to have a Plan B, yet."
""
"I love TED. It’s going to be a fantastic event; it always is. "
""
"I’m sure an application from a middle-aged former executive with one-one research on other middle-aged leaders who are quietly miserable, discontent, guilt-ridden and alone — and that we all have one thing in common — doesn’t sound like a “big idea.”"
""
"And before I lived it, I didn’t think it was either. But, it is."
""
"Truthfully, I will be bummed to be on the back side of the lights. But I am going to take this to the stage. This is a big idea, and it deserves a stage. Yours."
""
"When I find a stage, I would be honored if you would stand there with me. I’ll need you."
""
"Until then, I’ll see you on here."
""
"It’s my ball."
"I’ll shoot."
""
"Lon@normal40.com"

Sometimes, we just need a moment to ourselves. "
""
"Not a moment to be a boss, an executive, an associate or a position player. Not a moment to be a yard worker, a bill payer, a vacuum operator, a garage cleaner, a dishwasher, or a dinner maker."
""
"Not a moment to be who others need you to be. A moment to be who you need you to be. "
""
"Sometimes, you just need a moment to yourself."
""
"Usually, when this moment finds you, you don’t take it. "
""
"Even when you want it, and especially when you need it, you avoid it. Your mind is too busy reviewing all of its “should dos.”"
""
"But …"
""
"Sometimes, you just need a moment to yourself."
""
"Taking a moment doesn’t require you to take it away from someone or something else. But you have to accept, finally, that you can no longer take it away from yourself; that’s what got you here."
""
"Sometimes, you just need to take a moment for yourself. "
""
"Everyone wants more freedom in their life and more control over their time. I know because I’ve interviewed hundreds of people - just like you. "
""
"Yet at this moment, right now, you have the ability to make the time, and you have the authority to take the time. "
""
"You have the means, the house, the car, the family, the job, and if you’re fortunate, your health.  "
""
"You have the freedom, and you have the control, but you give too much of it to the ‘shoulds.’"
""
"I know why you’re here: You are searching. That’s the only reason you are here. "
""
"But you’re not searching for me; you’re searching for you. "
""
"You’re not here hoping to get something out of me; you’re here hoping I get something out of you."
""
"Here: Sometimes, you just need to take a moment for yourself. "
""
"This moment, for a few minutes this morning is one of those moments for me. I need it, I will take it, and my shoulds will wait."
""
"The list of things you could do right now is vast. And the list of things you should do is endless. "
""
"But the short list of things you complete will be all that matters."
""
"Before your ‘shoulds’ run off with you, take this time for yourself."
""
"Exercise the control and freedom you have. "
""
"My drill? "
" - Find a place - your place"
" - Get warm and comfortable"
" - A big-ass breath in and out"
" - Give thanks - from the heart - to five things in your life"
"	- You already have three: your place, your warmth, your life"
" - Be aware of what/who is getting your thanks … "
"	- And know that somehow they will benefit from this moment"
""
"Sometimes, you just need to take a moment for yourself."
""
"Sometimes, others benefit from that moment. "
""
"Sometimes, this moment gets to be that moment."
""
"If this is one of those moments for you, give thanks for the freedom and the control you have to take it. "
""
"You will never be sorry you did."
""
"Lon@normal40.com"

What would you say here if you knew there was no downside? "
""
"Not a repost from your company."
"Not a share from an interesting article in your field of work."
"Not a share of a quote in oversized font on branded stationery."
""
"What would you post here if you knew there was no downside? "
""
"Who would you hope to help, and what would you want them to learn - from you?"
""
"What wisdom do you have, what experience have you endured, what situation have you survived, or what life have you impacted, and how would you finally use it for good?"
""
"What would you say here if there were no downside? "
""
"What would it feel like to be done rehearsing what you would love to say if it was safe, and finally say it?"
""
"What would it feel like to have an identity that isn’t filtered through the permission of a company, a boss, a compliance officer, or fear of who might be watching?"
""
"For a decade, I posted here as a company executive. "
""
"I shared the company posts and posted about job openings. I shared all of the great stuff we do in our communities. I shared the news stories of the products that made an impact. And these were fantastic things to be a part of."
""
"But these were not the things that made me, me."
""
"I remember wondering what it would feel like to share on the outside all that I had learned, lived, and experienced — but had locked it up on the inside. And I remember rehearsing what someone should finally say."
""
"I felt so …. reduced."
""
"Reduced to who I had become and where I had landed. Reduced to only being who it is someone else needed me to be. Reduced to sharing what my image had created."
""
"Slowly, over time, that reduction started to smolder. And the longer I tolerated it, the more it would smolder insider."
""
"Then, I took it back. And it was hard - really hard. But I discovered something I would never have believed - and something you won’t believe, either."
""
"There is no downside."
""
"(Well, with one caveat: Don’t be an idiot. If you choose to go Yosimitee Sam, there is a downside. And the downside will be you. So, dude, don’t be an idiot.)"
""
"Truly, there is no downside. I discovered a community of unique, successful, intelligent, and decent people - who needed what I had."
""
"I discovered the most challenging things I’ve endured on my path could be a Lantern for others who are right behind me on theirs."
""
"I discovered that when I could help them, encourage them, guide them, and support them, I felt … alive again."
""
"When I showed up as me, I found more of me. And I did it by seeing more of you. And, yes, I’m talking to you."
""
"So, dude, what would you share here if there was no downside?"
""
"And when do you plan to finally do something about it?"
""
"I’ve got $10 that says you’ll be glad you did."
""
"Maybe it’s time you become the Lantern."
""
"There are people right behind you."
""
"And they would love some light."
""
"Go ahead, start being you."
""
"This is your Normal 40."
""
"And this is The Trade."
""
"This is your time."
""
"Own it."
""

"Mike Cassidy was the Google executive leading Loon. He is featured in this story. Mike was/is unbelievably (like, off the charts) intelligent, bold, and decent. I had never worked around someone who got more done and never looked stressed."
""
"You can look up “Project Loon” if you are interested, but make no mistake, the Loon and Aerostar team are total badasses regarding technology in the stratosphere."
""

"We talked a lot about balloons and the 50 years of advancements Google’s Project Loon made in 6 years. "

Many have asked about the chapter, the late adder, and the story that needed to be told. While I won't give it away, I will share a few lines from the first page of that chapter."

" The rest of the story, I can assure you, is worth the read."

" - Start quote - "
""
"Chapter 4: Hold on to me."
""
""..... I know, because I’ve been there – with an experience that shook me to the core and took me to my knees. An experience I didn’t expect to share outside of the walls of my house, let alone the pages of this book."
""
"But if my mission is to inspire change that inspires action against life’s regret, this is a story I can’t hold to myself. And I’m here to tell you, if it can happen in my house, maybe, just maybe, it’s happening in yours, too. And dude, always show up for the maybe.  "
""
"My daughter Grace made me a dad. She and I have always had what I thought was a great relationship – no, a perfect one. "
""
"My only daughter, my first child, and the one who first showed me the inexplicable love a parent has for a child. When she was born, my wife, knowing I was not used to holding or being around infants, asked me to hold her first. "
""
"Through my tears, I sat and cradled her, gazed at her, marveled at her, and then mustered the ability to speak the only words I could find: “You are three minutes old, and I’m already proud of you.” I was now a dad, and this was now my girl.""
""
" - End quote -"
""
"Some trades are greater than others. This is the story of #thetrade that can haunt you forever, and sometimes you're too busy to see it."
""
"Show up for the maybe. "
