I found the note I penned to my kids on the morning I resigned. "
""
"In hindsight, it was a note for me as much as it was for them. And today, as I write my book, ironically titled “The Trade,” I needed it."
""
"Dear Grace, Dawson, and Oliver,"
""
"For everything in your life, there is a first, and there is a last."
""
"Your life will be full of first days. These bring you experiences to be excited about; something to chase, and something that lies ahead."
""
"But in your life, because math being what it is, there will also be an equal number of last days."
""
"Your first and last day of work, your first and last day of school, of a relationship, of days with friends, of time in our house, and your time with your mom and me."
""
"A first and a last. Every start eventually ends."
""
"That is how life works, and it doesn’t discriminate. It’s a rhythm of life you must learn to dance with, or it will dance over you."
""
"Kids, in your life, live for the last days, not the first."
""
"A first day is about first impressions and fulfilling the aspirations of the person you may someday be."
""
"But your last day reveals the results; did you or did you not?"
""
"For in your life kiddo, it will be the last day that holds the score."
""
"Play for your last day."
""
"You see, today is my last day. "
""
"And I choose to celebrate this day."
""
"I choose to walk the halls slowly, to give my thanks, and to be romantically nostalgic about how every day before this day has shaped me."
""
"I choose to celebrate what these halls took from me, and give thanks for what I took from them."
""
"For 14 years, I’ve been building for this day."
""
"For 14 years, this day rested on a distant horizon."
""
"But on this day, we will share a sunset."
""
"Kids, on this day, I choose to give heartfelt thanks for the friendships and the lessons I learned on a journey—time spent with some of the world’s best."
""
"On this day, I give thanks to those who took a risk on me and celebrate those who stay to carry the torch."
""
"On this day, I choose to hear the rhythm, and I choose to dance to it. "
""
"In your lifetime, there will be as many last days as there are first days."
""
"On your last day, leave the company/friendship/relationship stronger, smarter, and better than you found it."
""
"When you do, it will do the same for you. And those are all things you can take with you, wherever your path leads you."
""
"And if you take it, you will have it."
""
"Today is my last day."
""
"I find myself nostalgic that my work here is done, but also proud of my impact. I’ve completed what it is I came here to do, so I dance."
""
"This is the finish for which I am prepared. And it’s a finish for which I am proud."
""
"Tomorrow I will wake up to a first day."
""
"A day that comes before all of the rest. And I am ready. "
""
"This is my trade."
""
"I will own it and chase it, and I will bet on me."
""
"Whenever in life you can, so should you."
""
"See you on the other side of today, kiddo."
""
"Cheers to the distant sunset."
""
"And cheers to all of the firsts in front of us."
""
"With love,"
""
"Dad -"
""

This week I took my son to Disney World. We were in Florida doing University visits, so we thought we would crush a day a Disney. "
""
"Fourteen years ago, my wife and I brought our kids here for the first time. We scraped together all of the money we had (literally). We used airline miles to get here, and bummed a room credit from Mindi’s dad to make it happen. We made a lifetime of memories. It was an outweighed few days that I’ll never regret."
""
"Fourteen years ago, college visits were the furthest things from our thoughts. It simply didn’t exist in our psyche. College visits were something that old people did. "
""
"This week, as we moved through the parks, I pointed out where fourteen years ago, we stopped to eat the sandwiches we brought to the park - the exact spots. I pointed out where we watched the parade, met the characters, where we purchased ice cream and where they spilled it, and where each of the kids had their inevitable meltdowns."
""
"My son pointed out how much I liked to complain about the lines, the prices, and the incredible attention to detail Disney puts into all of their experiences. Hey, I am a dad. "
""
"We also talked about the characters he loved when he was four. We talked about the shows he insisted we see, the rides he insisted we ride, and the trinkets he insisted we buy."
""
"The day reminded me what it was like to be a dad to a four-year-old and how those days never come back, and it reminded me that they don’t necessarily have to go away, either. But it reminded me how fast time moves if we don’t pay attention…or how fast it moves even when we do."
""
"For fourteen hours as we glided through three parks. And for all fourteen hours, fourteen years ago felt like fourteen weeks ago."
""
"I’m pretty sure I showed up here this week with the little guy on the left: A little dude who loved to ride on my shoulders, poke fun at me, and always had a story for anyone willing to listen. A few hours later, I left with the dude on the right. A dude who drove me to the park, a kid who still makes me laugh, still loves to ride, still loves a good story, and, thankfully, still like to hang out with his dad."
""
"This week has been a reminder that not all days are created equal; some are outweighed to others."
""
"Some days are outweighed by memory, love, joy, and decency."
""
"This week I was reminded that on these days - the outweighed days - you should show up differently, better."
""
"Because these are the days that will have an outweighed impact on how you will feel for the rest of your life."
""
"Fourteen years from now … I can’t wait to see what my trip to Disney will look like. But for now, I simply hope that I’m fortunate enough to find out."
""
"Today, my son will head home, and I will do what I’m called to do next: write. I’m going to sequester and finish my book: “The Trade.”"
""
"This will be another set of outweighed days. And I’m going to show up for them."
""
"In the meantime, I’ll take all the well wishes you can muster."
""

"“Seven years ago.”"
""
"“You’ve been working for the same company for twenty-eight years, and you have known for the last seven that you had a dream to chase, but you put your dreams on hold to avoid appearing disloyal?”"
""
"“Yes.”"
""
"“I admire loyalty, but it goes more than one direction. You can’t give all your loyalty to one thing. Over time, that type of loyalty becomes a thief. In this case, a thief of at least five years.”"
""
"“What do you mean?”"
""
"“Well, you have a dream — One begging you to take a chance on it. It keeps you up, excites you, and you know it is calling you to chase it. If all of your loyalty is reserved for the judgment of your employer, when does loyalty give your dream a shot? When does your loyalty show up for you? When does your loyalty finally show up better for your wife and grown kids? It won’t. You have to take it back and share it with what your future is begging from you.”"
""
"Long pause."
""
"“I had never thought about that. My loyalty cost me five years - five years when I knew I wanted to be somewhere else.” "
""
"“Dude, loyalty is admirable, but it is a balance. You are out of balance, and your gut knows it; that’s the gnawing you feel in your gut on Sunday afternoons. Now it’s time to be loyal to you. It’s time to be selfish—for you, your family, and your dreams. It’s time to do things—for you. It’s time to be loyal to the dreams patiently waiting while you put everything else first. It’s time to bet on you, and you are ready the moment you say you are.”"
""
"Long pause."
""
"“Dude, what are you feeling?”"
""
"Long pause."
""
"“I feel …”"
""
"“I feel …. I feel like this is my time.”"
"“I feel …. light. Like a huge weight just lifted.”"
"“I feel …. like this is possible, and this might be The Trade."
""
"“We have some work to do, boss. Next step for you, give yourself The Permission to feel good about taking a bet on you, and to accept that The Trade is the best loyalty of all; loyalty to yourself. Next week, let's start giving our loyalty to a dream; it’s time.”"
"______________________________________________"
""
"There will be a time in your life when the best thing you can do is become selfish. "
""
"There will be a point when you quit chasing everything that comes with a price and becomes more loyal to the things in your life that are truly priceless."
""
"When that time comes, it shows up as a pit in your gut, and it shows up on Sunday afternoon and leaves around noon on Friday."
""
"When it finds you, be selfish. Be really damn selfish."
""
"It’s your ball, boss."
"Take the shot."
""

This is my dad. He is 83 years old. For 77 years, he has lived within a few hundred feet of where he and my mom live today."
""
"Our farm is a place I write about. It’s a place you’ve seen pictures of, and it’s the place I still call home, even though I have not lived there for almost 30 years."
""
"I grew up here. I went to school, learned to ride a bike, ride a horse and drive here. I learned to fish and work, and I learned to love here. It shaped me in every possible way a person can be shaped."
""
"Growing up, I spent thousands of hours in the tractor’s buddy seat next to my dad. We tilled the soil, planted the crops, and then cultivated them."
""
"In most years, we also get to harvest them."
""
"I saw the world from that buddy seat — when the world was a small township in rural South Dakota. I asked my dad what this lever did and what that button was for. I asked about the gages, and I dreamed about one day doing what dad was doing."
""
"But as we rode on, barely a day went by when I didn’t knock my head against the window as I was dozing off, as Paul Harvey’s voice found a new gear in the old AM radio."
""
"This past fall, while I was harvesting with a neighbor, I asked my dad two questions: If he thought he could still climb into a monstrous Quad-track tractor. And if so, I asked if he would ride a few rounds with me."
""
"He said yes, and yes."
""
"My dad hadn’t been in a new piece of equipment in probably more than a decade. And it was a different color than what he knew. And at 83, I feared it would be hard for him to climb in."
""
"Instead, it was the best I’d seen him move in years."
""
"He climbed in, sat down, and harvest was on. But this time, forty years later, he was the one in the buddy seat."
""
"We talked for hours about how farming used to be, how it is now, how it’s changed, and who will be left to do it decades from now."
""
"Then he asked what this lever did and what that button was for. And he marveled at the screens, cameras, autopilot, and sheer power of the machine."
""
"And at least once, dad dozed off and bounced his head off the window next to him."
""
"Of all my hours in the past year, this is among the best. They are among the best hours of a lifetime, too. And all I had to do was make time before it was too late—and ask."
""
"If you ever wonder about The Trade, come back to this post. This moment, these hours, this conversation, this time on the farm with my dad—there’s no price tag I can place on that."
""
"These are the things money can’t buy. And these are the opportunities that go away before you’re ready. "
""
"This ride with my dad, I will never have to wish I had done it. I’ll never regret not taking the time to do it. I can only wish I had done it sooner."
""
"When you’re done writing the book of your life, it will end with one of two lines:"
""
"“I wish I would have.” OR"
"“I’m glad I did.”"
""
"You’re writing those lines today."
"Choose your lines wisely."
""
"#normal40"
"#thetrade"

"#onethousanddudes"

"PS - My dad is still in great health. I hope to do The Ride all over again this year.

CONTENT WARNING: The term “burnout” annoys me."
"We give it too much power, and too little definition."
""
"We use it to explain everything relating to underperformance."
""
"Think of the last time you heard it, used it, or felt it:"
"- “He was better before he burned out.”"
"- “You should take a vacation, so you don’t burn out.”"
"- “I don’t know what’s wrong with me; maybe I’m burned out.”"
""
"Burnout is always used as a diagnosis. It’s the disease that’s holding someone back."
""
"But burnout isn’t the disease. Burnout isn’t causing the feeling that gives you a pit in your stomach."
""
"Burnout is not stealing your energy, hijacking your sleep, sharpening your temper, or stealing your joy."
""
"Burnout is not the disease."
""
"Burnout is the side effect."
""
"You don’t suffer from burnout; you feel it."
""
"Dude, feeling burned out is real. The loss of energy, lack of focus, dreaded weekly updates on projects you can’t focus on, wishing for Friday on Wednesday morning, and the Sunday pit. This is what burnout feels like, but burnout didn’t cause it."
""
"BUT - "
"Feeling burnout does not come from the number of hours you put into work."
""
"Burnout comes from the amount of work you put into things that aren’t part of your future."
""
"Burnout finds you when the joy of your job has sifted down to the need for a paycheck."
""
"You can’t vacation, rest, work, think or spend your way out of it."
""
"You have to act your way out of it."
""
"Not annoyed yet? Hold on — I’ll try harder."
""
"The hardest thing you will do in your lifetime is come to know who you are, define what it is you want, and make the trade to get it."
""
"You will have all of the reasons why that’s for other people, not you."
""
"Your reasons will come down to being the provider, paying for houses, cars, and college. It will come down to bills and vacations. It will come down to upcoming bonuses and options vesting."
""
"It will come down to all of the things your money is asking of you. "
""
"And then you will feel burnout all over again."
""
"Dude, look. Burnout is real. And it sucks. But it is not the disease - it’s the side effect."
""
"The disease is knowing you should make a change, knowing that it’s not going to get better, knowing you’re off mission, knowing your best days are in front of you, and knowing the clock is ticking for you to do something about it."
""
"The disease is knowing you need change, but fearing what it will mean for how you live."
""
"The disease isn’t burnout. The disease is tolerance."
"And that disease is a bitch to fight."
""
"When you finally get on mission, when you finally do the work of knowing who it is you’re here to be and then muster the courage to stand in it, burnout doesn’t exist."
""
"This is where “Burn-in” lives."
""
"During burn-in, you can’t wait to put in the work. You can’t wait to take the chance. You can’t wait to take the call, write the paper and push an idea."
""
"Because it’s what you’re here to do."
""
"Time to burn in? "
""
"Join my closed & private LinkedIn community; see comments."
""
"Lon@normal40.com"
""
"#normal40"

Today marks one year."
""
"One year since leaving my job as a public company executive."
""
"One year since packing my office, giving the hugs, handing over the files, and passing through the doors of a previous chapter in my life."
""
"One year since trading the security of W-2 income, the big title, the big office and desk, for the uncertainty of…life."
""
"One year since being home all day, writing on LinkedIn, leading with curiosity, exploring, sharing, and, in fact, dreaming with you."
""
"I remember my last day like it was yesterday."
""
"It was the last page of my best chapter, so far. The chapter I authored — the chapter I ended before the next one was adequately titled. "
""
"As I crossed the corporate threshold for the last time, I asked myself one last question: “Where will I be in a year from now?”"
""
"Today, I can answer that:"
""
"I am here. "
""
"Right here."
""
"I help dudes put into words what they are feeling, and I give them a place to feel normal about it. On a good day, I also get to inspire them to believe that more is possible. And on the best days, I help them to name it."
""
"I am here."
""
"Right here."
""
"I give my time to hear the most amazing, inspiring, heart-wrenching, and real stories from people like you, every day. And I offer you all I can - everything I have - to help you work through it all. I hear what you are feeling, and I hear what you are dreaming."
""
"I am here."
""
"Right here."
""
"Most mornings, I put all I have into a post that maybe, just maybe, is the sign you’ve been seeking to finally ask for a call. A note that strikes just the right chord, and a punch that lands in the right place — to inspire you to act."
""
"I am here."
""
"Right here."
""
"One year ago, I laid down my title, left my office, started living off of savings, and traded who I was becoming for who I am capable of being. I traded a job for life."
""
"I still have good days and bad. I still have stress and need a release. I still wonder if this path is right, and I still look hard for the omens - the signs - that have led me here and will lead me to what’s next."
""
"One year ago, I was you."
""
"Not like you."
""
"I was you."
""
"Today, I am someone new. I’m someone I like better. Someone who doesn’t have your answers, but has your questions. I am on mission - to help you get on yours."
""
"I am a dude from the internet. The guy you don’t know how you found, but the guy you’re glad you did. My mission is to inspire change in the lives of one thousand dudes. My scorecard will be one thousand notes of thanks. Until then …"
""
"Dude, I am here."
""
"Right here."
""
"If you’re here too, on this day, it would be good to finally meet you. In whatever way you are comfortable, leave your mark. Give a like, leave a comment, share with a friend, DM, or send me an email. Lon@normal40.com."
""
"I am here. And I still wonder where I will be in a year."
""
"Just like you."
""
"But dude, one year later, I have a name for my next chapter:"
"“The Trade”"
""
"I am so glad I did."
""
"You will be too."
""
"I am here,"
"You will be, too."
""
"#normal40"
"#thetrade"

"#onethousanddudes

To chase or to be chased. One is happening now. "
""
"Give some thought to the times in your life when you felt your best, when you felt alive, in control, full of energy, and excited."
""
"Give some thought to a time when you were brimming with confidence, flowing with hope, and excited about what might happen if you got it right."
""
"Give some thought to when you cared more about where the journey might take you and who you might meet than what it might buy you."
""
"Give some thought to when you knew you might not get it right, but you woke up every morning excited to see where the day might take you - and you felt in control of it all."
""
"Give some thought to the time when you put it on the line to chase something larger, and the last time you took the shot when it was yours to take."
""
"Give some thought to who you were with, what you were doing, who you were helping, and how it eventually changed your life."
""
"Your life has two options: To chase or be chased."
""
"When you quit chasing, your preservation starts chasing you."
""
"When your reason to stay becomes to protect what you already have, you are being chased — but you don’t know it.  "
""
"Are you chasing your next chapter, or protecting your last?"
""
"Dude, you are a chaser. When you’re ready, I’ll be here. "
""
"What do you want, and what are you willing to trade to get it?"
""
"There’s nothing more powerful than a dude who has discovered who he is here to be, and has reconciled what he is willing to trade to live it."
""
"But declaring it is on you. Defining it is on you. Starting it is on you. Doing something about it is on you. "
""
"The people who do it — These are the people I admire most. "
""
"To chase to be chased."
"This is on you, boss."
""

"One of my favorite questions is lucky number thirteen:"

"Later today, you’re going to feel a pit in your stomach, just like you do every Sunday. "
""
"At that moment, put a name on the pit. Narrow it down to the one thing giving you that anxiety (dude, yes, that is anxiety)."
""
"In most cases, the Sunday Pit, the one you feel the moment your mind starts to think about Monday morning, is there as the result of a single awkward conversation that you’re avoiding with someone in your life. Someone you’re giving more power to than they deserve to have. "
""
"Who is in your mind right now? "
"Who is that conversation with? "
"What needs to be addressed?"

"When it’s fixed, how will that make you feel?"

"That conversation is wrecking your Sunday. "

I’ve been serving up a daily dose of reality in the hopes that it: 1) Educates you on what you feel, 2) Relates to you by creating copy that mysteriously nestles into where you are, and 3) Inspires you to see yourself through the lens you used to see yourself."
""
"This past week, Normal 40 The Podcast dropped an episode on the one thing we all have in common: The Financials."
""
"The traffic for this episode has been, well, a hockey stick."
""
"After thousands of Normal 40 conversations, I’ve learned there is a cost for everything—a cost for everything you’ve ever had, a cost for everything you have, and a cost for every new chapter you have to live for."
""
"Everything has a cost, even The Financials."
""
"The Financials eventually become the reason most people never try. There’s a number in cell Q37 on a spreadsheet saved to your desktop that holds all of the power. The cell keeps you exactly where you are, on the road to nowhere, and it can convince you that it is correct and safe."
""
"EDUCATE:"
"Being unfulfilled has a cost, too."
"It shows up later in life as regret."
"It will also show up later today in mood swings, the desire to be alone, shortness with your wife and kids, anxiety, a pit in your stomach, and two fingers of brown liquor you consume (alone) on your couch while the rest of your house sleeps."
""
"RELATE:"
"I’ve seen the cost in the dashed hopes of wildly-successful people worldwide, and I’ve lived it."
""
"I’ve heard it in their stories, I’ve seen it in their faces, and I’ve felt it in their tears."
""
"Yes, sometimes dudes cry. And sometimes they need to."
""
"Give The Financials some power, but don’t allow a spreadsheet to be the one thing you trust to live the Next 40 years of your life."
""
"There’s one more thing I’ve learned about The Financials:"
""
"INSPIRE:"
"You will care most about your compensation when that’s the only thing left keeping you where you are."
""
"When money is all that remains, and the rest of you has been sanded down to the wrinkled edges of someone you barely recognize, you will cling to it, believing that’s all there is."
""
"The financials matter. They should have a vote, but they shouldn’t dictate."
""
"So, what’s next?"
"You can join my free closed group on LinkedIn (see comments). You can schedule a free call or hit my website for a few free tools. You can download the 31 questions that changed my life … also free."
""
"BUT-"
""
"If you want to help reach the next dude who needs it, please share, like, or comment on this post. Email or DM a friend. Share a podcast. Leave a 5-star review or share a story that inspired you. Do something that helps make this a home."
""
"Be that dude."
""
"To make a community, I need people brave enough to claim it. So, I’m asking you to claim it. I’m asking you to be a Lantern, just like the one who brought you here."
""
"You made it to Friday."
""
"Go be who you are capable of being today."
"Nobody will show up to do that for you."
"And nobody needs to."
""
"From your corner,"
""
"Lon@normal40.com"
""

"#thetrade"

I talk to the people who show up here every day. I carve out time daily to ramble with people who simply ask to speak. It is, without question, one of the best parts of my new life. "
""
"Those conversations, hundreds of them, with dudes just like you, have taught me a lot … about dudes like you. "
""
"I’ve learned there are a million paths in a lifetime. Most look similar for 18 years, and none look the same after that."
""
"Ironically, no matter the path, they always meet again at this place we call Normal 40. Everyone arrives here with their own book of barroom and bedroom stories, but everyone arrives here with the same frustrating feeling. And nobody talks about it, except this dude from the internet. "
""
"A trauma surgeon and a manufacturing plant manager. "

Eventually, we all become the average of the five people we spend the most time with. We know that. But, for me … "
""
"The core five relationships in my life fill one of three roles:"
""
"1.) The Advisor. These tend to be friends who are further down a path I want to travel. They are wise and giving. They usually communicate in terms like: “Why don’t you…” "
""
"2.) The Connector: These are people who know their greatest gift is to connect people and situations to other people and situations. They remove themself and connect people with what’s next. They see the world in 1 + 1 = 3, and they see themself as the plus sign. They usually communicate in terms like: “Here’s how others do it….”"
""
"3.) The Encourager: These are rare people. They are friends who won’t let you dwell on the past; they won’t allow you to give in to a perceived weakness or settle for an outcome that’s too short. These people call you out, scare you, annoy you and frustrate you. These are also the people who also inspire you, push you, and excite your mind into seeing something new. These people make you see and feel what might be possible. They usually communicate in terms like: “What if we did…”"
""
"I don’t have to wonder who I am for my friends. I’m a dreamer, and I’m an encourager. I’m a pusher, and I’m an annoyer. And I’m a motivator and confidence builder. I refuse to let something go unchased because I refuse to let regret have a home."
""
"Dude, I’m good with that."
"		"
"Know who is in your core five. And if you don’t have an encourager, do whatever you can to have one in your corner. These are the people who see who you are capable of being —And they will see it before you do. "
""
"Finding that person will, and I promise, change who you become."
""
"This is what I do … It’s what I’ve always done. It’s what I will keep doing. It’s what I will someday do for you, even if we never talk."
""
"Lon@normal40.com finds me. OR … "

"On the flight to Hawaii, I was reminded of this:  You can’t find evidence of success before you muster the courage to finally start."

""
"It didn’t feel to them like a bigger risk or a smaller risk than it does to you, but they started. "
""
"They thought about waiting. "
"They wondered about failure. "
"They thought about what their friends might think if it didn’t go according to plan."
""
"But they started anyway.  And they didn’t start. Were you see them now. They started where you are now. "
""
"Normal 40 is my journey. I show up here and I share it with you. Some of you like it, and some of you don’t. I don’t have any guarantees that Normal 40 will be a success. It won’t be easier for me or harder for me than it will be for you. "
""
"The only difference between you and I is that… I started."
""
"Dude, you don’t have to quit anything to finally start. But you do have to start to ever finally quit. "
""
"Normal 40 is just getting started. You’re going to see changes this year. You’re going to see programs that I lead. And you’re going to see products that I’ve created. I know I will get some of them wrong, but I know that’s part of the trade. "
""
"And remember this, the Normal 40 is not about the years you have behind you. The normal 40 is about the 40 years you have in front of you. It is up to you how you choose to live them."

"I hope you continue to follow me on my journey. After all, you are the reason why I am here."

The lesson I took from M&A that changed my life:"
""
"It takes the same amount of work to chase, court, negotiate and achieve a small deal as it takes to live out a big deal."
""
"No matter deal’s size, you go through the same sequence. You ask the same questions, you fill out the same reports, you give the same briefs, you wrestle the same dragons, you work the same strategic integration plan, you have the same self-doubt, and you use the same math."
""
"Then, at the one-year mark, you answer the same questions:"
"  1.) Are we better as a result of this deal?"
"  2.) Did we think big enough when we chased the deal?"
"  3.) What should this deal look like in another year?"
""
"NOW re-read and replace the word “deal” with “dream.”"
"______________________________________________"
""
"Dude, dream bigger."
""
"Dream bigger than who it is you already are, and bigger than what you might someday do if you win the lottery."
""
"Surround yourself with those who dream big and act. In hours you will start to dream big and act."
""

Transcript from a Normal 40 Ramble."
""
"Dude: But what if I fail, Lon? What if I try for a year, or maybe two, and I fail? I don’t want to stay where I am for five more years. I want to quit feeling this weekly grind pushing me through life, and I want to be done waking up with a pit in my stomach. I want control over my time and to live “on mission,” to use your word, but what if I fail?"
""
"Me: Okay, let’s go there. You tell me, what if you do fail? What does failure look like to you? Not some BS, exaggerated failure your brain makes up. What’s the actual worst case?"
""
"Dude: It means my savings are a little depleted, and we might have to downsize our lifestyle … Then I could get a job, but it might not pay like the one I have today. It would be like I’m starting all over. I need a Plan B if this doesn’t work."
""
"Me: In the last thirty minutes, you have asked me four times: “What if I try and fail?” This can’t be your only question. That’s like a boxer saying: “But what if I get hit in the face?” If that’s all he’s thinking about, he will never step in the ring, and he will always wonder if he should have, and wish he would have."
""
"Me: I want you to answer a different question: “What if you stay where you are and you never find out?” What if you stay two, five, or ten more years, and you never test your dreams? You need to fast forward ten years: “Will you regret it if you don’t?”"
""
"Dude: (Insert F-bomb here)!  What if I never try? What if I never find out? What if I never get in the ring? What if I never take a punch and know what it’s like to give one back? Dude, unbelievable. Of course, I knew this, but hearing it from you was somehow the punch I needed."
""
"Me: Well, this is what I do. We all think about our “Plan B” from the place we sit today. But when you invest in your dream for a year and chase something new, something that fills you up rather than brings you down, you won’t be creating a Plan B from where you are today, you will be creating a Plan B from you who you are tomorrow. And you will be someone totally different. Someone with a completely different perspective. Someone with entirely new friendships. Someone with a completely new outlook on life. And someone who is completely clear on who they want to be next. "
""
"If you bet on yourself, there is no downside. It never leads to regret because of who you become along the way. But you have to be up for the journey, you have to start small, and you should have a guide to help fight the dragons. Becuase one of them will punch you in the face. But in this fight, you get to punch them back."
""
"END OF TRANSCRIPT"
""
"Normal 40 is an insurance policy against future regret. That’s what I do. Don’t turn ten years older and wish you would have tried. "
""
"I offer free calls (just like this one) and just posted a free mini-book: “The 14 questions that saved my life.” All available on my website. Dude, just go check it out. "
""
"It’s you ball, boss."

A few 2022 lessons from my chair."
""
"1.) Trading the life you have, to chase the life you want, will be the hardest decision of a lifetime."
""
"It’s hard because it is a choice; yours. "
"It’s hard because others have done it while you have not."
"It feels like you have more to lose. But that’s only true when scoring material things. If you want more material things, stay. When you want more of what money can’t buy, plan."
""
"2.) Spending 10% of your retirement today, to create a life you want to live forever, is the best investment you’ll ever make."
""
"If you do it right, you’ll add 15-20 years onto the years of life you WANT to work. "
""
"3.) The dream is a blessing when chased, but becomes a curse when ignored. "
""
"The greatest lifetime regret is never testing who you were capable of being. Failure you can handle. Regret will kill you."
""
"One day you will have to answer: “Did I try?”"
""
"4.) Don’t wait to start until you have the answers."
""
"If you’re waiting for it to be failproof, be prepared to wait, just like you waited in 2021 & 2022. Starting is not trading, it’s only exploring. Quit overthinking it."
""
"5.) How you feel when “On mission” is hard to describe."
""
"Being “on mission” is a perpetual mental state where you can’t wait to perform - from your core gifts. It’s a place where burnout is replaced by burn-in. Where the hours you work fill you, not drain you. It is where you feel significance, without guilt, while being who you are, as you are, daily."
""
"6.) You don’t need to quit anything to finally start something new. But you do need to start something new to ever quit anything old."
""
"The key: Fall in love with what it is you want to start - and who it is you want to become along the way. When the love of starting overtakes the fear of leaving, you have all the fuel you need, and all you will be given."
""
"7.) When preparing to climb a mountain, you don’t start training by conditioning for the final mile. You start by buying a pair of socks and walking around the block."
""
"Starting isn’t trading. It’s training—Quit overthinking it. Just act."
""
"8.) It starts the moment you decide it will. No sooner. No later."
""
"You can’t see it now, but you have options—hundreds. And none of them need to include resigning tomorrow."
""
"9.) Never start with “how.”"
"You have become all that you are by answering “how.” "
"You will become who you’re capable of being by first discovering “what” it is you’re here to do and “who” it is you’re here to be. "
""
"Armed with that, “how” you get there becomes instinct."
""
"10.) You’re ready. You wouldn’t be reading this line if something wasn’t tugging you through each word. You. Are. Ready."
""
"My mission is to inspire change in the life of a thousand men at this crossing. My mission is to be a Lantern for what is possible, and a Guard against future regret. I do that here."
""
"My mission is to be a Guide to help navigate the path. If this is finally your year for a Guide, quit waiting."
""
"It’s time. Yours."
""
"Lon@normal40.com"

In January 2020, I created a one-page tool that changed my life. It has probably impacted yours, too."
""
"Tools usually don’t work for me. At best, I give them a week."
""
"So it had to be simple, it had to fit into how I already operated, and it had to give me results - fast - or I wouldn’t use it. "
""
"It had to achieve these ten things in ten minutes a day:"
""
"1.) Organize my day to do the job I had better than I ever had"
"2.) Prioritize what really had to be done -  and kill what didn’t"
"3.) Identify the root of overwhelm - and name it"
"4.) Improve communication with my boss"
"5.) Improve communication with my team"
"6.) Capture the seeds of what I was feeling"
"7.) Build a life around my family, not the other way around"
"8.) Remind myself to dream - and to catch myself doing it"
"9.) Clarify: “What are the ingredients of the life I want?”"
"10.) Define what was killing me - and kill it first"
""
"It worked in 30 days. Weeks into 2020, what I was feeling, what was giving me stress and steeling sleep - it began to spill onto the pages, in my own handwriting. Then I found small pockets of time to explore what might be next. I did all of this, and nobody knew what I was feeling. "
""
"Dude, when you prepare to climb a mountain, you don’t start your training by practicing the selfie at the top. You start when you buy some socks and walk around the block. This tool is your socks. So …."
""
"While crushing it at work, I also became a certified coach, created a small business on the side, and tested the new life. I took all of my vacations, took time to go farm, to fly planes, and to be with my family. All shit that was available to me all along, but I never took the time to do it. It simply required me to see it and to make #thetrade for it."
""
"Then the energy I used to have started coming back."
""
"It didn’t happen overnight. I didn’t wake up one day and quit; I worked at it. And it started with ten minutes per day. The tool led me to Normal 40. Ironically, it has led you to Normal 40, too."
""
"At the urging of a few clients, I’m offering my one-page planner on Amazon - at my cost to get it to your door. "
""
"No over-selling here: It’s a bare-bones, only-what-matters, 10-minute-a-day planner. Just like your socks, it works."
""
"Search “Normal 40 Planner” by Lon Stroschein on Amazon."
"Or, download it for a few dollars from my normal40 website or in the “Featured” section of my LinkedIn page."
""
"Nobody shows up to do this hard work for you, boss. It will be, without question, a purchase you will never regret. "
""
"When you’re ready, I’ll be here: Lon@normal40.com "
""
"It’s your ball, boss."
"Pass or shoot."

Strange. The person in the mirror used to look younger."
""
"They used to laugh more, have more friends, be spontaneous and, best of all, make others laugh at the moment."
""
"They used to care less about how they dressed, what they drove, how they acted, and where they lived."
""
"They used to like that about themself."
""
"They used to stay up late. Talk all night. Take significant risks and tell great stories of times well lived."
""
"They used to live within their own smile, and it was natural. "
""
"They used to have friends at work, now they have work friends. "
""
"They used to have enough to live enough, now they trade their mental health and happiness to save enough to retire."
""
"The person in the mirror …."
""
"They used to spend time dreaming of the future, now they spend time thinking of their past, and give their tattered emotion to surviving the week."
""
"They used to be more excited about going somewhere new, and less excited about remaining somewhere familiar."
""
"The person who used to stare back, all those years ago, I wonder where he went. "
""
"I wonder what it was that rounded off all the will for adventure, erased his natural smile, and drained him of his ability to serve something other than his job? I wonder if he even knows that his family still clings to the shell of a spirit who used to thrive here."
""
"I wonder if he’s ever going to find his way home."
""
"To the person standing in the mirror:"
"This will be the youngest you will ever be."
"You are the readiest you’ve ever been. "
"You have more reasons than ever to do it. "
"The person you want to be is still inside, and this is your life’s halftime. "
""
"How you play it from this day forward is up to you."
""
"Live more yesterdays or chase your tomorrow."
""
"It’s your ball, boss."

"- Put family first, or the path will be lonely and without purpose."

"- Make your opportunities; solve the problems others step over."
""
"- Invest in your integrity every day."
"       - Sometimes it is all you need. Sometimes it is all you have."
""
"- In your lifetime, you will fail, but you will not be a failure."
""
"- When failure finds you, your integrity will carry you."
""
"- Use your head when you can; trust your gut when you should."
""
"- Avoiding a decision or an action - is a decision and an action."
"       - Avoiding will create the problems you deal with tomorrow."
""
"- Take the shot before you feel ready."
""
"- Thank those who took a chance on you along the way."
""
"- Eventually, you will transition into someone who gets to take a chance on others. Consider the awesome responsibility of this often. You control the mood around their dinner table."
""
"- Never underestimate the power of a hand-written letter."
""
"- Give a damn about things worth a damn."
""
"- The worst case in your mind is never to be trusted. "

""
"- Know that you are my favorite."

"- Be someone to love."
""
"- Call your mama."
""
"- Live lightly."

Every day I get asked: “What is Normal 40?”  So …"
""
"Normal 40 is not a company."
"Normal 40 is not an age, a title, or a financial class, and"
"Normal 40 does not represent the years behind you."
""
"Normal 40 is not mine, and it’s not yours."
"It’s not something you can think your way into, and"
"It’s not something you can think your way out of. "
""
"Thinking about it only brings you to the gates."
""
"Normal 40 is a feeling."
"Normal 40 is an experience - or it’s a regret."
"Normal 40 is a decision that emboldens reputation."
"Normal 40 builds a brand and life-long value; yours. "
""
"Normal 40 uncovers your moment of vulnerability and your moment of accountability. Then tests your courage to embrace it and chase it. Or to waste it."
""
"What is the Normal 40? "
"That is up to you."
""
"But how you respond to it will be the cornerstone of your legacy."
""
"Normal 40 isn’t here to taunt, guilt or pressure. Nor does it place any value on your office, car, title, frequent flier mile balance, or hotel upgrades. Here, we’re all the same."
""
"Normal 40 is an identifier and a milestone. A point in life that requires a choice. "
""
"Normal 40 is that fork in the road. The one you’ve been staring at for months or years and haven’t told anyone about. The same fork that brings you to this line - written by some dude from the internet."
""
"Normal 40 is the point in life where you choose if you are going to coast to the finish - or chase your greatest dreams."
""
"Dude - "
""
"Normal 40 is not about the years you have lived, it’s about the Normal 40 years you have left to live."
""
"Normal 40 is the punch to the gut that inspires you to act."
""
"It’s the moment you come to claim who it is you’re capable of being."
""
"Normal 40 is not free; it’s a trade."
"And it’s on you to make it."
""
"It’s your time to choose your next 40."

On this day one year ago, I was in a different place."
""
"This is the note I wrote to myself on this day in 2021:"
""
"“Trade your “So what” for your ‘So what if…’ And then own it.”"
""
"I wrote it here, on LinkedIn, but I wasn’t writing to you. I was writing to me. I was building the courage and wrestling with the decision to stay or go. It was an all-out street fight, and I was playing both sides."
""
"I was my audience. Below is the rest of my post."
"____________________________________________________"
""
"At some point in your Normal 40, you have to answer the dreaded question: So what?"
""
"-You’ve achieved the big title and the corner office. "
"	So what?"
"-You’ve made a lot of money and led a big team. "
"	So what?"
"-You’ve built a big house and have an expensive car. "
"	So what?"
"-You’ve invested well and done better than most."
"	So what?"
"-You’ve worked hard, and you deserve what you have. "
"	So what?"
""
"But at some point, we get there; we’ve achieved everything we hoped to achieve. We’ve done better than we thought we would."
""
"Then we sit in our den, alone, and ask ourselves …"
"	Dude, so what?"
""
"When that happens, it’s your second-half calling. Answer it!"
""
"What you’ve built to this point is incredible. This is your first half story. But it doesn’t define you."
""
"Your “So what if …“ will define you."
"	If you chase it."
""
"So what if - you use your skills to change or save someone’s life? "
""
"So what if - you trade your days to follow a calling you feel deep in your gut?"
""
"So what if - this post is the thing that gets you started?"
""
"So what if - you pull your kids from school for a few days and take them on an adventure they will remember forever?"
""
"So what if - you make today the day you untie yourself from your past and become the person you need to be?"
""
"So what if - you re-organize your time to create an endowment for a charity?"
""
"So what if - you start your mission to help 1000 dudes write their second half story?"
""
"So what if - you take a few days off work to write your second-half mission?  "
""
"And so what if - you put your most effective skills to work in a way that’s bigger than you could ever be alone?"
""
"So what if - today isn’t the day you change the world, but it is the day you change yours?  "
""
"So what if you started today? Would you regret it? "
"What if you don’t? Would you regret it?"
""
"Trade your “So what” for your “So what if …” "
""
"This is your normal 40, and you only get one.  So what if …….."
""
"So what if you share this post with your network because there are more people out there who need it?"
""
"Well, you might be helping me with my Normal 40: So what if I can help 1000 dudes write their second-half story? "
""
"So what if - one of them is you?"

"What is your “So what if ….”"

"“I never know until the morning. Give me a challenge.”"

"“Economics. Write about the economics of Normal 40.”"
"________________________________________________"
""
"For seven years, I lead mergers and acquisitions for a public company. I was good at it. There are paper piles of math, valuations, and legal disclosures that can bury bodies—all that while getting your first honest look at an asset that’s never as pretty as represented. Never."
""
"My gift isn’t valuation and legal. It is the understanding of the seller. More specifically, The “Endowment Effect” in every seller's mind and how to use it."
""
"The endowment effect is a human characteristic that leads owners to place greater value on the things they already own. It causes them to overvalue what they have, regardless of what any mathematical calculation or comparison would show."
""
"I know this is an emotion; others took it as a tactic. This is where “being tough” on a seller kills deals. Dude, math is a lousy tool to bring to a spar with emotion."
""
"My skill: Move the math to where the emotion is."
""
"I played into a seller’s bias; I accepted it. Then I established a mutual bias on a new asset: Time. I moved the math from the money they had - to the time they wanted. Then deals got done, and we both got what we wanted."
""
"So, what about you? That job you’re clinging onto - the one that pays well but offers a diminishing return on joy while keeping you up at night, and stealing your time from family. What about the position you chased and landed, but now has you wondering, “Is this all I’m here to be?”"
""
"Dude, maybe you’re placing too much value on what you own."
""
"Here’s how to know: Imagine you’re jobless with two options."
""
"Option 1: In three years, you’ll be back to the life you have."
"Option 2: In three years, you’ll be living the life you want."
""
"Would you trade the life you want for the life you already have? "
""
"If the answer is yes, stay. "
"If the answer is no, plan. "
""
"Maybe it’s time to move your math. "
""
"But don’t wait for the math to win you over your emotions. More dreams die at the hands of a spreadsheet than will ever be born."
""
"Maybe it’s time to move the math."
"That is what I do."
"It’s your ball."
""

Where might you be in one year from now?"
""
"It was in a small tavern in downtown Minneapolis when it happened to me. The build-up had taken years, but …"
""
"The release was instantaneous."
""
"My wife and I had been talking for months about me, us, our future, and what in life is really a risk versus an adventure. "
""
"We talked about our health, kids, and how life might look with four times more money or one-quarter of what we had saved."
""
"We realized that our lives, kids, priorities, and friendships would not know the difference of a bank balance. And we accepted that this life has no guarantees of any more yesterdays."
""
"At the time, I had a lucrative job offer in one hand, and the dream of being free enough to take back my calendar and launch my own life’s mission. The image of one was success. The image of the latter was a crazy and reckless, selfish risk-taker."
""
"We chose to accept the latter. "
""
"It was in that moment, in that tavern, with my wife - with her urging and with tears in our eyes that I finally accepted how I was feeling and what I needed to do next."
""
"Then I said out loud: “Maybe my work here is done.” "
""
"This sentence was my permission. It’s when I permitted myself to leave a wonderful part of my professional life behind and turn the page into a fresh chapter. One where I hold the pen, make the calls, control my calendar and decide what is and is not important."
""
"That moment was one year ago this week."
""
"Today, I’m releasing Episode 5 of Normal 40: The Podcast. "
""
"The episode is called: “The Acceptance.”"
""
"The episode that I never dreamed of sharing only one year ago."
""
"My mission is to inspire one thousand dudes to take back their calendars and to make #thetrade for their best half - your second half. "
""
"I tell my story for one reason: That it helps you launch yours."
""
"Share this with a friend. Leave a review wherever you listen. Don’t do it for you or me. Do it for the next person who needs it. This doesn't work without both of us."
""
"I would love to hear from anyone who wants to share some thoughts. You can find me at Lon@normal40.com."
""
"Oh, one more thing: The Acceptance - your acceptance - will not be how it ends, boss. It will be how it starts."
""
"There you are. "
""
"It’s good to have you back."
""
"See you on The Podcast."
""

""
"I don’t know who brought you here, and you have no idea what keeps calling you back. Even more, you have no idea how some dude from the internet has put into words what you’ve been feeling for a year or more."
""
"But you’ve found comfort in knowing you’re not alone."
""
"If you’re here, you’re hard-charging, lonely at the top, and it looks like you have it made, leader. You’re a dude who shows up, knows what’s expected of you, and you don’t need anyone else to tell you to do it. You’re just like the other guy reading this right now … the one who is also invisible. The one I know is here."
""
"No, I don’t know you. But I know your story and why you’re here: You’re searching. "
""
"Yes, you’re searching. The sooner you confess that, the sooner you can get back to where it is you used to be going. "
""
"You’re searching. No worries, thousands of others are, too. "
""
"The problem: You don’t know what you’re searching for. But you’ve come to think that maybe there’s more. Maybe there’s something more significant. Maybe your gifts aren’t fully used. Maybe this lifetime needs more out of you than title and pay. Maybe you were built for more and you’re under-living it. Maybe, maybe, maybe …."
""
"For the first time in your life, you are starting to wonder if the rest of your life will be lived on the path you’re on, or on a path you’ve not yet worn. "
""
"So you’re here, searching. Wondering, is this really it for me?"
""
"Dude, I don’t know you, but I know your story. "
""
"I know it’s lonely, frustrating, and confusing. You don’t want anyone to know how you feel. After all, you’ve got the life others would trade for in a heartbeat. "
""
"Yea, dude, I get it. "
""
"One more thing you need to know about your search. Something nobody else has told you, yet: "
""
"You are ready. On this day. In the moment. You are ready."
""
"Whatever your life has in store, you’re ready to search it. One more month, quarter end, or annual bonus won’t matter in your life. If you feel the call, it’s not going away. You have more to give and something is calling you out."
""
"So am I. "
""
"In the end you will answer this question: Did you try?"
""
"Dude, you hear the call, and you are ready. "
""
"Let's do the work, get the clarity and find out who you are."

""
"To get started, you need not know where your future is."

"To get started, you need only to know where it is not. "
""
"In that confession lies a beginning - yours."

The Normal 40. "
""
"This is not a reference to the years you have already lived. "
""
"This is a reference to the years you have yet to live."
""
"Normal 40 starts as a feeling where we slide away from trading our time for money into a place where we happily trade money for time. "
""
"Normal 40 is when we become more comfortable with who we truly are, and we muster the courage to lay down who it is we no longer care to be."
""
"Normal 40 is a process where we dream about what it would be like to finally be back in control of our time and in sync with our family. "
""
"Normal 40 isn’t an ending; it's a beginning. And it takes time to define is."
""
"It’s when we first ask what we want to be next and then give ourselves the permission to chase it."
""
"Normal 40 isn’t something to run from; it’s something to run towards."
""
"What you’re feeling is coming for you anyway. Maybe it’s time you be curious enough to see what it has to say."
""
"I hold time in my calendar for free calls every week. Find one on my website and book one. Thirty minutes has been enough to change the course of a life - on several occasions. "
""
"It’s your ball. "
""

Dear Self,"
""
"Give this day your thanks. Take a moment to name your blessings and recite them out loud. It is in this practice that calm will find you."
""
"But as you do …"
""
"Don’t allow yourself to be so contented with who you’ve become in the past decade that you never permit yourself to go be who it is you feel called to be for the next one."
""
"And remember …"
""
"This is the moment for which you were built. Your life to this point has given you all you need - everything - to set out toward what it is that calls your name. "
""
"And know …"
""
"The last decade was a gift, but it was never meant to be an end; it was always meant to be the means. "
""
"That moment you’ve been waiting for is upon you. The gifts you need are within you. The need you solve is around you. And I will ride beside you."
""
"And finally … "
""
"Don’t fear the fear, only the absence of it. Fear, too, is a gift. It’s a required ingredient for those blessed enough to feel awake and on mission."
""
"Look for me along the dusty path. I’ll be there. And I can’t wait."
""
"Very sincerely."
""
" - Self"

" - That moment when you knew this was the one. "
" - That moment when you were told you were the one. "

When you stare change in the face, it will stare back. "
""
"When you push yourself to make a change, it will push back."
""
"When you punch through the fear of change, it will punch back."
""
"That’s what happened to me. Yes, it sucked. Here’s what I did to get started."
""
"You are three questions away from having the courage to stare down, push back and punch through all of the fear:"
""
"1.	Who will I be if I stay on this path?"
"		- Do you like you are becoming"
"		- Do  you want to be on this path in 24 months"
"2. 	Who am I capable of being?"
"		- What are the things you used to love"
"		- Who are the people you used to admire"
"3.  What is the problem I want to help solve?"
""
"These questions get progressively more challenging. Most stop at the first question. This is the failure that allows discontent to set in. "
""
"Here’s your trade: You will do the work, or you will tolerate who you are."
""
"Moving through the process is hard; it is scary, and it feels lonely. Along the way, you will feel vulnerable, lost, and frustrated."
""
"You can keep doing what you’re doing, or you can start thinking about your plan - your story. When that time finds you, find me."
""
"My mission is to inspire one thousand dudes to make this hard trade. And it all starts with … you."
""

I write a lot. But that’s because I’m not selling anything."
""
"We all think we’re being groomed for the season of life we are living. And for most of our lives, that gets to be true. We may move from one job to another; indeed a transition, but not a new season. Just some new clothes in the same season."
""
"A new season of life - especially when it’s yours - can’t be noted on a calendar. If it were, you would avoid it. A new season finds some early, and finds others later in life. But as seasons do, they eventually move through all of us—even you. "
""
"This transition, the one you are in now, will be the single most confusing transition of your lifetime. "
""
"It’s a transition that you can’t put into words, but you feel it."
"It’s a transition you didn’t see coming, but it’s found you."
"It’s a transition you wish away because it’s stealing your drive."
""
"It’s a change that sweeps in and tamps down your enthusiasm for more money or posessions, and amps up the compassion you feel for others and your desire to finally use your gifts in a way that builds significance."
""
"It’s a change that dials down your willingness to tolerate 65-hour work weeks, and it’s a season that has a fast track to your emotions."
""
"It’s a time when choking back tears becomes harder, a time when your kids are getting older, and a time when aging parents have you thinking about mortality in a very personal sense. "
""
"It’s a hard time, and nobody talks about it."
""
"I’m a former public-company executive who has been there. I walked away from who I was becoming, to be here - to help dudes put a name to feelings and inspire them to move through it."
""
"Through hundreds of conversations, here’s the best advice I can give to anyone who isn’t sure where to start:"
""
"Accept that it’s happening. And allow yourself the grace to believe that maybe, just maybe, this is a gift being handed to you, not one being taken from you. "
""
"This isn’t the time to lock out the dreams working to the surface. This is the time to let them in and give them your curiousity."
""
"All of the great works from those you admire most, this is how it started for them, too. So …"
""
"Maybe it’s simply your turn. "
"Maybe this is your time."
"Maybe this is your call."
"Maybe this is your sign."
""
"One more thing for the three of you still reading: This transition is hard because it requires a trade."
""
"Option 1: Trade who you have become for the journey towards who you are capable of being, OR "
""
"Option 2: Trade who you are capable of being for the safety of remaining who you’ve already become."
""
"It’s not free; it is a trade. And the decision falls to you."
""
"But maybe, just maybe, it’s your turn."
""
"And maybe you’re more ready than you thought."
""
"Join me below…This is what we talk about."
""
"I’m a dude in your corner. See you when you get here."
""
"#normal40"
"#ownit #coaching #thetrade"

"Every day I make time to talk to people who have a dream to chase but they don’t know how to start. Some don’t know what the dream is, but they’re feeling an unexplainable tug in their gut to live more significantly."
""
"The greatest thing holding them back: Prudence. "
""
"They tell me that leaving what they have - to chase what they want, “Wouldn’t be a prudent move.”"
""
"So, instead, they stay one more year to build on their broken spirit, depleted energy, perpetually annoyed demeanor, and absence of the people they love."
""
"But they have their prudence - and a cool car. "
""
" - Prudent (adj): “Wise and judicious in practical affairs.”"
""
"Prudence isn’t the avoidance of risk. It’s the avoidance of regret."
""
"Here’s the sucky thing about age - you become wise. And what you want moves when you least expect it.  "
""
"With that wisdom comes a strengthening intuition and a greater mission that begs your attention. Soon, the desire for personal significance will take over your drive for financial status. "
""
"When this finds you, it is prudent to figure out how to live with it."
""
"The trade won’t be easy, but chasing it is impossible to regret."
""
"I’ve got a closed group for people like us. We talk about things like this. It’s free, private, and built for you. Joining it might prove a prudent move. "

At the top of my game, what I worried about shifted."
""
"I used to worry about:"
"- Will I get the next promotion"
"- Is the work I’m doing still good enough"
"- If I leave before 5:30, will I look lazy"
"- Can this email wait until after dinner"
"- Can I really do this for eight more years"
"- How can I save money on our family trip"
"- When will I reply to emails when on vacation"
"- Will my wife be upset if I work late"
"- Or worse, will she not even notice"
""
"Now I wonder:"
"- Who will I meet today"
"- How can I find more people who need me"
"- Will I be able to inspire one thousand dude’s journey"
"- Does my son want me to join him for lunch at school"
"- Will I be blessed enough to do this for 25 more years"
"- What’s the best way my family can build a memory"
"- Why I didn’t do this five years ago"
"- If my wife be upset if we go out tonight"

""
"Lesson: What you worry about is up to you."

If you live to age 90, you’ll live about 47 million seconds."
""
"In that time, there will be only a few split seconds that forever alter the trajectory of your life. These moments are truly one in a million, but they don’t happen accidentally. "
""
"In the 25 million seconds I’ve lived, my life has had a few perfect moments. Of them, there may be only one that was captured in a single still image."
""
"Twenty years ago this week, I proposed to my wife. It was a surprise proposal, and we were in an airplane that I was flying. "
""
"We flew over a field on our family farm where I had plowed into the ground: MINDI WILL U MARRY ME. "
""
"The letters were 80 feet tall and used 80 acres of land. It could be easily read at 36,000 feet; we were flying at 1,100."
""
"Our parents were in the field waving as we flew over, dancing and cheering as we passed overhead."
""
"This photo, taken with an old Cannon AE1, captured the exact second Mindi’s eyes first saw the message carved into the earth."
""
"It was a split second I will never forget. This image may be my life’s most perfect moment. A moment that didn’t happen by accident."
""
"In the millions of split seconds you (hope to) have left, what is one that might change your path forever? "
""
"What are you willing to trade to go chase it? "
""
"When you know your trade, be sure to show up for it."
""
"Oh, and bring a camera."
""

The question you quit answering: “Is this who I want to be?”"
""
"The passing of time can pull at the once vivid colors of your life’s most significant blessings - and then dull them into a blurry grey."
""
"The things we once viewed as a prize, over time, often fade into our surroundings, and we begin to take them for granted. "
""
"The car. The house. The title. The salary …. Your friends. Your kids. Your wife … Your calendar. Your happiness. Your self-worth."
""
"We allow our life’s greatest blessings to slip into the blurry grey to that we can make room for our next prize."
""
"Today, as you think about the prize you’re chasing next, ask yourself this simple question:"
""
"“Is this who I want to be?” "
""
"The answer is yes or no. There is no grey. "
""
"Dude, we all answer “no” at different points in life. But when we ignore it, everything in our life slips into a deeper shade of gray - especially our feeling of fulfillment."
""
"And it can’t be fixed until we see it."
""
"So boss, this life you’re living — Is this who you want to be?"
""
"Yes or no - you own it."
""
"See you on the inside. "
""

"My lifetime has a mission: To inspire one thousand dudes to write their second-half story; their life, their calendar, their terms, their trade. I call it #normal40 "
""
"In this mission there are no quarter-ends, no analyst calls, no month-end reports or fiancnial reviews. "
""
" - Those were the ingredients of yesterday’s dream. "
""
"In this dream, there’s a metric of one thousand thank you letters from one thousand people who have been inspired change their lives for the better."
""
" - Those are the ingredients of today’s dream. "
""
"May I have the courage to keep it."
""
"Welcome to the #normal40."
""
"#ownit "
"#coaching"
"#thetrade"
"#onethousanddudes

The life you’re living. The one all around you. The one you’re sitting on, sitting in, and living through. That one. "
""
"The life that has built your home and filled your garage. The life that has blessed your marriage and made your family. That life."
""
"The life you inherited, the life you built, and the life you share with everyone around you. The life that has crafted all it is you have become—that life. "
""
"That life of yours is pretty great."
""
"We all spend time on the well-worn path for More, Different and Better. But along that path, some places beg you to stop and enjoy the view. There are times in your life that beg you to be still enough to give your life a little of your love."
""
"The blur will rush back soon enough. But today, at this moment, give thanks for the things you would never have thought possible only ten years ago."
""
"See the gifts. Remember the journey. Give it your thanks."
""
"Oh, and be sure to give some credit to your spouse, who did at least as much as you did to create the life you share. "
""
"Dude, from my seat, you’ve got a lot of places to offer your thanks."
""
"As you head into Friday, give just one minute of love to what it is you have all around you. "
""
"See you on the next page, boss."
""
"Oh, and next time, bring a friend."
""
"#normal40 "
"#ownit"
"#legacy #coaching #thetrade

I’ve learned a lot about success and failure. I carry the deep scars of this lesson; I live my life by this lesson today:"
""
"The greatest difference between a successful or regrettable outcome is the uncomfortable conversation that either happened in time -  or was too late and avoided."
""
"Regret takes root in the uncomfortable conversations you avoid."
""
"Successful outcomes move proportionally with your willingness to have uncomfortable conversations that deal with misalignment."
""
"This is true of conversations with your team and boss. BUT - "
""
"This is also true of your willingness to have uncomfortable conversations with yourself - about yourself. "
""
"How the rest of your life plays out will depend on whether or not you show up (on time) for the tough conversation."
""
"This is your life’s halftime. Are you replaying the first half, or are you building your second-half story?"
""
"This is your second half, and it will be your story. You can choose to show up for it, or you can choose to avoid it."
""
"But it’s your ball, boss. See you in the second half. "
""
"Play me if you need me."
""
"#normal40"
"#ownit"
"#coaching #executivecoaching #thetrade"
"#inspiration

I may not (yet) know your name, but I know you’re here. And I know why."
""
"I may not know your story, but I know you’re here. And I know how you feel."
""
"I may never know what line of what post you read first, but I know that sometimes, in some posts, something tugs at your mind and pokes at your soul. Something brings you back."
""
"I may not see you in my comments, nor will you be able to share and like most of what I talk about here. But I understand why, and I know it feels lonely."
""
"Dude, I may not know you, but I see you."
""
"I know the hours you’ve put in and the trades you’ve made to get where you are. I know the headcount in the house is working its way down instead of up. I know your image is more important to you than you let on. I know you wonder about having “enough” to finally do it. I know you feel obligated to stay, but you feel anxious about what you might be missing if you do. And you’re wondering why the path doesn’t feel like it once did."
""
"I know you are balancing who you might let down against who you might finally be able to lift up. And I know you’re wondering who, if anyone, is safe enough to talk to about what you’re wrestling with. "
""
"Yea dude, I get it. "
""
"This week I attended several events in the community - more than I had since separating from W-2. I lost count of the number of people who said, “I read your posts.” and then went on to tell a story - their story. "
""
"The conversations started the same: “I think what you’re doing is, well, fascinating. I would love to talk about it sometime.”"
""
"I may never be lucky enough to meet all of you, but I hope you know I’m glad you are here. "
""
"To those I did meet in the last few days: Corey, Bob, Peter, Scott, Jeff (twice), Jeremy, Adam, Amber, Marlon, Scott, Justin, Eric, Kyle, Marv, Mark, Terry, Jason … and others … Now I see you - and I know you. "
""
"You have become a lantern for me. "
""
"My mission is to help one thousand dudes write their second half story. My proof will be one thousand thank you letters from dudes, their spouses, or their kids. A note that simply says, “Thanks for being here, dude. It mattered to me.”"
""
"When you share a post, a story, a like, a comment, or send this to a friend, or when you send me a DM looking for a call, you become the lantern for others who need it. "
""
"Do me a favor, be a lantern. "
""
"It will reward you in ways you can’t possibly fathom."
""
"It’s your ball, boss."
""
"#ownit "
"#normal40 "
"#coaching #executivecoaching #motivation "
"#thetrade

Your journey stops feeling whole when you quit wondering where the path will lead."
""
"When you quit wondering: "
" - What will I learn, "
" - Who will I meet, "
" - Where will I go, "
" - How will I help, and "
" - Who might I become; then"
""
"Your work here is done. "
""
"You can choose to recreate it through a reset of your wonder, or you can trade for a new path and a new wonder."
""
"What you do is up to you. But doing nothing will hold you right where you are, just like it has. "
""
"I admire people who knew when their work was done and acted accordingly. "
""
"There is no greater self-awareness than to understand when it is your work is done, and there is no greater courage than to do something about it."
""
"Your gut will know when your work is done long before your head will admit it. Your gut isn’t reminding you to preserve your old life; it’s showing up to prompt you into a new one. "
""
"So, maybe your work there is done."
""

Nine months ago, I separated from my executive position. When I separated, I thought freedom would be felt by saying “yes” to all that I wanted to do. I was wrong. "
""
"I discovered freedom was lived when I could say “no” without fear of repercussion."
""
"Freedom isn’t the ability to say yes; you have that. "
""
"Freedom is your ability to say no without fear - and it empowers you and gives you energy."
""
"Freedom like this truly is free. But freedom doesn’t come with W-2."
""
"But dude, that’s the adventure. Who put you where you are? Who, other than you, do you think will put you where you’re called to go? Just the thought of that should feel free."
""
"Freedom happens the moment you eliminate the ability for anyone else to issue a consequence. And …"
""
"Freedom happens the moment you eliminate the ability for someone else to take something away."
""
"Freedom is often traded away for your pursuit of more and the preservation of image. This sounds bad, but you know it’s true. "
""
"These will be your chains, but you are free to break them."
""
"Your freedom will be found in the “no.”"
" "
"Your legacy is found in the “yeses” that now get your time."
""
"And dude, your legacy matters."
""
"Share this with a world who needs it - you know them better than I do. Dude, if not you - who? "
""
"Then join this closed and private (free) community in the comments. Let this be one small step toward freedom. "
""
"#normal40"
"#ownit"
"#thetrade"
"#coaching #executivecoaching #careerchange #legacy

For those who have change brewing, this is for you."
""
"What are you afraid of? "
"Failure? "
"Change?"
"Tarnished image?"
"Not appearing smart?"
"Looking like a fool?"
"Appearing wreckless?"
"Looking like you’ve given up?"
""
"Or are you afraid of:"
"Success?"
"Living something new?"
"The pressure of learning every day?"
"Building a team, you’re responsible for?"
"Creating something more significant than you?"
""
"Chances are it’s all of the above. Welcome to The Trade."
""
"But - There’s one thing worse than all of that fear: Regret."
""
"You should fear turning five years older and still being right where you are, holding on and grinding to the end. "
""
"In ten years, you’ll wish you would have tried and failed."
""
"You’ll wish you would have sacrificed your image and risked looking like a fool."
""
"Because then you wouldn’t be wondering if you had it in you."
""
"Are you choosing fear or regret? You will trade one for the other."
""
"Fear or regret: You’re making that decision right now."
""
"When the trade arrives, have an answer to these two questions:"
""
"1.) What do I really want?"
"2.) What am I prepared to give up to get it?"
""
"Dude, it will not be free. It will be a trade."
""
"And you will own it."
""
"#normal40 "
"#ownit "
"#coaching #executivecoaching
Wondering how to talk to your spouse, here's an idea - share The Podcast with them. This dude did ... there's a new feeling in the house: Optimism.

“Dude, I love what you’re doing. I get something out of every post. Keep them coming!”"
""
"I hear this every day. And, yes, I read every note. It makes me feel good until reality sets in: What I’m doing isn’t fully working for this dude - yet."
""
"You show up, you read, you reflect, and you’re motivated. Maybe you’re even dreaming. Then you get back into your life, the grind, and think tomorrow you will have the energy to act. Next week will be easier. I’ll do it when this project is done. After the holidays I’ll be ready. "
""
"One year ago, that was me."
""
"You show up here believing that you’re getting something out of me, something out of one of my posts, or something out of one of these punches."
""
"But that is not what you want. "
""
"Well, to be clear, it’s not what I want."
""
"I don’t show up here to entertain. I don’t show up here to offer two minutes of mental freedom - of dreaming - so I can do it again tomorrow.  "
""
"Dude, that is not why I am here."
""
"I’m not here to deliver a clever line, a deep thought, or dopamine hit."
""
"I’m not here to sell you anything you don’t need, and I don’t have the time or the energy to convince you to do something you don’t want to do. "
""
"I am not here, so you get something out of me."
""
"Dude, I am here so that I can get something out of you."
""
"I don’t care what it is in you. I care that you let it out. I care that you dream about what it is, what it would look like, and what it would feel like to get it."
""
"I sit here and dream about how we get it out of you. And I dream about hearing you tell the story to your kids. "
""
"Be sure you dream about that day. That day is why I’m here."
""
"Dream big - you own it."
""
"Join me in this free closed, and private group in the comments. Only the members will know you’re here."
""
