Twenty-one years ago, I worked for a US Senator. I was 26."
""
"It was the world just before September 11, 2001. It was before a war on terror, Anthrax, iTunes, iPhones, and flat-screen TVs. It was before many US Senators carried mobile phones. "
""
"In May 2001, I was driving US HW34 with the Senator when the small, Senate-issued, flip-style phone in my pocket began to ring. And ring. And ring.  "
""
"It was a significant event, and the phone in my pocket was the conduit for anyone trying to reach the Senator. Everyone from the President to other Senators to major news outlets was calling. He took a few minutes for each of them."
""
"A young reporter from CNN broke the story. His name is Jonathan Karl.  Jonathan called my phone, asked where we were and said he was leaving DC immediately to connect with us. Quote: “Tell me where you are, and I’ll be there. I am coming.” "
""
"The story was significant - the coverage was great - the crew came and went in a day. I was a young, pion staffer who got to feel a part of it for no reason other than it was my day to staff."
""
"For the next twenty-one years, Jonathan continued to have a front row to the world … all while raising a family. Today, he’s the Washington Bureau Chief at ABC news and is seen by millions weekly."
""
"Last weekend, after twenty-one years, I was able to reconnect with Jonathan Karl. Only now, I’m not a staffer. I’m not an executive. I’m just a dude - with a distant memory of one event in the ten thousand he has covered."
""
"But …"
""
"Before I could sheepishly bring up our encounter of 21 years ago, he brought it up to me; he remembered. He said it was his first big story - the first one he broke. And he said it mattered to him, and he remembers the day clearly. "
""
"I never forgot that day in May 2001. And I was thrilled to learn that Jonathan Karl hadn’t either.  Even better, I was blown away by his decency, the time he took with students, adults, and faculty who wanted to meet him, and everyone he encountered. "
""
"Hey Jonathan Karl - thank you for the memory, thank you for being so present, and thank you for being a dude to admire."
""
"I hope to see you again on the #normal40 highway. "
""
"Next stop, Woonsocket!"
""
"When your phone rings, be curious enough to answer it."
""
"#normal40"
"#ownit "
"#abcnews #southdakota

Know who you are, and muster the courage to stand in it."
""
"It was hard for me to confess. It took years. The correctness of “humility” made sure it was in check - made sure I was in check. "
""
"But it was always there. "
""
"It guided me with decisions every day. It built my friendships, relationships, resume, and reputation. It drew out my honesty, it motivated my intention, it influenced my strategy, it informed my decisions, and it placed others before me. But I had to hide it."
""
"Eight months ago, I resigned from my corporate job. Maybe the most remarkable thing about separating is what I’ve come to accept about myself when I’m not a title, an image, a company, or a press release. "
""
"I no longer need to be the title on my wall, wearing the obligation that comes with it. Instead, maybe for the first time since grade school, I get to be the guy in my skin, re-learning the obligation of being me."
""
"In this time, I’ve come to accept what drives me. And I’ve come to accept who I am, and I’m mustering the courage to stand in it. "
""
" - I’m wired to be admired. "
" - I do things in search of admiration from others. "
""
"For 20 years, I thought admiration was a character flaw. I saw it as a selfish weakness - it was ego. Seeking admiration was lacking humility and was not servant. "
""
"But I don’t see it that way anymore. "
""
"I see admiration as a motivator to do great things with and for great people. I see it as fuel for good when it’s channeled towards topics that can improve lives. "
""
"I don’t hide from it anymore. Mostly because I’m done trying to be who I’m not, but also because I don’t have the time to be anyone else. So, I’m going to stand in it. "
""
"Maybe you’re like me. Maybe what you seek is an outlet for your skills that builds daily impact for others. Perhaps you would do almost anything if you thought it might help someone have a better day, week, month, year, or life. And maybe the delight you feel when helping allows you to feel the way you want. Perhaps impacting the lives of others brings you joy."
""
"Why am I sharing this? Because I hope it encourages you to think about who you are. How are you hard-wired; what motivates you, guides you, and fills you up?"
""
"I’m sharing this so that you stop and think - and maybe even reflect and act."
""
"I’m sharing this so that maybe - maybe - one day you will look back at this message and send me a note that says: “Dude, thanks for being a lantern when I needed it.”"
""
"For better or worse, that fills my tank."
""
"And I need that tank for the next guy, and the next. "
""
"That is who I am. So, I’m going to stand in it."
""
"Tag someone here that you admire, and tell me why. "
""
"It will fill their tank. "
""
"#normal40"
"#ownit "
"#coaching  #motivation #inspiration

" - Give up on dreams, "
" - Leave without a plan, or"
" - Abandon anything you love."
""
"I do write to dudes who:"
" - Love your jobs, teams, and companies; "
" - Have done well and achieved more than you thought possible;"
" - Work hard, have integrity, and are admired by others;"
" - Have good marriages - but marriages that need attention; "
" - Feel the time remaining with your kids (and parents) fading."
""
"I teach to those who:"
"- Feel called to chase something brewing inside and need someone to help navigate it;"
"- Are in the halftime of life and are doing the math on how to live your best half;"
"- Know your best days are in front of you, but you can’t live them from the chair you occupy;"
"- Feel a desire to do something more significant with your life, but don’t know where to start;"
"- Want the freedom of time and full control over the calendar - while you are still in your youth;"
"- Are ready to trade a financial metric for success for an impact metric of life success;"
"- Want to be admired for being the person you are capable of being, for your family, your friends and your community."
""
"I don’t write to people who want to quit."
""
"I write to people who are finally ready to start. "
""
"I’m writing to you. You are the only reason I’m here, boss."
""

""
"Maybe your time has come … not your time to quit, but your time to start."

"It’s your ball, boss.  And this is game day."

In February 2022, I made my life’s most significant professional trade."
""
"What I traded for was not given to me. It was not offered to me, I did not apply for it, and I didn’t get recruited for it. "
""
"It wasn’t already built, and it wasn’t clear how it would work."
""
"What I traded for didn’t come with a title, a team, a logo, or a paycheck. "
""
"And it didn’t have physical assets, revenue, or a guarantee."
""
"What I traded for was my freedom:"
" - Calendar freedom & freedom of time"
" - Email freedom"
" - Freedom of any deadlines"
" - Freedom from needing anyone's permission"
" - Freedom to explore"
""
"I traded all I had built for a chance to live out who it is I’m capable of being."
""
"This is a trade many don’t understand, and it’s a trade that may or may not work out."
""
"But it’s a trade I will never regret - not in this lifetime."
""
"What would you be willing to trade? Want to talk about it?"
""
"Join this group (in comments) or schedule a call. "
""
"It’s your ball, boss. "

You believe change is harder for you than it is for others."
""
"You believe a unique situation makes your mountain steeper."
""
"You believe your ruts are a little deeper; the risks are somehow greater for you than for others. "
""
"You believe your age, your savings, your family circumstance, your pay, your security, your responsibility, your reputation, your team …. You feel it is uniquely more challenging than it is for others."
""
"This is the narrative you want to believe - it’s the one you tell yourself every Sunday afternoon. This is the narrative you need to be true to let yourself off the hook."
""
"You took your own road to get here. Your desk. Your title. Your family. Your education. Your health. Your savings. Your reality - this is the sum of who you are today. You own it, and you know the road."
""
"But who you are is not any different than who I used to be. "
""
"Change for anyone who has established themselves is hard."
"- It feels like you’re quitting, but you’re really getting started."
"- It feels like a confession, but it’s a declaration. "
"- It feels like abandonment, but you’re taking back your life."
""
"Trading who you are for whom you want to become requires a remarkable level of grit, humility, and courage. "
""
"But if you have those three things, you’ve got all anyone has ever needed - or had - for the journey. "
""
"Once you confess you have all you need, you’re ready for the next step.  You’re ready for a ramble."
""
"See you when you get here, boss."
""

What if your calendar today was blank? What if the crisis you’ve been working on vanished? Seriously.  What if … "
""
"What if the month end didn’t matter, the new initiative took on your timeline and the reviews due tomorrow went away?"
""
"What if the evening text messages quit stealing from your family, the vacation emails never again happened, and your work calendar was never in front of your kid's activities or a date with your spouse? "
""
"What if you could go for a run anytime, not only when it fits?"
""
"What if you could take a trip when it worked for your family?"
""
"What if the work you were doing was something that filled you up rather than drained your soul?"
""
"What if you slept better after being freed from the list of things you hope to get done before the meeting tomorrow?"
""
"What if you had coffee on your deck and watched the sunrise, listened to the birds sing, and read a book … for an hour? "
""
"What if you quit considering retirement as an age and a net worth? And what if you quit killing yourself trying to get there?"
""
"What if you started thinking about retirement as the time in your life when others will seek your gifts and will compensate you for them on your timeline? "
""
"What if you created work you enjoyed using the gifts you love? And what if you would love to do that work - on your terms - well into your 70s?"
""
"What if you used some of what you’ve been saving for old age and used it to bet on a younger you? What if you quit giving up your freedom in youth for a chance of living with it in old age?"
""
"What if you did that? What could that feel like?"
""
"Dude, that feeling in your gut. I know what it is. It’s the bitter end of the freedom you used to have when you were younger, and it’s the overwhelming desire you have to get back. "
""
"Freedom like this is free. But it does not come with a W2. "
""
"That’s why I call it a trade. If you’re looking for the free option, you’re already living it. "
""
"Dude, this will be the hardest trade of your life. But it also gives you life."
""
"This takes time. It takes a process. It takes courage. It takes a strong marriage, and it takes work. "
""
"But what it gives in return can’t be articulated in a post."
""
"If you’re feeling the bitter end of your freedom, let’s take it back."
""
"Do me a solid - share this. It’s your turn to be a lantern. "
""
"Welcome to the #normal40 highway."
""

When I was 20, I admired people who were funny. "
""
"When I was 25, I admired people who were smart."
""
"When I was 30, I admired people who were in demand."
""
"When I was 35, I admired people who had wealth."
""
"When I was 40, I admired people who had power."
""
"At 45, I admire people who are free, and know how to live it."
""
"I admire people who know who they are and then muster the courage to stand in it (most of us can’t)."
""
"I admire people who know who they no longer need to be and muster the courage to leave it (most of us won’t)."
""
"I admire those who shed the weight of being who they are not for the chance to live who it is they are (most of us will regret it)."
""
"I admire people who give of themselves to leave a mark (most of us spend our life missing it)."
""
"I admire the people who take a bet on themself. And,"
""
"I admire them more every time I see them get back up."
""
"Who do you admire and why? "

LinkedIn has thousands of posts on how to “hustle.” "
""
"Chances are you’ve done it, or you’re doing it. I did it. But - "
""
"I am done with the “hustle.” It’s a word I’ve come to loathe. "
""
"Hustle is a trade of your time, your energy, your health, your friendships, and your relationship with your wife and kids - for money. "
""
"Hustle places money as your life’s success metric."
""
"But after years of hustle, your energy starts to fade. Your belt size goes up, your health is a factor, you sleep like crap, you discover you have no real friends, and your family is indifferent to whether you are home or gone. "
""
"You realize maybe you chose the wrong metric. And it sucks."
""
"To make it worse, this is a one-way trade. None of that hustle buys any of that back … not your time, your health, your friendships, or your family. "
""
"Look, dude, we all work. We all work hard. If we’re lucky, we love our work. During our work, there will be times when we have to hustle. Sometimes that hustle lasts months or even a year. "
""
"But if you’re on year five or year ten of the hustle, where success is always “more than last quarter,” you might be the one getting hustled."
""
"Dude, take it back. Yes, it will be hard. But it will also be the best trade of your lifetime. And you know it. "
""

"I meant to ..."

"Sorry I didn't show up for ..."
"I had meant to share this with ... "

""

"Others will judge you on your actions, not your intent."

"At the end of your life, nobody stands up and says: "His thinking about doing something really changed the life that I could have had if he would have done it.""
""
"Your action defines who you are. Action does not hustle, a word I'm coming to loathe, action takes priority.  "

"The output of your action will someday be all that remains to show you were here."

I’ll never let it happen again. You are wrong: Hope is a strategy."
""
"We’ve all heard it. Some of us have tried to be the smart one to level it in someone’s direction. After all, what a great line. It hits hard. "
""
"You know how to play this phrase: You wait for the moment where there’s insufficient data to support an idea, then you pounce. “That sounds great, Mike, but hope is not a strategy.”"
""
"The conversation is dead. One dude feels smart. Mike looks like an idiot. And we get back to being who we were yesterday. We quit dreaming, we avoid instinct, we don’t listen to our gut, and we avoid the nonsense of imagination."
""
"Brilliant. "
""
"I left my job to start a company I hadn’t yet defined. "
""
"I have all the same expenses in life you do. I have one child in college, and another is going in less than two years.  I’m not independently wealthy. I don’t have enough to live on for the rest of my life, and I feel the pressure to address that gap."
""
"My mind told me to stay - to ride it out and play it safe."
""
"My gut told me this was my time. My passions led me to help people make tough choices that give them their freedom back. My instincts told me a thousand people felt what I was - but nobody was talking about it - and my imagination showed me a thousand ways this MIGHT work. "
""
"My hope told me I didn’t need to have the right one to start."
""
"Don’t confuse your hope for courage, boss. It’s my hope that led me to leave. It is my hope - nothing more - to bring you these words. And it remains my hope that I will make it work."
""
"Dude, hope might not stand alone, but it better stand with you - no matter the path you’re on."
""
"To work for a company without hope, for any cause, for any single person, or to work alone without hope … is hopeless. "
" "
"Dude, your best self will be found on the other side of hope."
""
"The note below - this is what hope looks like. "
""
"Whatever is inside of you, I hope you get it. "
""
"Dude, it’s time. "
""

An open letter to you - for you - from you:"
""
"Available. For what and to whom are you available?"
""
"To be available would suggest that you have time in reserve - and that you’re willing to give it where needed. "
""
"So, again, what time today will you be available - for me? "
""
"What else will be competing for that time - with me?"
""
"What else will you be thinking about in that bit of time you said was available - to me? "
""
"What thoughts do you hope I don’t see? What concerns do you hope I don’t discover? What part of your life will again take more of you than it deserves - from me? "
""
"What percentage of you will I get when you claim to be available? And what projects will be rushing your availability - for me? "
""
"Your availability doesn’t bring any value unless you also make me a priority. "
""
"To make me a priority is to deem me more important - AND to treat me as such."
""
"To make me a priority requires you to be available, and it requires you to be present. To make me a priority places your interest in me above your interest in others. And it requires that you show up here ready to put in the work. "
""
"Look dude, I know you have a lot going on, and some days are more about survival than being available. Those days are tough."
""
"But I also know that someday I’ll be gone - I’ll be reduced to someone who used to bother you and something you used to think about. "
""
"I’ll fade from the best of you into the moments you never had and the dreams you still wonder about. "
""
"But today, I am here, just like yesterday. Today I will cling to the hope that this is the day you’ll make yourself available - for me."
""
"After all, it matters to both of us."
""
"Sincerely,"
""
" - Your Future"
""
""

When I was 25, I knew where I wanted to be by age 30."

"When I was 30, I knew where I wanted to be by age 35."
" - So, I did it."
""
"When I was 35, I knew where I wanted to be by age 45."
" - So, I did it."
""
"When I was 45, I knew where I did not want to end up when I was 50."
""
"And that confused me. So, I stalled. I didn’t know how to pivot or where to pivot towards. I didn’t know who to talk to. I didn’t want to feel like I did. "
""
"I felt guilty for wanting something else - something more significant."
""
"And that made me feel … well, guilty. Alone. Discontent."
""
"Your path has been obvious for twenty years. You show up ready to work and put in the time. You take the assignment, you do all of the travel and give up nights and weekends and work on vacation."
""
"You trade your time away from home for a shot at the next promotion, the larger team, the larger title, and the greater pay.  We set our sights on a goal, and we give our lives to making it happen. "
""
"Look, your first half was not the wrong path. Climbing was not a bad thing. Your first half is not something to regret, feel wrong about, or apologize for. This is who you are, and that was the path you chose. As a result, this is the life you have."
""
"But there’s a pivot. One you didn’t expect and one that happens at the top of your professional career."
""
"Your first half was built by 2010, you; the dude you used to be. Your second-half life needs to be built by 2022, you; the dude you’re finally able to become. "
""
"What don’t you want to be in 5 years? What are you going to do to avoid it?"

I’ve worked for three fantastic companies, traveled to 25 countries, led countless classified Pentagon briefings, was badged into Google[X], was in the Macy’s Thanksgiving Day parade, was promoted and demoted, helped purchase some fantastic companies, and helped transact a $2.1BN public-to-public company sale. “I’m sorry, but it’s time to get your affairs in order.”"
""
"We all think this is advice heard by other people, until it isn’t. "
""
"In your life, chances are high that you will get an opportunity to hear this for yourself."
""
"When it’s your turn to hear those words, you won’t be thinking of your 401(k), your IRA, or your automobiles."
""
"You won’t be thinking about the project due next Thursday, the quarterly results, your annual bonus, your promotion, your title, your image, or your memberships. It’s only in your health that these things matter. "
""
"At the moment your health is taken, all of the above shed all of their value to you."
""
"Instead, the moment you lose your health, you will be thinking about how you will adequately express the depth of your love to your wife and wish you would have done it more often."
""
"You’ll be thinking about how to articulate the pride you felt for your kids from the moment of their birth and wish that you would have made them a greater priority."
""
"You will be wondering if you created enough memories, done enough traveling, shared enough laughs, and offered enough guidance to last their lifetime, now that it’s outlasted yours."
""
"The affairs you will care about most in your final days will not require a court to split or be taxed by a state."
""
"Your life‘s most valuable affairs are not the assets you accumulate in your health. Your life’s most valuable affairs rise to the experiences you shared with those you love and how you were able to show them your love, every day. "
""
"Getting your affairs in order doesn’t start with a diagnosis. Only the urgency does. Getting your life affairs in order begins when you’re done reading this post."
""
"Are your affairs in order?"
""

This is the last mile of a four-mile lap I’ve run hundreds of times."
""
"In the distance, you can see the farm where I grew up. "
""
"There’s nothing in this photo that wasn’t placed there by my family. Not only the fence, even the road itself, after being vacated by the township, is now under the care of our family. After all, according to the township, this road doesn’t lead you anywhere."
""
"On this mile, the last mile, I chose to run with my head up."
""
"In the miles before, I could be distracted by uneven ground, the curiosity of a neighbor’s operation, thoughts of fatigue, or a nearby sound. "
""
"But on this mile, the last mile, I run above the pain as I reel in the distance between where I am and the place that built me."
""
"The four-mile trek around the section has come to represent a lot in life:"
" - The aggressive start down the road as we leave home.  "
" - The second mile, uncharted territory moving us away from our roots."
" - Then, in the far distance, in our self-made world somewhere in mile three, the outline of home in the distance starts its gentle tug."
""
"As I move closer, step by step, the memories of a thousand laughs and a thousand struggles romance around in my mind."
""
"In the final mile, I give thanks for each step behind me and all the lessons each step has taught me. "
""
"Finally, I thank the mile ahead, the mile that’s been vacated, as it delivers me back to where I started."
""
"Home isn’t the place you leave. It’s the place you return to, physically or mentally, when you need it. And if you’re a farm kid, something about you changes somewhere in that mile."
""
"Not everyone can have a place like this, but everyone can have a place that grounds them and reconnects them to who it is they are and who it is they used to be. "
""
"For me, these are the two necessary elements of determining who it is I’m willing to become."
""
"If you have a place, maybe it’s time to pay it a visit."
""
"If you don’t have a place, a park will do perfectly."
""
"We all need a place that grounds us. It can be vacated or populated. Then, show up for it and give it your wonder."
""

It’s okay to put your life into your career and to wake up at the height of it - to find it unsatisfying. "

"It’s okay to have a skill, a brand, and a talent that you’ve given your life to, and it’s okay that you don’t want to give it the rest of your life."
""
"It’s okay to wish you didn’t feel the way you do. It’s okay you haven’t talked about how this is gnawing at you. And it’s okay that you’re still where you are; grinding through the years."
""
"But - "
""
"Dude, it won’t be okay to still feel like this in a year. It won’t be okay never to tell anyone how you feel. And you will regret it, until your last breath, if you don’t explore who it is you might have been."
""
"Look, to desire change is not an admission of wrongdoing. It is not a confession of a wrong choice, nor is it burnout, burnup, or flameout.  "
""
"To desire change is the wisdom of knowing when your work in one place is done, and it’s the power of accepting there’s a larger story begging to be told."
""
"The desire you have for change is not a curse. "
"The desire you have for change is a blessing."
""
"Don’t wonder who you might have been."
"Trade for it. "
""
"What’s been your life’s best trade? "

Delay your satisfaction? You’ve done that long enough."
""
"We teach our kids the lesson of delayed satisfaction, just like our parents did to us, and theirs to them. "
""
"You have become remarkably good at it. Sure, you have the satisfaction of a big job and good income, some great trips and a nice car. "
""
"But you’ve delayed the satisfaction of certian wants, like:"
""
"- The satisfaction of having full control over your time."
"- The satisfaction of having total control over your calendar."
"- The satisfaction of having your phone off in the evening."
"- The satisfaction of not taking calls while on vacation."
"- The satisfaction of getting up one day and deciding that this is a day for your kids and your family."
""
"Look, dude, if you’re reading this, you’re not average. So quit wandering through this post as if you are."
""
"You’ve achieved more than you ever thought. You’ve done what you set out to do, and at some point today, you’ll sit at your desk and wonder: “Is this it? Is this what I will do for the next 7-10 years of my life?” "
""
"What you don’t realize is that you answer that question every day.  Every day you live just like you lived yesterday, you’ve decided to delay your satisfaction … again. "
""
"Maybe you’ve delayd it long enough. Maybe this is your time to cash in on some satisfaction. Maybe it’s time to start a plan."
""
"A delay isn’t forever, boss. Even your ten-year-old gets the new bike or new gadget eventually. And so should you."
""
"This is your permission: You have delayed it long enough. This one is on you."
""
"This satisfaction won’t be free, but it will be easier to walk around with than the regret you’re going to have if you don’t own it."
""
"In case you’re wondering, that is why I’m here - to be a guide. "
""
"Send a note, book a call on my website of join this closed LinkedIn community in the comments. "
""
"But dude, do something. It’s so satisfying."

Throwback Post - The dreams of a bygone era."
""
"The prairie landscape is dotted with long, vacated, quiet, lonely, and fragile farm sites. These farms, vacant for a generation, now stir deep wonder."
""
"Deep within these homesteads, I see the under-appreciated struggles of yesterday through a somewhat romantic lens, and with much-deeper admiration. "
""
"From the driveway of this site, I will sit for hours trying to relive how things were on this site 50, 70, or 90 years ago. And I wonder how the farm and the family and the landscape changed over time to arrive in the state it is today."
""
"Abandoned farmsteads are not about the failing buildings that will soon melt into the earth. Instead, they represent the hopes and dreams of a family who risked it all to build a barn to protect their animals, THEN a house for the family. "
""
"I hear the laughter of children playing after chores. I can see the horses being harnessed in the barn. I feel the hope of a plentiful harvest and imagine the trips to the well. "
""
"I also feel the gut-wrenching devastation of drought, the loss of loved ones unable to find care, and the decision to auction the life they built after relentless drought."
""
"I feel it all from the driveway."
""

In the prime of your life, you will evolve from doer to teacher. "
""
"Most people get lost in this transition. You will become annoyed, frustrated, and irritated — making you annoying, frustrating, and irritating to those around you (ask your family)."
""
"Your transition will not be a demotion or an erosion, although it may feel like it at first."
""
"Your transition will not be stagnation. Nor is it burnout, flame out, or a blowout - unless you fight it."
""
"This may be your life’s grace - but only if you embrace it."
""
"There will be a time when it will be wise to reduce your focus on the answers that make you smart, and it will be smart to place your focus on the experiences that make you wise."
""
"The transition takes years. And you have two options:"
""
"You can chase smart and remain a doer. "
" - Many people do this for a lifetime - and are frustrated. "
" - Chasing smart is a competition against others - it never ends."
" - Chasing smart is a fight to maintain who it is you used to be. "
""
"Or"
""
"You can embrace wise and become a teacher of others. "
" - Few make this transition gracefully; it requres a trade."
" - Wisdom is the accptance of self independant of others."
" - Wisdom is given away to those who seek it."
" - Which, in turn, creates those who seek you. "
""
"Knowing when to make that break can be the challenge. "
""
"The key is knowing when it’s time and then owning the transition."
""

For everything in your life, there will be a best day to start."

""

"Your gut doesn't bother with best and worst. Your gut knows that today is the only day you really have and that everything else is in your mind. It knows your mind will stall."
""
"You’re going to read this and mostly agree. Then you’re going to think about it for 37 seconds and maybe like or comment. But porbably not."
""
"Then you’re going to go about your day. Then, by this evening, you’ll have another day in your life in your rear-view morror.  At that point, this day, the only day you have, will be fading into another yesterday. Another day moved from the column on the right, to the column on the left. "
""
"Another day you’ll never get back."
""
"OR - "
""
"Maybe today is the day to make the call. "
"Maybe today is the day to sketch the plan."
"Maybe today is the day to tell a friend."
"Maybe today is the day you do it different."
""
"Or, maybe it’s not. "
"But know this - this one is on you."
"Nobody shows up to do this for you."
"This note, this is as much as the world is giving you today."
""
"Boss, it’s your ball. This is your day.  Own it."

There is a shift brewing inside of you, and it is real."
""
"Rehearse your answer to the following:"
" - “So, what do you do?”"
""
"No matter what you do for a living, you make this answer interesting."
""
"You also set up the delivery to elicit questions - questions that also make you sound more interesting. But you only want one or two follow-up questions. After all, you don’t really want people to know you; you only want them to find you interesting. So, you change the subject to give off some sense of modesty, and you return the question. “And what do you do?”"
""
"Dude, yes, you do this. I did it, but at least I own it."
""
"Interesting is jet fuel. It’s healthy and motivational. It drives performance and stirs curiosity."
""
"You love to do interesting things at work, be on an interesting project, read about interesting people, go to interesting places … primarily so that you can go to social settings and be, well, interesting."
""
"But somewhere in life, ironically around your age, what you find interesting starts to shift. What used to be interesting, the things that have come to define you, the things you mention in your introduction, start to erode, albeit slowly."
""
"There’s a shift happening. You’re still doing interesting things, mainly as defined by others, but you’re becoming fascinated by things that have a larger meaning and fewer financial links."
""
"Control over your calendar sounds interesting to you. Time with your growing family is interesting. Using your skills to help others is not something you do because the non-profit board is made up of heavy hitters, but because the mission compels you."
""
"Dude, you did that."
""
"There’s a shift: You wonder if the interesting work you’re doing will matter in ten years, and you wonder what you would do if you weren’t making so much money and could start something new - something you’ve been dreaming of. "
""
"You wonder what it would be like to be that free."
""
"You’re starting to think less about what makes you interesting and more about where you can have an impact on the things you love, with the time you have left, using the gifts you’ve created."
""
"You’re thinking about chasing impact."
""
"Chasing impact is different. It’s not about the stories you make; it’s about the stories you hear. "
""
"Chasing impact isn’t about maneuvering a resume in the short term; it’s about building a legacy over the long time."
""
"Chasing impact seeks the answer to a different question: “What do you do to impact lives?”"
""
" - Interesting is fun, but it dies with you."
" - Impact is rewarding, and it lives on after you."
""
"Impact is the new interesting. Trade your interesting and chase your impact."
""
"Dude, THIS is why you’re here."
""
"Looking for some clarity? Answer this:"
""
" - What will you do to impact lives? "
" - How will you know you’ve been successful?"
""
"Welcome to your #normal40, boss. "
"It will be everything you make it."
""
"Nothing more and nothing less."

"Most of all, you’ll feel alone. "

My wife teaches kindergarten. She asked her kids to write down one wish - anything in the world: Here’s what they wanted:"
""
"“I wish my dad could stand and play catch with me.”"
" - His dad was in an accident and is in a wheelchair."
" - He wanted this for his dad more than for himself."
""
"“I wish I could eat ice cream that never melts.”"
" - This isn’t about the ice cream."
" - This is about the company who eats it with her."
""
"“I want six moms. Well, the same mom, but one for each day of the week.”"
" - Kids know when we’re present and when we’re not."
" - Kids know when we’re home on time and when we are not."
""
"“I wish unicorns were real.”"
" - Hey, who doesn’t want unicorns to be real?"
" - This one may have been mine.  Sorry. "
""
"“I wish I had a park in my backyard.”"
" - His parents are divorced and split custody."
" - His dad has him every-other weekend; they go to the park."
""
"Kids know. They see it. They hear it. Most of all, they feel it."
""
"It’s more than words. It’s more than kids just saying things."
""
"You will never get another trip through this time of life. Be sure the trades you make are also trades you won’t live to regret. According to a few kids in this class, the rules are shockingly simple: "
""
" - Be home to play catch when asked."
" - Be a parent each day who shows up with curiosity."
" - Be the icecream that never melts"
" - Put a park in your backyard. "
""
"If you do this, your kids will find their unicorns."
""
"If you had, but one wish, what would yours be?"
""
"Your kids have an answer. So do I."
""
"Maybe it’s time you did, too. "
""

This is my daughter, Grace. This is a photo from today."
""
"This is an open note to dads:"
""
"For every first, there will be a last. "
"Often they meet on the same day. "
""
"I've shared a roof and a permanent address with Grace for the past eighteen years, five months, and three days. "
""
"But today is the last."
""
"In that time, we've shared thousands of firsts."
"In that time, we've shared an equal number of lasts."
""
"Time is real."
""
"The time you have left with kids in your home is moving from a list of firsts to the balance sheet of lasts. Before it's all over, it will balance perfectly. "
""
"Your life has a clock. It will be cruel, relentless, and unwavering. You'll come to learn the only thing you really know of it is that it started. "
""
"Dude, eighteen years, five months, and three days ago will be here before you know it. "
""
" - Don't arrive at your future and wish you would have done more. "
" - Don't arrive and wish you would have cared less about your car and more about our kids. "
" - Don't arrive and regret you didn't take the trip; book the date, give the hug or chase the dream. "
" - Don't arrive and wish you would have tried harder, done more, or loved better."
" - Don't arrive with regret for a dream you never chased. "
""
"I haven't got it all right in my life. Nobody does. But if there's one thing you ever take from me, let it be this:"
""
"It is better to risk living with failed attempts than to die with a pocket full of dreams."
""
"Eighteen years ago will be here before you know it. "
""
"Live with the risks of trying, or die with the regret of not. "
""
"You own that, dad."
""
"So do I."
""
"#normal40 "
"#ownit "
"#fatherhood

I trust my gut."
""
"You will hit a time in your life when you wonder if the path you’re on is still the right path for your lifetime. This is often the first time in your life you’ll feel out of sync, out of energy, and out of sorts with where you’re going and what your life is costing you."
""
"It will be confusing, frustrating, and lonely. It also delivered you here."
""
"You spent the first half of your life listening to your head. You did the right things, and you played by the rules as you knew them. You have many of the spoils of your aims as a result."
""
"But the answer to what’s next is different.  It won’t be found in your head; it will be found in your heart and your gut. "
""
"When this feeling awakens inside, you have two choices: "
"    1.) Ignore it and keep living another yesterday, or "
"    2.) Explore it and build a new tomorrow."
""
"Your gut wants you to answer your call. Your brain wants you to buy something new to hold you still. "
""
"This is the battle brewing you. It’s the one that brewed in me. "
""
"The change you seek won’t take five years, but the decision to make a change often does. The feeling you have isn’t new. You’ve been putting it off for months or years. You’ve been ignoring it. But your gut is like that; it doesn’t settle. It storms."
""
"Maybe it’s time you trust your gut. Here's how to start:"
""
"1. Define what it is your gut wants. Bring it clarity. Write it down."
"2. Answer, "Why do I want this?" Write it down. Explore it."
"3. What is one tiny action that helps you explore? Write it down."
"4. Tomorrow, start with number 1."
""
"This action is simple, but it won’t be easy or fast. A few of you will do this, but most won’t.  A few of you will get a new tomorrow, but most won’t. "
""
"That is how this works. "
""
"When it comes to your gut, listen to it, challenge it, cultivate it, appreciate it, and trust it. But never ignore it. "
""
"Ignoring what in your gut has one caurantee later in life: regret. "
""
"Trust your gut now, or wish you would have tried later."
""
"That’s your trade - and you own it."
""
"#normal40"
"#ownit "
"#coaching #executivecoaching #lifestyle

Part of knowing what we’re missing is re-discovering all that was never lost; it was only under-appreciated."
""
"I wish there were a pressure-relief valve for this feeling in my gut."
""
"I used to think about this … a lot. I needed one. "
""
"Sometimes there would be a ball wadded so tightly in my belly that I could sit still and feel it - all the time. It never left."
""
"It was a perpetual feeling of having just eaten an entire pizza, and all I was left with was the regret of eating it, the curse of digesting it, and the misery of wearing it. "
""
"And it would sit there, in my gut, day after day."
""
"Then I found a pressure relief valve. Well, I found one that worked for me. Maybe it will work for you. "
""
"The ball in my gut started to deflate when I did at least one of three things. "
""
" *** Spoiler alert: Dude, this isn’t free; it will require your effort. There’s no pill or vitamin (I've looked), and there’s no shortcut - you do the work or keep the ball in your gut."
""
"Here’s the hard work: Decide."
""
"That’s it. Decide. Well, decide and act. "
""
"There are one of three things you have to decide on that will relieve the pressure. "
""
"1.) You will decide to be the absolute best at what you’re asked to do - at home and work. You’re going to decide to show up and put in the effort. "
""
"2.) You will decide to take action on something new. You’re going to decide to make a call, write an email or write the plan. You will decide to form an LLC, invest in the company or talk to an investor. YOU are going to decide and act."
""
"3.) You will decide that nothing is so wrong with your life that it can’t be mended by all that is right. You will decide to give thanks for your many blessings and all of the wonders in your life you’ve been too busy to appreciate. You’re going to accept, for yourself, that where you are is precisely where you need to be for where it is you are going."
""
"Dude, if you want to live lighter - if you're going to get rid of that ball in your gut, do all three."
""
"Give your blessings some love today, boss.  My guess is they will all love you back. "
""
"Dude, you built that."
""
"So, own it."
""

Sometimes your goalpost gets moved, and you react with anger."
""
"But usually …"
""
"You move your own goalpost and hope nobody notices."
""
"You were going to do it by now, but you haven’t started."
""
" - You were going to talk to someone by now …"
" - You were going to ask for it by now …"
" - You were going to have it set up by now …"
" - You were going to end it by now…"
" - You were going to start it by now…"
""
"Instead, you moved the goalpost. And almost nobody noticed."
""
"But you noticed - and you’re mad. At yourself. Now it’s smoldering in your gut and eating at you. "
""
"Accountability is a thing. We use it because it works."
""
"Accountability keeps you honest and moving forward - towards a goalpost that won’t move. We all think we’re good enough not to need accountability…but that makes us wrong. Even you. "
""
"Wondering where to start? "
""
"Share it."
" - Share your dream."
" - Share your fears."
" - Share your views, wants, and desires."
" - Share what you feel and where you think it may lead."
""
"Share with a spouse, a child, a friend, or a dude from LinkedIn. But it doesn’t start until you share it."
""
"Let it be a lousy first draft. But let it be a draft. Then it exists. "
""
"It will be harder than you think - and more helpful than you know."
""
"I get to hear a lot of lousy first drafts, every day, from people all over the world. I’m always impressed, often inspired, by how un-lousy the “dream drafts” tend to be."
""
"Do you have something in you that needs to exist? Share it. Until you share it, the dream doesn’t really exist…yet. "
""

I left my corporate job seven months ago. I remember all the feelings. Here are a few things I felt as I resignd, and a few things you should know if you’re considering a change."
""
"First this: In seven months, there hasn’t been one moment of regret. Not one. "
""
"Here’s my advice - "
""
"You’re going to feel like you should have the answers."
" - Be grateful that you're curious; you can’t start until you are. "
""
"You're going to wish you would have started earlier."
" - Be grateful that you’re mentally ready to build a plan."
""
"You’re going to wish you knew the path was safe."
" - Be energized about you’re willingness to chase."
""
"You’re going to feel lonesome like others are ahead of you."
" - Find comfort in knowing you have someone to follow. "
""
"You’re going to worry about losing what you’ve already built.  "
"- Take pride in knowing you built it for this."
""
"You’re going to be convinced one more bonus will make it easier. "
" - Be reminded that it didn’t matter last time."
""
"You’re going to believe tomorrow is a better day to start. "
" - Your gut will remind you that time will make it harder."
""
"You’re going to wonder what others are thinking.  "
" - Be assured they’re praying to have your courage someday.  "
""
"You’re going to be looking for someone to follow. "
" - Be aware, soon enough, others will be looking to follow you. "
""
"You’re going to give chase to the unspoken dream inside you."
" - Be kind to yourself; the clarity of it will take time to define."
""
"You’re going to chase all it is you’re capable of being."
" - Be thankful that you already have all it is you’ve become."
""
"You’re going to ask for signs - omens - that affirms your path."
" - Be open to the fact that this note may be the omen you seek."
""
"You’re making a trade that is equal parts exciting and scary."
" - Be reminded that nobody regrets chasing a dream."
" - The only regret letting them fade."
""

5:35 AM Note Today: “What do you want your legacy to be?”"
"I was asked this question already this morning."
"Here’s the good news - I have an answer (below)."
""
"Here’s the reality - it took me years to know what it was."
""
"I often ask others the same question. How it sits in your gut tells me where you are in your #normal40 journey, and it tells me what you need for the journey."
""
"The GUARD:"
"If the question annoys you, seems too grand, or if you move the topic to include your opinion on how the word “legacy” is not aligned with your view of self, you’re deflecting. You need a GUARD to prevent you from getting 15 years further into life and regretting the actions you have so far failed to take. "
""
"The LANTERN:"
"If the question confuses you, and the best you can do is give several answers, you’re ready to explore. You’ve come to accept that your clock is ticking, and you know you have something more significant to give than what you did yesterday. You accept that your life will have a legacy, regardless of how you feel about the word, and that you’re not yet living up to it. You need a LANTERN to show you the first steps off the only path you know. "
""
"The GUIDE:"
"If the question causes you to share a few dreams and passions you know are begging for attention, or if you’re thinking and dreaming more vividly about what’s next but are afriad of the first step, a trade is imminent. You need a GUIDE to help you refine and organize and bring clarity to your next steps so that you have the courage to do more than think about it."
""
"The KEY:"
"If you answer the question without hesitation, and if you use the answer to clarify for yourself and others how you prioritize your time, you are ready. You have the Key of clarity. "
""
"On your journey, you will need each: A GUARD against future regret. A LANTERN to light a new path. A GUIDE to help you find your way. A KEY to let you inside the life you’ve been missing. "
""
"So … "
""

Maybe that’s not stress you’re feeling. Maybe it’s anxiety."
""
"Stress has physical symptoms like irritability, anger, fatigue, and sleep troubles.  Ask your spouse or kids; they see it."
""
"Stress is what you feel on Monday morning when you know you didn’t get the project done, you’re behind, or you feel underprepared for what’s ahead. "
""
"Anxiety is different."
""
"Anxiety is a persistent, excessive worry, often undefined, that doesn’t go away. Only you see it or feel it. It smolders in the gut and you are unable to name it. "
""
"Anxiety is what you feel when you start to care less about the things that used to matter. It is what you feel when you know today’s actions will be just one more step away from the place you want to be. "
""
"Anxiety is when you know something needs to change, but you don’t know when, how, or who can help you with it."
""
"Stress can be solved by looking out and offering more hours of work - so you do. "
""
"Anxiety is dealt with by looking in and exploring who you’re seeking to be and accepting it - but you don’t."
""
"If you get to the weekend and still have a pit in your gut (even after self-medication), maybe that’s not the stress of work. Perhaps it’s the anxiety of believing you’re on the wrong path, knowing you don’t have a plan for change, and the frustration of not knowing where to start. "
""
"And it sucks that it all has to be done alone - mostly for your image."
""
"Dude, look, you will tolerate the wrong path for many years before anxiety sets in. You may be at the top of your company when it does. "
""
"When this finds you, there’s a trade you make: "
" - You will trade your calling and accept who it is you are becoming. Or"
""
" - You will trade who it is you’re becoming in order to know who it is you are capable of being."
""
"You cannot do both. One is traded for the other. "
""
"Nobody does this one for you, boss.  You own it. "
""
"My life changed the moment I made the trade. "
""
"Yours can to, but it requres you to show up for it."
""
"It requires a trade. "
""

When I walk at the farm, it is not for physical exercise. It is purely a spiritual, emotional, and mental exercise. "
""
"I slow the pace of everything, not the least of which is my gate."
""
"I let my mind find things to wonder about and then dwell there. "
""
"Inevitably, somewhere along the long dusty road, I find a few rocks I will adopt for my journey. "
""
"Sometimes I sit to study them, and I wonder about them. I wonder about how they made their way here, and I wonder, in their lifetime, how many living things they’ve come to know. And I wonder what about them caused me to stop and make the connection. "
""
"I walk by millions of stones on a walk, stones that have been here for, well, a long time. Then I wonder about the odds that we would come to know one another - on this day, during this mile."
""
"Inevitably, I bring a few with me on my journey. Or maybe they take me on thiers. We spend time together on the path, sharing what it is we hope to one day know, see and experience. Then I’m reminded that my days will number fewer than his. "
""
"Along the walk, I will hold a stone in each hand, and I will get to know its edges and smooth surfaces. I will think both about how the edges were formed and about how long it has taken to mellow."
""
"I will think about their color and what it is that created the complex shades and streaks of personality into every little crevasse. "
""
"In an hour, we will have shared a lot. By that time, the stone had seen my rough edges, the many shades of my thoughts, and witnessed what makes me stop, be still, and give thanks. By now, the stone knows better about who I am than many of the people I spend equal time with in my life. "
""
"All because we share the dusty path. "
""
"Inevitably, my time on the path comes to a close. The hardest part about our journey is when I let my stones go back into the dusty path."
""
"I don’t know what it is about the bond that gives me pause, but the irony and wonder of how we came to know one another make it hard for me to allow them to settle back into their former life. "
""
"Or, maybe it’s hard for them to release me back into mine."
""
"Whatever it is, this is a small dose of wonder that keeps me coming back. And I will be back, so long as the dusty path and the stones will have me, for I will be unable to journey long before they’re unable to host. "
""
"That’s a fact, and I own it."
""

""
"This change is different. "
""
"This change cares less about money and title, and more about time with family, control over the calendar, the death of working guilt, and weekend emails. "
""
"This change is the desire to finally chase who it is you are capable of being - and that who it is you want to become."

"This pursuit is not about change."

" - A bigger title is change. "
" - A new community is change. "
" - A larger salary is change. "
""
"But these are no longer the changes you seek."
""
"Becoming who it is you’re capable of being is transformation. "
""
" - Deploying your core strengths in service to a mission you can’t yet write is transformation. "
" - Building a revenue stream from your core gifts that you would give away is transformation.  "
" - Hoping you get a chance to show up, only as you are - being who you are, at any time of the night or day of the week, for someone in need of precisely who you are, this is not change. "
""
"These are transformations. "
""
"Quit coming here looking for change. Start coming here believing in a transformation."
""
"Quit showing up here wondering, hoping, something will find you."
""
"Start showing up here believing it’s on you to define what transformation is and that it’s in you to define it."
""
"Quit showing up here believing your best days may be behind you."
""
"Start showing up here believing that transformation isn’t only what the world wants from you, but maybe it’s also something the world wants for you. "
""
"Start showing up here … for you."
""
"This one is different, boss. What’s next for you probably isn’t on a recruiter's active list. "

There’s a thin line between what’s next and what you want. You not only choose the answer, you choose the question."

"What do you want? VS. What’s next?"
""
"Do you want what is coming next?"

""
"Do you want the future you built with yesterday’s work?"
" - If not, what do you want?"
""
"Do you want to continue down the path you embarked down 20 years ago - before you were you?"
" - If not, what do you want?"
""
"I tend to ask this question a lot. I tend to get the same two answers:"
""
"1.) Everything"
"2.) I have no idea."
""
"The truth is, they are the same answer, but one of them accommodates ego."
""
"Before Normal40, you seek to answer the question: “What should I do next.” This is usually an option presented to you by someone who shares the path you’re on - by someone who also benefits. You will talk to friends and mentors about your thoughts and dreams."
""
"After Normal40, you seek the answer to a different question, often for the first time: “What do I really want?” You feel this in your gut; a dream or a calling felt only by you. You don’t talk to anybody about these thoughts and dreams. On some days you feel guilty for having them."
""
"The soul of Normal40 is to have an answer to this question: “Dude, what is it that you really want, and what are you willing to trade for a chance to taste it?”"
""
"Once you know what you want, you will immediately know exactly what you no longer need - and will no longer tolerate."
""
"This is the moment of clarity. And this moment isn’t often found on yesterday’s path, with yesterday’s thoughts using yesterday’s roadmap. This brings you more yesterday. The answer you seek is inside you - right where you left it 20 years ago."
""
"Don’t think about what’s next. Those days are over."
""
"Think about what you want. And be prepared to start with one of the two answers above. Then start down the long path of defining it - and chasing it. "
""
"This post is the lantern to your path."
""

"20: I traded my farm kid credentials for U.S. Senate staff"
"25: I traded the Senate for a chance to learn business finance"
"30: I traded finance for a chance to learn international business"
"35: I traded international business for a chance to learn executive leadership as company president"
"40: I traded general management for the C-Suite leading M&A"
"45: I traded the C-suite for a chance to … well, write this. "
""
"I live, ironically, a five-year rhythm. "
""
"I’m here because I made a trade a few months ago, a trade that looks wildly out of place … Well, because it is. "
""
"You’re here because there’s a trade on your horizon. A trade that feels wildly out of place for you. Well, because it is. "
""
"When you get to this point, you’ll lock up. I did.  "
""
"- The trades are no longer easy; the stakes are higher"
"- The trades aren’t fast; there’s more to navigate (in your mind)"
"- The trades have more risk; but only perceived finance & image"
"- The trades feel lonely; your guide is a dude on LinkedIn (hello!)"
"- You’ll overthink worst-case; seriously, we all do."
"- You’ll think one more bonus check will make it easier; it won’t."
""
"I made my most significant professional trade of a lifetime this year. "
""
"It wasn’t given to me, offered to me, and I didn’t apply for it."
""
"I took it. "
""
"There was no guarantee."
"There wasn’t a safety net."
"There wasn’t a backup plan."
"To this day, I don’t have a resume."
"But I jumped anyway."
""
"I didn’t know what my future was."
"But I did know what my future was not."
""
"I did not know where my future was."
"But I did know where my future was not."
""
"When you are sitting where your future isn’t, it’s time for a trade."
""
"When you’re ready to take it, let me in."
""

"It’s hard to know if clarity was less important until this point or if you simply had it as you were moving up. "
""
"But today, you want clarity. "
""
"-Clarity on this feeling you have in your gut. "
"-Clarity on whether you should stay or go. "
"-Clarity on what it is you’re here to do. "
"-Clarity on who you’re here to help. "
"-Clarity on what will bring you joy and how to get your time back. "
""
"Heck, you’d love some clarity on what it is you wish were clear. "
""
"Clarity. "
""
"Some of the hardest questions to answer in midlife are the ones that deliver personal clarity. For these are the ones we find in the places we’ve never been taught to look. "
""
"Lack of clarity is not a fog that lifts; it’s a wilderness to navigate."
""
"But in that wilderness, there is clarity to be had, but it will be on you to seek and find it."
""
"The moment you find it — the moment you step out of the wilderness and can put into words what it is you’re going to do with the rest of your life, this is a moment that literally changes your life. If it’s happened to you, you know. "
""
"Clarity is not found in your office chair. If it were, you’d have seen it by now."
""
"But something brought you here - to the edge of your base camp. Maybe this is the day you organize your search."
""
"A journey is best navigated by those who have passed this way before."
""
"If this is your day, pack a lantern and choose a guide. "

I talk to a lot of dudes. I learn a lot of things. "
""
"After hundreds of hours, here are a few common themes & questions I get each day."
""
"- “I know I have more to give. I mean, I like my job and my coworkers, and I make good money, but I don’t want to be here in five years.”"
""
"- “I feel deflated, almost defeated. I feel like my best days are in front of me, but I also feel boxed into this position.”"
""
"- “We’ve created a lifestyle that relies on this income this job brings. It’s not an option to walk away; we have bills to pay.”"
""
"- “My wife stays home with the kids, so it’s on me to suck it up and put in the hours, take on the travel, and sacrifice my nights and weekends.”"
""
"- “How do I know if I have enough? You’ve done it. How did you know you would be okay? And dude, what did it feel like when you left?”"
""
"- “What did your wife say, or how did she or the kids feel about your decision to leave?”"
""
"- “How did you know what you wanted to do?”"
""
"- “My family is getting older. I want more time with family, and I want some freedom to travel. But I don’t know what that looks like outside of how I did it yesterday.”"
""
"- “I don’t want to regret not taking the chance, but I think next year it will be easier. Just one more bonus. One more set of options to vest. One more round into the 401K…One more year. Then I know I will be ready.”"
""
"These are the conversations of someone in their Normal40."
""
"Here’s what I can tell you."
""
"The moment you discover who it is you want to be for the rest of your life is a moment you will never forget.  BUT - "
""
"That moment doesn't arrive on the back of a lightning bolt and end with you yelling, “Eureka.”  "
""
"That moment finds you after one-hundred small moments of clarity where you sit back in your chair and say: “Huh. That was interesting.”"
""
"Those moments of clarity start when you take your first step off yesterday’s worn path. "
""
"The first step off yesterday’s worn path starts the moment you decide it does. That new path requires a lantern and a guide - and that’s what Normal40 is here to be."
""
"This is on you, boss. More yesterdays, or a new tomorrow."
""
"You know where to find me."
""

“How did you know what was in my head?” I hear it daily. "
""
"Well, because I get it; I’ve been there. "
""
"If you’re here, you’re searching. You hate to hear it, but it’s true."
""
"But worse, you don’t even know what you’re searching for."
""
"You’re smart, and you do good work. You’re highly respected. You’ve even done more, made more, and accomplished more than you thought possible."
""
"You’ve saved the way you were taught and outworked others to achieve all you have."
""
"Your relationship with your spouse has been strained, but it’s probably better today than it was two years ago. Although, even now, it’s running on autopilot. "
""
"You take time with your kids, and you have a strong relationship. But if you’re being honest, usually you’re too exhausted to go all in with them on any given day. So, like your marriage, they get what you have left after a long trip, a long project, or a long day."
""
"On the outside, you’ve built an image of a loving spouse, a well-rounded mind, a strong leader, and above average earner, and you have an impressive resume. Your image in the world is strong, not by accident. "
""
"But on the inside, you feel uninspired and oddly alone. "
""
"You wonder why you don’t feel the joy that you once had. You wonder why you don’t take risks at work you once did. You wonder why you don’t have the passion for projects you once had. And you wonder if anyone else can see what you feel."
""
"But nobody but you, and this dude on LinkedIn, have any idea how you feel. So …"
""
"You’re here searching, not for a job, but a feeling."
""
"Dude, I’ve been there. And this is normal. Even when nobody else will talk to you about it, I promise, it is normal."
""
"Six months ago I walked away from a signing bonus larger than I ever thought I would see in a lifetime. I didn’t have another job or employment option. I didn’t have a list of clients. And I didn’t have a guarantee. And I’m not indpendantly wealthy."
""
"But I had all I needed to take a bet on my second-half story. I had clarity. "
""
"Clarity isn’t easily discovered, and it doesn’t come risk free. Clarity requires you to show up for it, and it requires all of you, not what’s left of you, to create it. "
""
"This is your Normal 40, boss.  "
""
"It will be whatever you make of it."
"It will start to bring clarity in the moment you seek it."
""
"Here’ the hardest part about where you are:"
""
"It’s not that you don’t have a choice which makes it hard."
""
"It’s that you do have a choice, and you’re there anyway."
""
"I’m not here to ask you to follow me - I don’t need you."
""
"I’m here to ask you to get inside yourself and follow your gut - You need you - and your family needs you."
""
"I’m just here to guide, if needed. I’ve wandered this path before."
""
"I created a free space for dudes like us. Find a link in the notes."
""
"PS - If this isn’t you, but you’re still reading, I’ll see you tomorrow. "
""
"You’ll be back…I’m in your head now. "

A note to the Voice in your head from the rest of you; "
""
"Dear Voice, I need your help."
""
"Let’s not pretend. You know there is something we talk about from time to time that gets my emotions racing. You know how inspiring it is to me, and you know how it would use each of the strengths we’ve built over the last twenty years. "
""
"You know it’s something that makes my heart skip and makes my stomach churn. And you know it raises my excitement and blood pressure anytime I’m around anyone who mentions it."
""
"Voice, I know you can hear the same calling I hear, but I need you to be better about listening to it. "
""
"When my curiosity happens, I need you to be on my side. "
""
"Too often, you point out the safety of where I am. The responsibility I have to provide. The challenges I would have getting started. The concerns over what other people would say. The risk of not betting on something that’s not fully built."
""
"Voice, you talk about me as if all we’re capable of being is all that we’ve already done.  But Voice, our finest days are in front of us. You talk as if we’ve not done this before. My potential will be best filled by others. But I need you on board - with my heart, my gut, and our courage."
""
"I need you on my side. "
""
"Being on my side is you sitting next to me, helping me get clarity on what it is I can and can’t — should and should not — attempt to impact. "
""
"Being on my side means that you help me find answers. It means you motivate me to make connections. It means that you give me the courage to share my dreams with others - and bring those in my house along side of me. It means you encourage me to be brave enough to ask for help. It means you stand me up when I’m feeling down. It means you show up and fight with me when it’s time, and you slow me down to ask for help when it’s not. "
""
"Voice, we’re in this thing together. We will live with the same love, with the same hurt, and with the same pain. We will also live with the same adventure to tell in our final act, or we will die with the same regret for the things we didn’t chase."
""
"Voice, I want you to guard me against my future regrets through your help, not anchor me to my past accomplishments through your tolerance. And I want to get started today."
""
"The moment you agree, we’re going to begin our journey of becoming all that we’re capable of being.  "
""
"I can’t wait to hear your thoughts."
""
"Sincerely, "
""
"The Best of Me."
""

Maybe my work here is done? "
""
"The moment those words left my lips, my life changed."
""
"It was November 2021. I was in a restaurant in downtown Minneapolis with my wife. We were discussing our past, our present, and our future. It was awesome. Try it. "
""
"It was in this moment, in this conversation, when I let go of an invisible burden I had placed on myself years before. It was the burden of maintaining the security of a monthly paycheck at the cost of losing my daily joy. "
""
"It was the moment that I realized what was gnawing at my soul, and what I was feeling in my gut: I was trading all I was capable of being for the ability to buy all the things I already had."
""
"It was the moment I decided to trade it back."
""
"I recalled that life isn’t always best lived when it’s the most safe; it’s best lived when it’s most exciting."
""
"Excitement is a state of being; it isn’t a mood. Everyone you share a roof with knows when you’re in a mood and when you’re in a state. I wanted to be done with my moods."
""
"This was the moment I was reminded that you can love your work and still find the return-on-fulfillment diminishing."
""
"This was the moment I let myself dream again about who it was I wanted to be. This is the moment I permitted myself to chase something more significant, even though I couldn’t yet define what it was."
""
"This is also the moment my wife took my hand and told me she wanted me to — No, she asked me to — chase whatever the world was asking of me. "
""
"She, too, was done with my moods."
""
"Dude, listen closely:"
""
"Maybe all the skills you’ve created, the good you’ve done, the strategies you’ve implemented, and the lessons you’ve learned have run their course. "
""
"Maybe you’ve given all you’ve come to give. And maybe you’ve taken all there is to take. "
""
"Maybe you feel like you do because you know - in your gut - your work here is done."
""
"If that’s true, you won’t be quitting; you’ll simply be finishing. "
""
"If that’s true, it’s time to start something new … and you won’t know what it is. Hello, awkward excitement!"
""
"When your work is done, your gut will know long before your head. It will be your gut begging you to chase something new while your head begs you to tolerate your W2. "
""
"Trust your gut; your head is too moody and governs by fear. Your gut is consistent, and it always wins the long game."
""
"So, when you’re ready to listen to your gut, it’s time to file a new flight plan; one that gets you back to where you used to be going— a flight plan that brings you safely back to you - and your family."
""
"When you need help with that plan, that’s what I do … because that’s what I did. "
""
"My flight plan delivered me here, to your eyes. Now I’m in your head. But dude, trust me, you want me here. The dude in your mirror 10 years from now will be really glad I landed here."
""
"Maybe this is your moment to remember. "
""
"Welcome to your #normal40 flyway, boss. "
""
"Today, you own it."
""
"Lets fly. "

It’s been five months since I stepped away from corporate life."
""
"I’ve discovered about myself and others. Here’s a list of a few."
""
"1.) It takes a long time to unwind. Not weeks, months."
""
"2.) After 20 years of stress in a high-pressure job, a mind manufactures things to stress over."
""
"3.) Leaving is not a vacation, and it doesn’t feel like one. (see #2)."
""
"4.) I don’t need as much as I used to believe I needed. Nor do I want as much as I used to think I wanted. This is true of everything except time and friendships"
""
"5.) Your kids need you more than they let on. You have to figure out what that means for you and then show up for them."
""
"6.) The best people in your life never go away; they wait for you to make time. They will need you as much as you need them. "
""
"7.) Investing in a heartfelt conversation with your life’s favorite people builds roots that strengthen, not leaves that blow."
""
"8.) Many dream of a similar journey. Most think others have it easier or that tomorrow will be better. This is the thought of every person who never leaves where they are today. An easier tomorow will never show up, it simply makes you believe it might."
""
"9.) The long game matters. My long game is to prioritize time for people over money for things. We all need both, and we all choose the order."
""
"10.) Your friends will think you’re crazy for leaving. But a few months in they will ask you how they can do it, too."
""
"This is my #normal40 story. "
""
"What questions can I answer about my journey?"
""

Your life has a clock. But none of us know what time it is."
""
"We all assume there is more clock left than there is."
""
"Every calling you ignore—every passion you suppress. Every dream you allow let pass without chase … We do so believing there’s more time on our clock. "
""
"And maybe there is. But maybe there isn’t."
""
"The clock is not kind. It is emotionless, relentless, and free of any burden of feeling or self-image. It cares only for itself. It’s both giving-to and taking-from (you) right now. That is what the clock does: It gives, then it takes."
""
"The clock gives you windows. It gives you the chance to do or not do it. It gives you time to get ready, and it gives you a window of time to chase what you’re being called to chase. That’s what the clock gives."
""
"But if you don’t use the window, if you wait too long, the window breaks. As it shatters, the clock takes leaves and takes with it your dreams and your courage to give chase. "
""
"Then it offers your window and courage to someone else. This is what the clock takes. "
""
"So …"
"- What window are you in? "
"- What opening has the clock given to you? "
"- What’s in your gut begging for a chance?"
""
"The windows matter."
""
"Use your window and your courage, or live with the thought of someone else using it. You will decide - through action or inaction."
""
"What’s calling you? Own it. "
""
"I help dudes own their windows. Regret has no home here."
""
"See you on the next page, boss. "
""
"Your ball. "

In February 2022, I left my job as a 1% wage earner. "
""
"I had caught what I chased: The Office. Pay. Travel. Image …"
""
"Most think I left unhappy; I’m smarter than that."
""
"What I did was harder; I left something wonderful. I left a place that I helped to build, and I left a place that also built me."
""
"You are reading this because you’re wrestling with something. You won’t comment or “like” this post because you don’t want anyone to know how you feel and what you’re thinking. "
""
"Dude, I get it. Not long ago, that was me. "
""
"As you wrestle, know this:"
""
"Leaving your job does not require that you first come to dislike it."
""
"It simply requires you to have a more extraordinary dream inside of you that deserves your pursuit. "
""
"It requires a desire to test yourself on your own merits, and it requires insane courage to trade what you’ve already created for something you can’t even articulate."
""
"BUT know this:"
""
"The friendships you’ve made along the way are portable; they don’t end when the paycheck does."
""
"The skills you’ve gained along the way will be there for you at the moment you need them. "
""
"The people you thought were deal friends may turn out to be the people who become your real friends."
""
"You will learn there are hundreds of people just like you … all hiding the same thing and tolerating the same yesterday. "
""
"You will suddenly have time to have deeper conversations with people you admire. And you will learn they need these conversations as much as you."
""
"You will wake up each morning with time to yourself to work on the things that are calling you. And you will eventually be free of the things that used to stress you.  "
""
"People will admire you for owning your clock, and they will want to talk about it. It will be hard for them not to feel some sort of quiet envy. "
""
"You will be in a hurry to do “what’s next,” but that hurrying is dangerous…it’s too easy to slip into something near what it is you used to have. "
""
"New stress will find you … like why your calendar is so empty today. Yes, truly, that will be an actual stressor in your life. "
""
"Dude, look … "
""
"If you wait until you’re unhappy to leave, you’ve already done it wrong."
""
"But if you’ve got something burning in you, especially if you don’t know what it is, but you know it’s there, we need to talk."
""
"When you’re ready to leave something you love to pursue something more, you’re prepared to take the steps I took."
""
"You’ll be glad you did. If you need an omen, this is it. "

I wrote this while at the farm. The tree somehow asked for it."
""
"One mile from where I grew up, there is a tree."
""
"It wasn’t placed there by anything more than its own will."
""
"There’s nothing particularly interesting about this tree, unless you bother to look."
""
"From the road, it’s a tall & healthy cottonwood. It is proportioned, stoic and tough, and offers shelter and shade to those who choose to seek it."
""
"The tree doesn’t ask for anything from anyone. It never has."
""
"Nobody nurtures it. At best, we tolerate it."
""
"In return, this tree gives its thanks for the opportunity to remain where it is, doing what it does, offering what it has. For the tree, it’s better than any alternative. "
""
"The tree grew up on the wrong side of the fence. It has experienced a lifetime of burdon from the livestock with which it shares space. "
""
"He offers his shade anyway."
""
"I pass by this tree a lot. I have my entire life. "
""
"Sometimes I amon foot. "
"Sometimes I am in a tractor. "
"Sometimes I am in a truck. "
"Sometimes I am on an ATV.  "
"But I seldom see the tree. "
""
"When I do, I see this tree from the road. "
""
"That’s easiest for me. It allows me to check a box without any real effort. "
""
"But this day was different."
""
"On this day, I left the road; I chose to see the tree from the fenceline. "
""
"I chose to see the tree from the angle that those who benefit from its shade greet it. "
""
"On this day, the tree revealed what couldn’t be seen from where I had always been; from the view I had always offered - the view most convenient to me. "
""
"The view from this angle is different. "
""
"From this angle I can see the aging limbs being pulled at by stress and age. "
""
"From this angle I can see the burden each of the upper limbs is placing on its once-invincible trunk."
""
"From this angle the tree can no longer hide that it has become split to its roots and is mortally wounded."
""
"From this angle, I see something I didn’t know was there."
""
"From this angle I can see all of the burden. "
""
"I had no idea."
""
"The trees in our life aren’t going to come to you. Instead, we all need to do more than see the trees from the road and check a box. "
""
"We need to see the trees in our life from the fenceline."
""
"Sometimes that’s the only angle from which we can see the burden. And only then can we help lighten the load."
""
"#normal40 "
"#ownit

Dear Managers: Why are vacations so hard to take? "
""
"If your team is not using their vacation time, that’s a problem."
""
"It’s not their problem - it’s your problem. Here’s why:"
""
"A vacation requires one thing: It requires you to take it."
""
"BUT - "
""
"A vacation that is healthy, enjoyable, relaxing, and positive requires two things:"
""
" 1.) It requires to you book it and to take it, AND"
" 2.) It requires a boss to support it, value it and respect it."
""
"If your team is not taking time off, you’re probably the reason. "
""
"You own that. "
""
"Do something great today: Ask your team why they’re not using their vacation - and then make it easy for them to take it. "
""
"If you get in their corner on this, they’ll be in your corner every day they are back."
""
"That’s what great leaders do (see #2 above)."
""
"How’s this for a metric: “Everyone on my team uses all of their vacation time. When one of us is gone, the rest of us step in so they can truly be away. This is how our team supports one another.” "
""
"That’s a metric we all could be proud to show off. "
""
"Have you ever had a vacation destryed by a bad boss?"

The other voice we all hear. We all have it; it’s always there. "
""
"It’s the voice we often trust when we shouldn’t. The voice we don’t trust when we should. The voice that motivated you to become all you are, frustratingly becomes …"
""
"The voice that prevents you from chasing all you’re capable of being."
""
"“Stupid Voice” (-Homer Simpson)"
""
"You may hear one voice, but it’s from different parts of your brain. Two voices; both trying to protect you. "
""
"Both can be right. Both can be wrong. Both think the other voice is the greater risk to you."
""
"Voice of Safety vs. Voice of Courage. "
""
"One argues the logic of preservation, the other the logic of continued pursuit."
""
"Safety cares deeply about you. It’s the voice that begs you to ride it out where you are. This is the voice that confuses the familiar with the safe. This voice calculates regret through the loss of things gained; it is blind to regret of missed windows."
""
"Courage cares deeply about you. It’s the voice that got you here. It’s the voice beggin you to push forward. This voice doesn’t calculate regret through the loss of things gained; it will gain them back. This voice calculates regret through the failure to act on windows of opportunity."
"	"
"Safety is content to ramin who it is you’ve become."
""
"Courage wants to explore who you’re capable of becoming."
""
"Today, you will listen to one of these voices."
""
"You’re listning to one right now. "
""
"Know your window and choose your voice."
""
"The windows will not stay open foerver, and no do-overs."
""
