Life lives fast and speeds up with age."
""
"My best life wasn’t lived in the fast lane. "
" "
"My best life happens here."
""
"This is a road that leads to my family’s farm. My kids are the fifth generation to use it. Just saying that makes me happy. "
""
"The road is maintained by my family. It gets used by trucks and tractors, by cars and four-wheelers, and by cows and horses. "
""
"And occasionally, it gets used by me for running."
""
"To some, this road is too slow. Its wrinkles make for a rough ride and it doesn’t lead anywhere. It isn’t seen by anyone who isn’t lost, and it doesn’t leave an impression if you’re in a hurry."
""
"To me, on this day, it’s for these reasons I stop and give this road my wonder. On this day I study each of the roads many wrinkles, and I give them my curiousity."
""
"I wonder about the rocks that are connecting with the rising sun. I wonder about the grass that waves me on. I wonder about the meadowlarks and pheasants in the distance who in turn wonder about me. "
""
"But today, I also wonder about that single tree in the distance. A tree that has been there for longer than I can remember. And I wonder if that lone tree ever finds itself lonely, or just alone. "
""
"From where I stand, at this moment, I can recall a thousand laughs with my life’s best friends."
""
"From where I stand I can recall working the ground that spreads out all around me."
""
"From there I stand I can recall the blisters, the pinches and the scratches we all earned while planting fence posts and stretching the barbed wire that floats on for miles and still earns the cows respect. "
""
"And I love it all. And love is the right word. "
""
"Nothing around me happened fast. Not here. I won’t let it. That’s exactly why everything around me runs so deep. Deep enough to leave wrinkles. "
""
"Eventually, we come to love our wrinkles. But our greatest wrinkles require our time and our wonder. "
""
"Today, take time to work on your wrinkles. "
""
"Post a photo of how you’re loving your wrinkles. "
""
"Here’s mine. "
""
"Welcome to the Normal 40. "
""
"It’s sort of awesome. "
""
"Wrinkles and all. "
""

Dear Territory Pioneers: "
""
"You must have left everything to get here. Maybe you arrived with a family, or perhaps you came alone. But thank you."
""
"You put it all on the line for a shot to make a mark on the world.  For a shot to build a legacy built with little more than your grit."
""
"You lived in a hole dug into the south side of a hill. "
""
"You tilled by hand, ox, or horse. You endured sweltering summer days, the typical summer drought, the occasional prairie fire, and the inevitable prairie blizzard. "
""
"Some of you survived, and some of you didn’t. Some of your family survived, some of them didn’t. "
""
"I’m personally not familiar with that level of courage. "
""
"Look, I wanted you to know that in some places, albeit fewer every year, the proof of your courage survives."
""
"These tracks in this photo — They are yours. And they remain."
""
"These are the tracks that you forged deep into the South Dakota prairie as you, well, pioneered the untamed lands. "
""
"The path you wore with your horse and wagon still lives on. In the spring of the year, before the grasses equalize the prairie, the rawness of your presence can be seen and appreciated."
""
"When I see them each spring, it fills me with wonder and curiosity that can never be satisfied. For it’s a courage that I simply find hard to comprehend. It is truly awesome."
""
"I don’t know where these tracks start or where they end. I don’t know if these tracks were traveled in good times or bad, with happy thoughts or sad, or with a family or alone. "
""
"But the tracks live on. "
""
"I want you to know, several generations later, we give thanks for all you did to tame the prairie. Thank you for risking it all to make it possible for others. Thank you for taking the chance. Thank you for showing up and showing the way. "
""
"For 140 years, we’ve done our part to keep this prairie native. My only hope is that the same will be true another 140 years. "
""
"Oh, and thanks for reminding me that risks are worth taking. And if you do it right, the mark you can leave will last a lifetime. Thanks for doing something bigger than you. "
""
"It matters to me. "
""
"Sincerely,"
""
"- Lon"
""

"#ownit"
"#agriculture "
"#farming "

Can you get the gate?"
""
"Growing up, I heard my dad ask me that question hundreds of times. In retrospect, it was more of an instruction than it was a question. When we stopped at a gate, we both knew the expectation. Being the 'gate guy' was not hard, but it felt somehow disruptive and unfair."
""
"When moving from field to field, we always listened to baseball. I think that’s what I would have chosen to listen to, but our pickup only had an AM radio with a single speaker in the center of the dashboard. It was enough."
""
"It was always tuned to the Minnesota Twins. I can still hear Herb Carneal and John Gordon, the voice of the Twins, coloring my mind with visions of the park and the crowd, while also taking me on a pitch-by-pitch journey of my ears - one that drew a sharp contrast with that of my eyes."
""
"Sometimes we arrived at a gate as the game would be drawing tight. While dad didn’t have to say a word once the truck stopped, we both knew what would happen next; I would get the gate. "
""
"But when the game was tight, as Herb drew our ears and our eyes from the prairie around us into a keen focus of the single, crackly AM speaker inside the dash, it also went unsaid that the gate would wait a minute. "
""

The lens we use to judge changes over time. This is true of our judgment of self and our assessment of others. It’s also true of others' judgment of us."
""
"When young, we are admired for the magnitude of our dreams and the velocity by which we give them our chase. The strong sense of self, the quest for more, and the courage to give pursue get high praise. "
""
"When we are young, confessing our needs is admired. Expressing our desires for what we don’t yet have earns us favor. Our willingness to sacrifice brings us fans."
""
"As we age, all of that changes."
""
"We become judged, so too do we judge, by the completeness of one’s harvest to which they gave chase. "
""
"Or worse, we are judged by our ability to capture what others thought was more deserving of our chase. "
""
"There comes the point in our life when you feel like having a dream turns from an asset to a liability.  "
""
"There comes the point when you feel the magnitude of your dreams before you, and the velocity by which you will give them chase changes from one of great character to that of questionable judgment.  "
""
"There comes the point when talking about all that you want to achieve will sound like a confession for all you don’t yet have."
""
"The moment you feel that, know this: This the moment you are doing what others aren’t strong enough to do. You are doing what others are not brave enough to try. You are chasing while others are to afraid to try. You are the focus of their jealousy and are reminding them of the risks and dreams they have chosen to to chase. "
""
"The moment you feel judged, go all in.  Make the trade. Give chase and go. Anything less will lead to regret.  "
""
"So, own it.	"
""

It’s been 60 days since I stepped away from corporate life."
""
"Here’s a list of 10 things I’ve come to learn about myself since deciding to separate and take back my time:"
""
"1.) It takes a long time to unwind. I still haven’t. It feels odd. "
""
"2.) After 20 years of stress, not good or bad, just the stress of high-pressure jobs, my mind manufactures things to be stressed over.  Yours will, too."
""
"3.) This is not a vacation. I report to nobody, ever, except the people with which I share a roof.  Yet, this is not a beach, and there aren’t drinks with umbrellas. It’s a middle ground between two extreams - extream work and extream relaxation. It’s new."
""
"4.) I don’t need as much as I used to believe I needed. The time for myself has acted to release the urgency I used to have to acquire and have “more” of … all sorts of random items. (Look at your Amazon orders from the last 60 days. What if that was need versus want)."
""
"5.) Your kids need you more than they let on. But it’s on me to connect. Transitions are challenging - for everyone. And there are more transitions in my house than I was aware; now I see it andI own it."
""
"6.) I had forgotten how many truly wonderful people there are in my life, and I’ve had the chance to connect with so many of them. "
""
"7.) I can show up more often - and I can be more present for my friends, wife, and kids.  "
""
"8.) Thousands of people are on a similar journey, and all of them are hoping someone else can help them navigate the path."
""
"9.) Having dad home more is an adjustment for everyone in the house.  Not the least of which is my daughter’s cat. "
""
"10.) The answers don’t come fast, but that’s okay. The long game matters. And I couldn’t be happier with how I chose to play it. "
""
"There are at least ten more … but more on that on the next page."
""
"This is my #normal40 story."
""
"What’s yours?"
""

Need a push today?  Here, try this."
""
"We all leave to go to work differently. But once we arrive, most of us wear the same face. In an instant, we transition. "
""
"We leave from different homes, the health of which is uniquely yours. We drive different cars, the choice for which was uniquely yours. We take different routes. We have different routines. We eat different breakfasts. Some work out, some don’t. Some have healthy marriages, some don’t.  Some have young kids, some don’t. Some have addictions, some don’t. All of it is uniquely yours."
""
"When we get to work however, we tend to look the same. The same logo. The same priorities. The same deadlines. The same reports. The same calendar invites. The same group email. The same problems. The same complaints. The same …. None of it is uniquely yours.  "
""
"Until, once again, it is time to return home. Once again, you will reunite with whom you were before you left. "
""
"  - 	I know, some of you think you’re the same person for 24 	hours, all year.  Bad news; you’re not.  And you know it. "
"  -	Don’t believe me? Bring your spouse to work, or better, bring 	your coworkers home for a week. "
""
"There is a loophole. It’s for those who bring work home every night, so there is no separation.  Well…."
""
"If that’s the case, you’ve probably forgotten who you are, but you have become one of the best at knowing exactly what other people need you to be for them. "
""
"Try this:"
""
"Just before you walk into work today, write down five emotions that you feel at that moment. Don’t overthink it; write down the words. THEN ASK: Who is in charge of how I feel right now?"
""
"Later, just before you walk into your home after work, write down five emotions you feel. THEN ASK: Who is in charge of how I feel fright now?"
""
"Spoiler alert: The answer is you. You are in charge, if you take charge. Others can impact how you feel, but you have to give "
"them that right. You can give it to them, or you can take it from "
"them. But it is yours. "
""
"You control how you feel. Know what else? You control how those around you feel. Especially those with which you share a roof. You control a lot. "
""
"Look, dude, it’s not easy. It is miserably hard. Taking it back will be one of the harder things you do. Thinking about it causes paralysis of the mind, a pit in the gut, anxiety, depression, dependency … and that’s before the rest of your life starts piling up. It’s hard.  But dude, you’re not alone."

N=[40] question.  "
""
"I was asked by someone along the N=[40] highway about how to address shortcomings in their retirement account. They are in the middle miles and worried that they'd be working than they wanted.  "
""
"Here's how I view it - but I want to hear from others. "
""
"For dudes, "will I have enough" is a central question to our self-worth. "
""
"Heck, I've gone a few decades with this as the scorecard for how well I'm doing as a dad, husband, investor, human. But most of all, I used it as a scorecard for my image.  "
""
""Enough" also tends to be something we're good about obsessing over privately but poor about sharing with others outwardly. "
""
"Why? Because if you show me yours, I'll (have to) show you mine.  "
""
"The thought of sharing is when self-doubt sets in."
""
"What if I don't have as much?  What if they are way ahead? What if I've been doing it all wrong? What if they discover I'm not as well off as I act? What if I find out I'm living way too far outside of my means? What will they think of me? "
""
"Come on; you've all been there. "
""
"Here's some advice."
""
"First, find a friend to talk to about it. Show them yours, and have them show you theirs. Get vulnerable and share. "
""
"Compare. Discuss. Debate. It will make you feel better just knowing.  "
""
"As an executive, an M&A guy, and a banker, I still have a few friends I share with ... and they share with me.  And it helps me."
""
"Second, remember, THERE ARE TWO SIDES of the ENOUGH LEDGER."
""
"Dude, when you search for the answer for what is "enough," you have options.  But these options require a trade. A trade that is in your control."
""
"Your options to arrive at "enough" are to:"
""
"Dial-up your monthly savings now and skinny up how you live now to get to your "enough.""
""
"And/or you can add a few years to your years of work to get to your "enough."  "
""
"And/or you can dial back your living expenses, now and later, and arrive at your "enough" sooner than you had planned."
""
"So, what's the best path for you, and what are you willing to trade? "
""
"You own the answer to that. "
""
"Once you decide, own it. "
""
"Once you decide and act, the stress of this runs away.  "
""
"It did for me.

"The fact is, this is how companies survive. It’s just how it works."

"But - "
""
"That’s not true of your family; that’s not how your family survives."
""
"If you left your family today, it would devastate them. The void would not be filled in a week, month, or year.  And maybe not in a lifetime."
""
"If your family left you today, it would launch you into a tailspin that you’ve never before experienced. The void would not be filled in a week, a month, or a year.  And maybe not in a lifetime."
""

""

""
"What if you spent as much time preparing for how you would invest your time as you do thinking about how you will invest your dollars?"
""
"What if you think about how you want to spend your time, instead of how you want to spend your money? "
""
"What if you gave your highest-functioning time to yourself, your family, your friends, and your interests instead of to your employer?"
""
"And what if you gave the best of you to the things that meant the most to you?"
""
"Dude, it’s not your money that matters, it’s your time. "

""

Want to be a leader who people respect and remember?"
""
"Great leaders share a few common traits. Here’s a list of many of the lessons I’ve learned from the great leaders in my life."
""
"1.) Give away your time to the teammates that give you theirs."
""
"2.) Take risks on a high-potentials, then let me run."
""
"3.) Give people a chance to win or lose, to succeed or fail. And know they will do both along their way to executive leadership."
""
"4.) Be strong enough to show weakness, and smart enough to use it to build trust and to do good."
""
"5.) Write notes to people around you.  Do it every day."
""
"6.) Cast a large and clear vision of the future, and communicate it more than you think is necessary. Then explain each persons role in your vision."
""
"7.) Make the decision, even when you don’t know if it’s right. You have to decide and move, or you’ll get sniped."
""
"8.) Acknowledge teammates for thier contributions and how they helped (see #5)."
""
"9.) Don’t tolerate poor performance longer than appropriate."
""
"10.) Hold 1 hour a day open for people to come see you. "
""
"I've been professsionally blessed by wonderful coworkers and by great leaders.  These are the traits I aspired to have, and traits I learned from others."
""
"If you can do these, you’ll build a team that not only maneuvers through the storm, it will be a team that wins championships."
""
"What are a few traits of the people you admire most?"

Day 10: The now-dreaded question."
""
"“Hey, great to meet you. What do you do?”"
""
"Dude, I used to love that question."
""
"The corporate ‘show me yours and I’ll show you mine.’"
""
"Game on."
""
"The question was usually on the table before my hand had slid comfortably back into my suit pocket."
""
"WDYD?"
""
"It’s the go-to for new connections and new conversations."
""
"Look, I would kill it with my answers."
""
"In my 20’s: “I’m employed by the United States Senate where I serve as a senior member of a United States Senator’s staff."
""
"In my 30’s: “I’m a bank Vice President where I created and manage a private client group of the bank’s fiannacial elite.”"
""
"In my 40’s: “I serve as a public company executive where I lead M&A across our three operating divisions.”"
""
"Then, without fail, I always asked in return: “And what do you do.”"
""
"Time to show me theirs."
""
"Last week, after 14 years, I separated from my company.  "
""
"Now the question sounds a little different."
""
"Actually, who am I kidding. "
""
"The question is the same, only my answer sounds different."
""
"The last 10 days has reminded me that a title is sought for the benefit of how others will perceive you."
""
"Don’t believe me?"
""
"Look no further than your email signature to see how you compete in the corporate game of “show me yours.” You don’t even wait for the question…you just answer it."
""
"It establishes a place in the pecking order."
""
"A title is your corporate armor. And it invariably makes its way into how you answer the question “What do you do?”"
""
"Dude, chasing that isn’t bad.  I have. And I may do it again."
""
"Good or bad isn’t the point. So, chill. "
""
"Here’s the point:"
""
"Beneath that corporate armor is the person who showed up on day one wondering if you would survive the week."
""
"Beneath that is the dad or mom, the son or daughter, the sister or brother, the neighbor, volunteer and friend.  "
""
"Beneath whatever it is you have on a business card or email signature, you have other titles that mean more to the people who mean most to you."
""
"Don’t forget about the person beneath that armor. "
""
"Don’t forget to let that person be what it is you do.  "
""
"If you had to answer the question “So what do you do?” without relating it in any way to how you make money, how would you answer it?"
""
"Me?"
""
"I’m Lon Stroschein."
"Just a willing dude who gives a damn about good humans."
""
"I want to help 1000 dudes write their second half story. I show up here on most days and try my level best to leave my armor in the closet."
""
"Some days are better than others."
""
"There’s room for any good human to join our journey. All you have to do is give a damn enough to show up."
""
"See you along the #normal40 highway."
""
"Now, tell me again, what do you do?"
""
"#normal40"
"#coaching"
"#motivation"
"#personaldevelopment

Look dude, it’s not that hard. Who gets you?"
""
"The answer is easy. "
""
"Look at your phone; who gets you today? "
""
"Ironically, you’ll know minute by minute who will get you today."
""
"Who had you yesterday?"
""
"Who had you last week and last month?"
""
"Dude, who gets you? What is their name?"
""
"Mabybe more important …"
""
"Who gets you second?  "
""
"Wait, what’s their name?"
""
"Seriously, write down their name under “Second place.”"
""
"Who gets your third? "
""
"What’s their name? Write thier name under “Third place.” "
""
"Finally, who did you not give any of you when they asked?"
""
"Who asked you for a few minutes and you simply had nothing left to give?"
""
"What’s their name. "
""
"Write their name under “Last place; I don’t find them to be important in my life.”"
""
"Dude, who gets you matters."
""
"Don’t give all your time to the short game, the one that plays to your image and your resume."
""
"Give time to the long game, the one that plays to your character and your soul."
""
"This is a trade, and trades are not easy."
""
"But avoiding a trade is messsy. Really messy.  "

I don’t feel sorry for you. "
""
"So, you failed. It didn’t work the way you wanted. "
""
"You made a bet, took a chance and you came up short. So…"
""
"Now you feel betrayed and abandoned in the process."
""
"You’re wondering where all the support went, the support that used to surround you. And you wonder why they haven’t called. "
""
"Next you’ll start placing the people around you on sides that don’t exists and you label people as for you or against you.  "
""
"Finally, you isolate a little more each day as you try to seperate from the experience and contomplate if you can recover.  And eventually you’ll start to wonder if you’ll ever got back to where you used to be going."
""
"That’s normal."
""
"It sucks. But it is normal. "
""
"But I don’t feel sorry for you."
""
"Here’s the deal …"
""
"If you lead long enough and brave enough, these events and feelings will find you. Every leader has a story."
""
"Every real leader."
""
"If you drive change and innovation, or if you’re overhauling a culture, there is going to be a failure.  Both big and small."
""
"There always is."
""
"And it always hurts."
""
"It really hurts. It feels public and now you feel beat down. You wonder if your image is damaged beyond repair."
""
"It’s not."
""
"You feel of embarassment, as if everyone knows."
""
"They don’t."
""
"You wonder if everyone is talking about you and about what really happened."
""
"They’re not."
""
"You feel like this was your life’s only shot at a postion at this level and that you’ll never have another chance."
""
"You will."
""
"You think that nobody has ever gone through this agony."
""
"I have."
""
"So, dude, I don’t feel sorry for you."
""
"Instead … "
""
"I feel sorry for those who try to get in your way after you’ve reckoned with the loss and have decided to own it and to use it."
""
"I feel sorry for those who don’t yet have this education, and I feel sorry for those too weak to take a position bold enough to ever risk it."
""
"Look …"
""
"No person is more powerful than the one who has reckoned with a loss and learned to then use it as thier gift."
""
"Leaders are built through failure, not destroyed by it."
""
"In time, this will be your gift."
""
"Need a copilot?"
""
"Call me."
""
"#normal40 "
"#executivecoaching #failure #coaching

""

""
"Let me talk to your boss to see if we can get you some budget for some better tools and training."
""
"Hey you, dude in the mirror, I assume you’re the COO, I want to talk about some opportunities for improvement in IT.  Their entire department is watching YouTube videos to do their job."
""
"Let’s put a plan together to address thier needs and consider a top-grade of the talent. IT is the lifeline of a company, and we need to be faster. Is that something you can work on?"

"So Mr. COO, you’re telling me that there is no budget for any upgrades?"
""
"Okay, let me talk to the CFO. Oh, and by the way, dude, get a hold of Facilities. You’re office is a disaster. These pictures aren’t going to hang themselves and your garbage doesn’t walk itself to the dumpster. It wouldn’t hurt to run a vacuum through here, either. And a cat in the office? We’ll talk about that later."

Day 2:  The great reflection. "
""
"This was the first day since June 1999 that I’ve not had W2 income."
""
"That’s not a big deal to some. But for a lot of us, it’s a massive change. For many of us, it’s THE thing that makes a trade so challenging."
""
"#normal40 has thought me that many of us have a “What” that resides in our gut. This is something that we long to chase and a life we long to live.  But we push it down."
""
"Right next to that is the “Why.”  This is simply who we want to do it for; it’s who we want to help. But we push it off."
""
"But next comes the “How.”  And it’s the how that stops us."
""
"It’s the How that stopped me.  "
""
"And it’s the How that is stopping you. "
""
" - How do I give up income"
" - How do I give up my identity"
" - How will I pay my bills"
" - How will I justify my decision"
" - How will I build my new future"
" - How will I recover if I fail."
" - How … How … How … How … "
" - Stop … Stop … Stop … Stop …"
" - Settle … Settle … Settle … Settle …"
""
"The How is a simple thief.  It steals your What and your Why. "
""
"And, over time, we get comfortable with that. "
""
"In fact, we rebuild our How narrative into reasons to stay. "
""
"But …"
""
"How has a profound weakness: Action."
""
"That’s it. Action."
""
"Your How will go down with one punch."
""
"Just one.  Trust me."
""
"In the moment you act, your How collapses.  "
""
"Every time. "
""
"Then, when it staggers back to it’s feet, it gives you the answers. "
""
"Your How will tell you what went well and what didn’t. Your How will show you the next step, and the next. "
""
"From there, instinct takes over. "
""
"This is the moment to chase; the moment of instinct."
""
"It was a good day. "
""
"A day that started with a square punch."
""
"If you’re starting your How in the face, punch it. "
""
"This is your #normal40 - own it."
""
"See you on the next page … "
""
"#normal40 "
"#ownyourclock"
"#coaching "
"#motivation"
"#midlife

So ... here's an interesting turn of events.  "
""
"Today (January 31, 2022), around 12:20PM/C, I'll be live in the studio with Lori Walsh and South Dakota Public Radio ... they want to talk about the #normal40. "
""
"I guess there's more people listening than I thought. "
""
"If you listen and like it, let SDPB know. "
""
"If you don't like it, let me know what I can fix."
""
"I guess it's time.  "
""
"Dream on -

Dear son,"
""
"For everything in your life there is a first, and there is a last."
""
"Your life will be full of first days. These bring you experiences to be excited about; something to chase and something that lies ahead. "
""
"But in your life, becuase math being what it is, there will also be an equal number of last days."
""
"Your first and last day of work, your first and last day of school, of a relationship, of days with friends, of time in our house and your time with your mom and me. "
""
"A first and a last. Every start eventually ends. "
""
"That is how life works, and it doesn’t discriminate. "
""
"Son, in your life, live for the last days, not the first."
""
"For in your life kiddo, it’s the last day that holds the score. "
""
"A first day is about first impressions and fulfilling the aspirations of a person you may someday be. "
""
"But your last day reveals the results; did you or did you not? "
""
"Your last day holds the score."
""
"Son, play for your last day.  "
""
"You see, today is my last day."
""
"And I choose to celebrate this day."
""
"I choose to walk the halls slowly, to give my thanks, and to be romantically nostalgic about how every day before this day has shaped me."
""
"I choose to consider what these halls took from me, and what I took from them."
""
"For 14 years I’ve been building for this day."
""
"For 14 years this day rested on a distant horizon."
""
"But on this day we will share a sunset."
""
"Son, on this day I choose to give heartfelt thanks for the friendships and the lessons I learned on a journey … time spent with some of the world’s best."
""
"On this day, I give thanks for those who took a risk on me and celebrate those who stay to carry the torch."
""
"In your lifetime son, there will be as many last days as there are first days."
""
"On your last day, leave the company/friendship/relationship stronger, smarter, and better than you found it. "
""
"When you do, it will do the same for you. And those are all things you can take with you, wherever your path leads you."
""
"And if you take it, you will have it."
""
"Today is my last day."
""
"I find myself nostalgic that my work here is done, but it is. I’ve completed what it is I came here to complete."
""
"This is the finish for which I am prepared. And it’s a finish for which I am proud. "
""
"Tomorrow I will wake up to a first day."
""
"A day that comes before all of the rest.  And I am ready. "
""
"This is my trade."
""
"I will own this.  And I will bet on me.  "
""
"And son, whenever you can, so should you.  "
""
"See you on the otherside of today, kiddo."
""
"Cheers to the distant sunset. "
""
"I’ll share a ride with you, anyday."
""
"Dad - "
""

Lookin for something easy to do today? Get angry."
""
"It’s simple. There’s a 90% chance you’ve already been there today.  "
""
"Anger is the lazy person’s emotion.  "
""
"We all feel so good when we’re angry. Sometimes we’re even impressed with ourselves (I’m not alone in this, right?). "
""
"To be honest, there’s nothing I like more than to listen to an angery stranger rip … so long as I agree with them."
""
"If I don’t agree with them, then they’re just being an idiot. "
""
"See, I found anger again.  Dude, it’s so easy. "
""
"Here’s the deal …"
""
"Anger serves one of two purposes:"
""
"1.) To motivate and inspire change in self, or"
" "
"2.) To deflect and reassign your known individual inaction or shortcomings."
""
"Let me say it different, anger can be a tool to improve (reflective and hard), or as a tool to colorlfully articulate how others are worse than you (deflective and lazy)."
""
"These are two vastly different uses for the same emotion.  "
""
"We all use some of both. But we all have a default."
""
"How do you know which you are serving? Answer this:"
""
"What do you do when you’re angry?"
""
"If you answered this question with an action you can take (bitching is not an action), and action that will require your time and energy, you’re being reflective."
""
"If you answered it with a list of things others have done to mess something up, or a list of things someone should do to fix whatever you find bothersome (read bitching), you’re probably being deflective. "
""
"Actually, maybe there’s another way to know. "
""
"If you’re reading this and your inspired to post about what an idiot I am, before you do, please review the definitions above."
""
"Truth time: I can place each of my friends into their default use for anger.  And they can do the same of me …. And yours can do the same of you.  "
""
"You get to choose how you will use anger."
""
"But know this: That which you choose most often, over time, becomes the place where you will dwell.  "
""
"What do you choose? "
""
"#normal40 "
"#ownyourclock"
"#coaching "
"#midlife"
"#motivation

""

""
"Harsh reality 3: Things are not going to follow your definition of fair. "
""
"You will choose to seek what it is you want, or you will choose to stay in the seat you are in."

"That is it.  "
""
"And you make that decision every day you wake up with intent, but fail to take an action."

"Your clock. "
""
"Your time. "
""
"Your life."
""
"Your outcome."
""
"You decide.  "
""

On this #thankfulthursday, I give thanks to music. "
""
"I give thanks to those who write it, those who perform it, those who listen to it and those that dance to it."
""
"There is almost nothing in this world that can improve and sustain my mood better than a string of notes that enter through my years and jolts through the rest of my soul."
""
"What else makes you a rock star in your own car, a lyrical poet in your shower and the world’s greatest air-guitarist is your kitchen."
""
"Music has been with me during every day of my life.  And it is awesome. Music is also a time matching capable of transporting me back to a place in time.  "
""
"On my loneliest day, and on my longest journey, the one thing I always carried was a tune. For as long as I had a song in my head to sing, or a tune in my soul to feel, the journey was never really that lonely."
""
"My music selection is all over the place.  I will shuffle from Willie Nelson to 2PAC, to George Strait to Mellencamp to B.B. King to Whiskey Meyers to …. You get it. "
""
"Who I choose to listen to is a function of two things:"
"1) How I feel in that moment, or"
"2) How I want to feel in the next."
""
"And it always meets me right where I am."
""
"Thank you music, and musicians, for being awesome."
""
"What music are you most thankful for? "
""
"Turn it up."
""

DANIEL S. FREITAS asked a super question that's at the heart of your #normal40."
""
"How do you find clarity on your second-half story.  This is, without a doubt, the hardest question you will face in your #normal40."
""
"This discovery is an iterative process. It will take you MONTHS, and maybe a year. It took me two years, but I didn't talk to anyone about it, and it didn't start making sense until I did."
""
"The discovery can't be rushed and your first draft will be shitty, but it will have you marching in the exact direction you're being called."
""
"The sooner you accept the timeline and the journey, the sooner you can make real progress."
""
"Here's what I did:"
""
"1.) Find a place that connects you to something greater.  A place that you consider a home. A place that makes you feel both big and small, all at the same time. A place that you long to return to someday, for reasons that are uniquely yours, and then go there. Go there physically or mentally, but go there. Let yourself experience it again .... not for the last time, but for another time. You'll know when you're there; you'll feel it."
""
"2.) Define who it is you really are.  Yes, write it down. Answer this: When I was at my best, when I was living my days with passion and when I was doing the most good, I was _________________. "
" - Where were you?"
" - Who were you with? "
" - Who were you helping? "
" - How do you know you were having an impact? "
" - What did you hear?"
" - How did you feel?"
" - What did you learn?"
" - What do you hope you left?"
"Write 25 things about this time. No less.  More is better...let it flow for as long is it flows."
""
"If you can't come up with 25 things, you've chosen the wrong experience. Start # 2 over again."
""
"3.) Of those 25 things, what do you want more of?  What is it that you want to do more of ... for the next chapter of life? This isn't about where, or who, this is about what.  What is the one thing you want more of."
""
"4.) Of those 25 things, what is it you want to avoid?  The things you would write-out of that portion of your life. This is your second-half story, so be really true about what you hated - f*ing hated - about that time.  This matters."
""
"5.) In this life, who do you want to help? Be specific."
" - Who are they? "
" - Where do they live? "
" - What are they going through? "
" - How do you know them? "
" - What do you know them when you see them?"
" - What do they need? "
" - Who else is there for them? "
" "
"6.) Pull it all together - and you'll need to write this down. Can you use the past (question 1) and connect it with your core (questions 2) to create a list of your core talents (question 3), and avoid the things you dislike (question 4) to help a community that's waiting for you to lead (question 5)."
""
"6.) Then share your story with me."
""
"7.) We will quit talking and act. Take one small act and start testing the plan."
""
"This is the start of your second-half story. But only if you do the work."
""
"Intending to do the work is what got you here."
""
"Doing the work will get you there. #normal40

To have what you really want, you have to be who you really are. "

"You have to give up what it is others think you are."

"Cliché? "

"Probably."

"But this cliché will show up someday and stare you right in the face. "

"This cliché will be a demon you will face in your life, and it will show up when you don’t expect it and don’t know how to deal with it."
""
"When you’re staring it in the face, you’ll long for the day it was rendered a cliché for the weak. "
""
"When you see this demon, take it on. "
""
"Fight it and own it. "
""
"The fight will be harder than you think."
""
"But the view from the other side will be worth the fight.  "
""

Everything in your life can fit into one of three buckets."
""
"Love bucket"
"Hate bucket"
"Indifference bucket"
""
"Your best life is when you have things in it to love."
""
"But the next best is when you have the clarity of mind to hate."
""
"The worst of life is to give or receive indifference."
""
"In love you will act protect, guard and grow."
""
"In hate you will act to mitigate, remove or avoid. "
""
"With indifference, you plunder, toil and stress."
""
"The opposite of love is not hate, it’s indifference."
""
"Wherever you have indifference in your life, trade it. "
""
"Trade it for something you can learn to love, or something you will hate."
""
"But trade it."
""
"You’ll never move forward with a heart full of indifference. "
""
"You’ll only burn your clock, and the clock of others who are counting on you to make a choice. "
""
"That’s on you."

Dear daughter,"
""
"Your great grandfather once said, as he was reaching his life’s end: “A lifetime is a long time, but it doesn’t take long to live it.” "
""
"My prayer for you is that phrase is several decades away from making sense to you.  "
""
"But for me, I’ve reached the age where the ironic brilliance is setting in. "
""
"This photo of you is 15 years old. But if I close my eyes, I can transport my being back to this moment as if it were yesterday.  "
""
"I can walk every step from our farm house to this spot on the earth. "
""
"I can still feel the grass under my boots, smell the dry prairie that begs for rain, hear the gentle wind as if swirls through the bluestem, and I can see the wonder in your eyes as you balance the curiousity of the herd with that of your own."
""
"This image is a reminder to me that a life is given to be lived."
""
"This image is a reminder to me that we all have a journey that is unique to us."
""
"This image is a reminder to me that we should live a life as if it were long, but appreciate that it doesn’t take long to live it."
""
"Daughter, we all enter this world with an inheritance, and we all leave it with a legacy.  What you do in between are the pages you get to write."
""
"There is nothing you can do to change your inheritance. You did nothing to earn or to deserve it. The good or bad, the easy or hard, the healthy or unhealthy; this is what you were given.  But how you choose to use it will define you."
""
"Your legacy will be defined by others. Your legacy will boil down to the words people use to describe your good deeds while on earth. But mostly, and more importantly, your legacy will be lasting based on how you made people feel. "
""
"But the pages between are yours. This is your endowment, and you need to own it on your terms."
""
"Please own every page.  Start the page without lines and fill it with the colors of your courousity and the shapes of your passions. But own every page, for they fill quick. "
""
"Your endowment will be built on the decisions you made for yourself, and of the experiences, memories, and lessons you learned along the way."
""
"Your endowment can’t be given to you.  Nor can it be taken from you without your consent. It’s in your control. "
""
"Your endowment will be filled with the best of times and the worst of times. The highs and lows will be a given, so don’t seek more or less of them, seek instead to move through them.  For this is where you will find your grace. "
""
"Eventually, your endowment will lay the foundation of your legacy, but it also plants the seeds of the inheritance of those who will come after you.  These things matter in our life. "
""
"They have mattered in mine."
""
"Daughter, you and your brothers are part of your parents endowment. And if we’re remembered for nothing else from our time here, we will leave a lasting legacy in each of you."
""
"And that is enough."
""
"Keep filling the pages, kiddo.  And please #ownyourclock."
""
"Thanks for being you."
""
"See you back at the ranch,"
""
" - Dad"
""

"In 2021, who I admire changed."
""
"I’ve come to admire those with the courage to own their sense of enough, and the confidence to make the trade for it. "
""
"I’ve come to admire those who trade their job, their salary, their cars, their trips and their image - as defined by others - for enough, as defined by self."

"In 2022, I’m making the trade."
""
"I’ll own what it is that I will no longer tolerate."
""
"I’ll make the trade and I’ll own the outcome."
""
"I’ll know my enough, and live it."
""
"This year, instead of chasing more, you have the option of chasing enough instead."
""
"You own that trade. Nobody else."
""
"Own your clock."

I grew up in a small town.  Actually on a farm 12 miles from a small town.  My home town was a basketball town."
""
"From the age we could hold a ball, we were dribbling.  Some of us played through high school, some stopped along the way. "
""
"At some point, every one of us practiced the half court shot."
""
"There were three styles, all simulated buzzer beaters:"
"1. We did the two-handed runner (a classic)"
"2. We did the one arm lancher, and "
"3. We did the fade away but fouled … so we could shoot free throws when we missed."
""
"But none of that “practice” happened when Coach was there."
""
"In fact, practice like this was reserved only for the moments when we knew Coach was otherwise obligated. "
""
"Once Coach was there, we worked on the things none of us found all that fun.  In fact, it was hard and exhausting. "
""
"We did box out a rebound drills."
"We did hours of conditioning."
"We practiced “jump-to” defense. "
"We ran an offensive rotation for 10 minutes without ever being able to take a shot … to practice flawless passes."
""
"Passing? Really coach? We have to practice passing?"
""
"But you learn life lessons in a small basketball town.  "
""
"You learn that you can put a lot of time into looking like a winner. "
""
"OR "
""
"You can put a lot of work into the unsexy skills that can make you a winner. "
""
"You also learn that if you’re never in the game, a half court shot at the buzzer is worthless and is allowed purely for the ego of shooter."
""
"We all like to be winners, in however we define it for ourselves."
""
"A lot of people will put in enough work to look like a winner."
""
"But few people are willing to put in the work to win the trophy."
""
"Few people are willing to practice the flawless art of a pass."
""
"Who around you is waiting for the half court shot, and who is moving the ball around the defense? "
""
"And which one are you? "
""
"If you don’t know which you are, the person next to you does."
""
"Own your clock.  "
""
"⏱"
""
"#normal40 "
"#executivecoaching

"Know the trade. Your joy will count on it. "

"Own the clock."

Dear Son,"
""
"During your lifetime there are things you are able to control, and things that will be outside of your control. "
""
"But there will also be things that fall outside of anyone’s control."
""
"The life events inside of your control will be shaped by who you are.  This is what you will build from your gifts. "
""
"The events that fall outside of your control will shape who you become. This is what you will build from your grit."
""
"The events that fall outside of anyone’s control will shape who you will be for others.  This is what will inform your spirit. "
""
"Give thanks for your gifts; we are given many."
""
"Be an architect of your grit; your character springs from within."
""
"Live in a dialy connection to your sprit; for it balances the fair with the unfair and allows you to reason."
""
"In your life, most of what you do will fall into one of three buckets: complaining, planning or acting."
""
"You will find yourself surrounded by complainers, this is the most common."
""
"Complaining is the only option left to those who haven’t the gifts or the grit to plan, nor then grit and spirit to act. This is Hell’s Kitchen.  "
""
"Live instead in a place of planning and deliberation.  "
""
"Live instead in a place of big dreams and intense passions.  "
""
"But live there briefly, until it is your time to act.  "
""
"Your spirit will call upon you to search, to plan, and then to act."
""
"It’s in the act that you will find your greatest challenges. There will be no one there to push you to act, only your spirit. "
""
"But it’s in acting that you find the concert of your gifts, your grit and your sprit engaged in the chase."
""
"Don’t be in a hurry to get there, but be aware of the path. "
""
"Take your time and learn daily, but also follow the omens as they surface early."
""
"My path was my own, just as yours will be to you.  "
""
"But a path is not meant to be traveled alone. "
""
"If I’m here to guide you, I hope you ask.  If I’m not here to guid you, seek someone you trust. By now, you’ll know who they are."
""
"Lastly, if you make a plan and never test it’s might, I will not waiver in my love for you."
""
"But know this: If you make a plan and never set out to test it’s roar, it is you who will carrying regret. That’s safety’s trade off."
""
"There’s a time to plan and a time to act."
""
"When it’s your time to take the shot, take it. Every time."
""
"For it’s in this action, not the planning, when we find that which we were seeking."
""
"Thanks for being you. "
""
"See you along the path."
""
"With regards, "
""
"dad - "
""
"#normal40"
"#midlife "
"#dad

Here’s something to chew on this weekend."
""
"Your life has three distinct phases that you will move through."
""
"INHERITANCE — Key question: “What was I given?”"
"In this phase:"
" - You are 100% dependent on others"
" - You inherit your birth place, your family and your health"
" - You inherit a religion, habits and stability (or lack thereof)"
" - You inherit the good and the bad your parents relationship "
" - These are the cards you were dealt; you can’t change them"
"You lead with your mouth."
""
"ENDOWMENT — Key Question: “What will I build?”"
"In this phase:"
" - Others are 100% dependent on you"
" - You make decisions for yourself; college, location & career"
" - You chose your spouse, your family size and your home"
" - You choose how you will show them love and allocate time"
" - You are in total control of what and who you will tolerate"
" - You are in control of someone else’s inheritance"
" -  You learn to win with the cards you were dealt"
"You lead with your ears."
""
"LEGACY — Key Question: “What will I give?” "
"In this phase:"
" - Nobody depends on you beyond what your willing to offer"
" - Working is an option, and it’s done on your terms"
" - You prepare to give away your Endowment of life’s experience"
" - You now work to build someone else’s endowment"
" - Your life has it’s meaning assigned and it’s being lived"
" - You consider the question: “How will they know I was here?”"
" - You see everyone’s cards, and you understand your own"
"You lead with your heart. "
""
"It’s in the transitions that we get confused. "
""
"What were you given?"
"What will you build? "
"What will you give?"
""
"The compass you seek will be found in these answers. "
""
"Own your clock."
""
"⏱"
""
"#normal40 "
"#midlife "
"#executivecoaching

Chasing who it is you want to be is worth the chase. "
""
"Searching for what it is you’re feeling called to do is worth the search. And,"
""
"Fighting against the pressures to keep you where you are is worth the fight."
""
"Trading it all, on your terms, is worth the trade."
""
"Who am I, really? "
"What am I meant to do, really? "
"What’s in me that I need to get out, really? "
"Who am I meant to help, really? "
""
"You feel like the answers should be obvious. They never are."
""
"You believe chasing what’s in us should be easy. It never is."
""
"You believe that everyone else has it figured out. We don’t."
""
"In your lifetime, making money and building wealth will prove to be the easy part. It’s the only thing everyone does. "
""
"Discovering the passions and having the wisdom to understand them will be much harder. It the only thing very few do."
""
"Interpreting what you desire and putting that into action is miserable fight."
""
"Manifesting the courage to trade your financial worth for your personal worth is nearly impossible. "
""
"Most will never do it. Most will only talk about what they would do, if they did it. "
""
"Most will judge others when they do it."
""
"But years later, we’ll all admit that we most admire those who made the trade, while we sat and watched."
""
"We admire people who own their clock, and don’t apologize for doing it. "
""
"If this is your time, explore it."
""
"If this is your time, get curious about it."
""
"If this is your time, make time for it."
""
"If this is your time, own it."
""
"For there’s more risk in stagnating than there is in pursuing."
""
"You own that, too."
""
"Welcome to the Normal 40 highway.  "
""
"I hope you take it.   "
""

Symptoms of Normal 40:"
""
"1.	You want more than what you have"
""
"2.	You want less stress in your life, but can’t ditch it"
""
"3.	You have dreams and desires that may never be fulfilled by your current path"
""
"4.	You feel guilty and selfish about having a great job with great income and that you’re not content with it"
""
"5.	You have deep loayalties to a boss or employer and you find it hard to think about violating that loyalty for other oppourtunities"
""
"6.	You work 60 hours a week and wonder to yourself if the week behind you will even matter in two years"
""
"7.	You spend portions of your week feeling like your on auto pilot; you’re not charging as hard as you used to and you wonder if people notice"
""
"8.	You check your retirement at least once a week and read articles like “How to retire on $400,000” "
""
"9. 	You start to put more energy into looking important than you put into being important"
""
"10.	You want the Subaru, but buy a BMW because it’s … somehow easier to explain"
""
"11.	You wonder what’s in it for you if you just stay the course"
""
"12.	You want to explore something else, but the change seems like too big of a risk; you have assets to pay for"
""
"13.	You want to explore something else, but you’ve never stopped for long enough to get clear on what it is. You know you’re here to give more "
""
"14.	You have a pit in your stomach on Sunday, and a skip in your step on Friday"
""
"15.	You have become less outgoing and it’s become normal to have a drink alone on your couch"
""
"16. You know you don’t have the spark you had a few years ago, and you’ve tried several things trying to get it back (insert your receipts for different OTC supplements here)"
""
"17. You’ve put on weight and you hate it…but you don’t hate it enough to actually make a change"
""
"18. 	You want to get back to where it is you used to be going"
""
"19. You’ve known for more than a year that things were off, but you’ve not done anything about it. "
""
"20. You talk about things you’re going to do someday, but you’ve not done anything more than talk about it in the last 3 years"
""
"Chances are, I’m talking to you."
""
"These are real symptoms felt by real leaders."
""
"All of thse are normal. "
""
"The road back to you starts with your first step.  That’s it."
""
"This is where your second half story starts, if you let it."
""
"Interested in feeling normal or being part of a small-group session? "
""
"Email me: Lon@Normal40.com"
""

On June 28, 2018 I wrote a note to myself.  "
""
"The title is: What advice would I give to my younger self?"
""
"Here’s what I wrote:"
""
" - Be sure to go to work in a place where you can find people to admire."
""
" - Find a leader who inspires you, and emulate them."
""
" - Put your family first, or the path will be lonely and without a compass."
""
" - Make your own opportunities by solving the problems others are stepping over."
""
" - Know that at some point in your life you will fail, and that it’s okay. "
""
" - When you fail, be sure to come out the other side with your ingretity, even if nothing else.  It’s all you will need."
""
" - When you succeeed, be sure to come out the other side with your integrity.  It’s all you will want."
""
" - When you fail, get back on your feet. The people around you will need a leader, and a leader needs to be on their feet."
""
" - Use your head, but trust your gut."
""
" - Take the shots that need to be taken.  And don’t wait too long to take them."
""
" - Remember always, and thank often, those who took a chance on you along the way."
""
" - Know that eventually you will transition into someone who gets to take a chance on others. And know that will be one of your greatest blessings."
""
" - Never under-estimate the power of a hand-written letter. "
""
" - Give a damn about things worth a damn. "
""
" - The worst-case in your mind is never the worst-case in reality; it’s better."
""
" - Be someone to love."
""
"Not bad.  "
""
"If you’re not yet in your #normal40, or even if you are, you might want to jot a few of these down."
""
"You can’t do all of these this weekend, but you can certainly be someone to love."
""
"Own your #normal40."
""

This is the last mile of a four-mile lap I’ve run hundreds of times."
""
"In this photo you can see the farm where I grew up. "
""
"My family build the fence. "
""
"Even the road itself, after being vacated by the township, is now under the care of our family. After all, and according to the township, it doesn’t really lead you anywhere."
""
"On this mile, the last mile, I chose to run with my head up. "
""
"In the miles before I could be distracted by uneven ground, thoughts of fatigue or by a nearby sound. "
""
"But on this mile, the last mile, I run above the pain as I reel in the distance between where I am, and the place that built me."
""
"The four-mile trek around the section has come to represent a lot in life:"
" - The aggressive start down the road as we leave home.  "
" - Entering the second mile; uncharted territory as we move further from our roots."
" - Then in the far distance, in our self-made world somewhere in mile three, the outline of home in the distance starts its gentile tug. "
""
"As I move closer, step by step, the endowed memories of a thousand laughs and a thousand struggles romance in my mind. "
""
"In the final mile, I give thanks for each step behind me and for all the lessons each step has taught me. "
""
"Finally, I give thanks to the mile ahead, the one that’s vacated, but takes me back to where I started. "
""
"Home isn’t the place you leave. It’s the place you return to, physically or mentally, when you need it. "
""
"This is my place.  This is the place that grounds me. This is the place that connects my past to my future as I live in the present. "
""
"Where is your place?  Maybe it’s time to pay it a visit."
""
"Knowing your destination is key to the journey.  "
""
"Own your clock. "
""

""

"The Choice: The moment you choose to take back the things that are most important and when you take control back over your life on your terms."

""
"Leave the attachment to physical things, and pack your life experiences of wins and losses; you’ll need both."
""
"Leave the emotional attachment of status, and pack your self confidence and humility."
""
"Leave the sense of power in your current life, and pack the contents of your character."
""
"At the end of the day, it’s your definition of success, your sense of adventure, your wins and losses, your confidence and humility, and your character that will matter.  "
""
"Nothing more."
""
"These are your terms, and this is your character; own them."

""

""

""
"Let’s all get back to where we used to be going."

We are all in control of our lives."
" "
"Right up until the dreaded moment we are not."
" "
"This is a photo of my friend Tim Schut with his family. This is his wife and their three school-aged kids."
" "
"They are my friends."
" "
"They are a lot of people's friends."
" "
"They are the type of people we can all aspire to be. Ask anyone who knows them...it will be unanimous."
" "
"Tim is a dude, no different than me or you. But maybe better. "
" "
"He's got a sterling reputation. He's worked hard, been a company executive, coached his kids, volunteered in the classroom and on volunteer boards, and has played the long game. Tim owned his clock and played the game according to the rules. "
" "
"Tim is a dude who always shows up. He's a dude you want in your corner. He simply makes the world a little better. Every day. "
" "
"A few weeks ago, Tim's life changed. "
" "
"And it changed after a trip to the hospital. "
" "
"Tim was diagnosed with Primary Progressive Apraxia of Speech."
" "
"The journey through this is long and lonely. And, as of today, there's not a cure. "
" "
"It will, in time, take over the clock he has so beautifully managed. "
" "
"Like any of us, Tim would love your thoughts and prayers for his journey. Please bring them."
" "
"Like any of us, Tim would love to take a few family trips while he's still able to communicate verbally with his wife and kids."
" "
"But today ..."
" "
"Tim simply wants a friend who is on the same journey."
" "
"Can you please help my friend find a fellow journeyman?"
" "
"There are only about 300 people in North America on this journey."
" "
"Tim wants to find someone to lead him, and someone else to lead along their journey. That's the type of dude he is. "
" "
"Please help us find a partner for the journey."

"Share this note."
" "
"Like this note."
" "
"Tag someone to this note."
" "
"I'm asking you to share a few moments of your clock so that he can appreciate more of his."
" "
"Primary Progressive Apraxia of Speech."
" "
"Who else is on the journey?"
" "
"Own your clock, today. "
" "

"Success: “Attainment of wealth, favor or eminence.” (Merriam Webster)"

"Success, in life, is not about a destination."

"Success in life is about your plan and your journey and how you allocate your time in order to experience it."

"Success in life is not found by looking outward and comparing."

"Success in life is found by looking inward and improving."

Dear Daughter,"
""
"Have you ever choked back tears so hard that your stomach hurt? Your dad just did."
""
"Nostalgia. This is an emotion unto itself. And it’s hard on dads."
""
"In your life’s journey, this is an emotion that will find you. When it does, love on it. "
""
"You see, nostalgia is an emotion owned by a destination, not the journey."
""
"In life, your journey will have joy and pain, but you won’t find nostalgia along the path."
""
"You will create moments to rejoice and to regret during your journey, but you can’t have nostalgia."
""
"Nostalgia is different."
""
"Nostalgia requires you to stand face to face with something you know is about to end, and to embrace it."
""
"Nostalgia is not a single emotion, it’s the collision of all your emotions crammed into a single moment."
""
"Nostalgia collides your happiness with your sadness, and then it rests on your heart and releases through your eyes."
""
"Nostalgia makes you forget the pain of a journey, and sets its focus on the friendships the journey created."
""
"Nostalgia allows you to appreciate the exact moment you’re living more than you thought possible."
""
"Nostalgia allows you to miss the past and ponder the future, all through the same hugs and tears."
""
"Daughter, when this finds you, take note and give your deepest thanks. But also know nostalgia is a gift, one to take with you. "
""
"For it’s in these moments that you’re experiencing some of the best of what life has to offer. But you have to own it and take it with you."
""
"If you wonder why dad’s cry, it’s when we see moments of nostalgia unfold beautifully, perfectly and painfully for our kids as  your life experiences come to an end. "
""
"When this collision of emotions finds a dad, and when dads can feel and see the nostalgia in our kids, we choke back tears so hard our stomachs hurt. "
""
"But truth be told, it’s compounded by the awe we have in you. All of this is too much for dads. Well, at least it’s too much for this dad. I simply couldn’t be more proud. "
""
"Thanks for the nostalgia, kiddo.  "
""
"I love this moment, and I can’t wait to see what’s coming next."
""
"Thanks for showing me how to own my clock."
""
"Dad - "
""
"#normal40 "
"#midlife "
"#coaching

"It's normal for successful people to want something different than they have built. "

""
"It's normal that they have a drink regularly to help distract the feelings. "
""
"It's normal to have tried countless different vitamins to try to recover a feeling they used to have. "

"It's NOT normal to muster the humility to talk to someone about it. "

"Thank you for teaching me:"
" - a toy truck and dirt hill was enough,"

" - rain really is a gift, "

" - that I can teach a 1100 pound calf to lead by his nose, "
" - the smell of fresh-cut alfalfa is maybe the best smell in the world,"
" - you can build a connection to the land that will last a lifetime,"
" - the term "overtime" is an opinion, "
" - a neighbor will show up when needed, "
" - you will show up for a neighbor when a neighbor needs it,"
" - you don't attend your neighbor's farm sale, "
" - little league baseball games take priority over all,"
" - it's a bad decision to wear your Sunday shoes into the feedlot,"
" - a poorly tended fence leads to a lot of cow chasing and sorting,"
" - you should never put yourself between a calf and its mama,"
" - that 10 years old is old enough to drive, and shoot, "
" - riding a bike on the lonely country roads after dark drums up crazy things in your mind, "
" - listening to Paul Harvey is mandatory,"
" - an overnight with a buddy was the best day of the year,"
" - a 45 minute bus ride is something you get used to,"
" - the county Fair IS the annual vacation, and"
" - to live there has to be something worth dyeing for."

Wondering where to start, build your answer to this question:"
""
"What is enough?"
""
"It will be different for each of us, but it will work for all of us. "
""
"The enemy of enough is more.  Get serious about defining your "enough." "
""
"There's a lot of ways you can work through this, but I'll tell you how I did."
""
"First, find your number. "
""
"Figure out how much you live on right now; annual living expenses, all in.  Let's say that's $200K.  "
""
"Then assume a 4-5% distribution of assets. At that rate, you will need a $4M net worth to live the exact lifestyle you have now.  "
""
"Next, build your plan to get to $4M. Then start working your plan. "
""
"Treat yourself like a business: You can figure out how to make more, get a better return on assets or cut expenses. "
""
"Build the plan and set three actions you will take.    "
""
"Before you ask ... You can change the number whenever you want.  "
""
"But if you don't lock in a number, you're going to get more of the same. Frustration with "more of the same" is what brought you here."
""
"It's fun with numbers - so have fun with numbers."
""
"So, what if enough for you, and what are the elements of your enough.  I'll put mine in the comments.  "
""
"How can #normal40 help you? "
""
"Own it!

I have failed, but I have learned that "
"After a failure, you have two choices:"
"Try to bury it and move on, or"
"Hold it up as the single best lesson of your lifetime."
""
"You can’t do both."
""
"Well, I guess you can try to do both, but that just makes you an idiot."
""
"If you hold it up and own it, it will help you build a personal brand of approachability, experience and authenticity. And it will give you power and freedom.  "
""
"We all fail."
""
"The moment you choose to make a failure something that works for you, instead of something that works against you, is the moment you redefine who you are as a leader."
""
"By the time you get to your #normal40, you have plenty of wins and losses in your endowment. Be sure to hold up the losses a high as you do the wins.  These are an essential part of your legacy."
""
"Oh, lastly, when you do fail, don’t wear the rag of failure longer than you need to."
""
"Get up, dust off, refocus and own it. Don’t waste your clock wondering who’s thinking about it. Chances are it’s only you. Get up and carry on; people are counting on you."
""
"Own your clock. "
""
"Share this with whoever needs it. "

What do you wish you would have started 5 years ago?"
"What stopped you then?"
"What is stopping you now? "
""
"The good news: There's' usually only one person that stands in the way of change and your pursuit of something awesome. "
""
"The bad news: That person tends to be you. "
""
"The ugly news: The reasons you tell yourself why change is impossible are all reasons inside of your control.  "
""
"For all of us, This all comes down to a measure of courage. Either we will have it, or we won't. "
""
"To justify your lack of progress you'll use words like:"
" - RISK  (It's too risky right now)"
" - TIMING  (Next year is much better year to do it)"
" - COLLEAGUES  (I can't abandon my coworkers right now)"
" - LIFESTYLE  (I need to maintain my current cashflow for bills)"
" - IMAGE  (What will people say if I leave this job)"
" - FAMILY  (I can't do this to my family) "
""
"These words each represent one side of a box you've worked yourself into, but now can't work your way out of. "
""
"That's The Box, and this is #normal40."
""
"These will be the same words you use in 5 years from now when I ask again" What do you wish you would have started 5 years ago?""
""
"Own the clock. "
""
"There will be a lot of dudes, more than you know, dudes like me, cheering you on.  "
""
"The next 5 years starts today."
""
"#normal40"
"#leadership"
"#motivation"

A paycheck is an exchange of time for money."
""
"You trade your time for someone elses money."
""
"That is how it works. "
""
"You don’t get your time back. Nor do you have to give the check back. The deal is not unfair, but it requires review."
""
"Cash and carry.  "
""
"All sales final."
""
"No returns."
""
"When you get to your Normal 40, be sure, really damn sure, you’re not on the wrong side of the deal. Be sure the math still works."
""
"Your clock is ticking. "
""
"Make sure your time is worth the trade; the math is changing and your time is a limited resource."
""
"If this is hitting you, don’t listen only to your head; it’s biased. It’s the one that got you here. That’s great, but … "
""
"Listen to your gut. "
""
"Your gut is honest. It doesn’t trash talk you like your head; it only feels for you and it doesn’t judge. "
""

The irony: It is impossible to kill time.  "
""
"The reality: Time is killing you.  "
""
"Time is relentless and cruel."
""
"We all kill time. It’s usually a phrase we use to get through what we don’t enjoy in order to experience what we do."
""
"Every living thing on the face of this earth shares the exact same fate to the exact same force, a fate that eventually succumbs to time. "
""
"Time is life’s ultimate currency, and it’s a gift. But it can’t be gifted. "
""
"Your time is a gift from somewhere else. From where it comes is for you to decide. "
""
"Time is a gift that we can’t size. It has no shape or volume. There is no way to measure how much of it you have been gifted or have left. You have today, or you don’t. That’s it. "
""
"Time is a currency we can’t bank and it can be taken from us in any given second of any given day.  Including today. "
""
"Time is going to kill all of us."
""
"Do something today to make your day, week or month better.  Call a friend. Write a note. Make a call. Take a walk. Change your job. Apologize. Thank a teacher. Volunteer your time. Coach a friend. Make time for your kids and spouse. "
""
"Live like your clock is ticking.  Own your clock."
""
"Do the things you’ll be glad you did when time comes calling. "
""
"And ask your friends to do the same."
""
"#coaching"
"#leadership"
"#motivation"
"#normal40

Ignore the data."
""
"You can find data that will support any decision."
""
"That doesn’t make it correct - or smart."
""
"Data is a luxury."
""
"But data is usually only information in disguise. "
""
"We all try to justify what we’re doing by referring to the data. That’s the safe way."
""
"As a result, we all get wrapped around the axel at some point in the process."
""
"Stop."
""
"If this is you, or your project, or your team, or your career, stop. "
""
"Get out of the weeds; mentally and physically."
""
"Get your mind back to 30,000 foot. Forget the data, but remember what you know and what you’ve learned."
""
"Ask, what is the reality?"
"What do I know for sure?"
"What can I control? "
"Now, where will I place my bet? "
""
" - Ignore the data"
" - Trust your instincts"
" - Make a decision"
" - Learn and be ready to pivot. "
""
"Then you will have the data you need.  "
""
"What will you bet on today? "
""
"#leadership "
"#coaching "
"#motivation "

Success isn’t fast."

"But at least it gets to be messy."

"Every decision you make in life has a consequence. The sooner you get comfortable with that, the faster you will move. "
""
"Some consequences are so large, at least in our mind, that we paralyze ourselves from ever deciding."
""
"At some point you will have a big career opportunity find you. It will show up in the form of the opportunity you thought you always wanted."
""
"But only after it arrives will you consider the trade offs.  "
""
"This is all normal."
""
"When this happens to you, be sure your mind is working for you, not against you, because it will. Your mind hates change, so know that it is biased.  "
""
"Your mind won’t manufacture the obstacles; they are real. But your mind WILL inflate obstacles to be something they are not."
""
"When this happens, find a friend you can trust. "
""
"Go to coffee with a mentor, and get out of your biased head.  "
""
"Know in your gut who you are. Know in your heart who you serve, and get comfortable with the fact that you will crush any obstacle that gets in the way of who you want to be.  "
""
"Then, live with the consequences, unapologetically."

"Own your second-half story. "

"Welcome back to who it is you used to be."

Welcome to the group."
""
"I am the organizer, and I am a dude in midlife."
""
"I arrived into my 40's feeling like I was winning it all. I have a great family. I have a great job.  I've done well financially. I live in a great house and a bustling community.  I have wonderful friends and I'm in good health.  But as I move through my 40's, I'm embarrassed to say that I feel like something is missing.  "
""
"As I've shared these thoughts with other dudes, which in itself took some courage, a few of them thought I was crazy. But other dudes, most dudes, dudes who are equally or more successful than me, quietly admitted they had been feeling the same thing.  "
""
"I created this as a closed community so that dudes can feel comfortable sharing. As a dude, I know that we all want to avoid being at all vulnerable; we can't show that there's any chinks in the armor, so a closed community can help keep the conversation here, where it belongs. "
""
"Are you on a journey like this? Does any of this resonate with you? "
""
"If so, join the conversation."
""
"What can you share about where you are and what you are experiencing?  What have you done that has worked, and what have you done that has not worked."
""
"Show up as you are. Take whatever it is that's here to take. Please also leave whatever it is you have to offer for the rest of us."
""
"Welcome to your Normal 40.

"Simply observing it is a waste of your time. It’s like going to the gym to watch other people work out. "

"1.) Search topics that interest you."

"3.) Be honest in your feedback, favor original content. "

"5.) Post something original of your own. Don’t worry about getting it right. Worry only about getting it started. "

Today I did something I wasn't expecting. "
""
"I wrote a song. "
""
"On my bucket there's an item that reads "Write a song ... that people want to hear." "
""
"Well, I can't be sure that anyone would want to hear it, and being a self-taught guitar player who can't really sing, it's a longshot."
""
"I did it anyway."
""
"The process of writing the song was far more emotional than I anticipated. The song is called "Girl Dad, Forever." And it steps through the relentless passage of time, and the role a dad hopes to play in the life of his daughter. "
""
"If you're a parent you can imagine why this could get teary. You can also appreciate the passage of time, the fact that we can't control it and that, for as long as we're able, we'll be proud to be a Girl Dad, Forever."
""
"This one can't come off my bucket list yet, because I'll probably never muster the courage to play it for anyone."
""
"However, wow, the process of getting my mind still, writing, singing, connecting and and giving thanks for my life's many blessings sure was worth the time I gave to it. And it cost me zero dollars."
""
"Today, I learned the byproduct of getting still and giving thanks for your many blessings is a blessing in itself. "
""
"And that is enough, if we let it be. "
""
"Try it; grab a pen, find 25 minutes, play 6 songs, get your mind still, channel a deep focus and write thanks for anything that comes to mind. Don't write a song, just write a note of thanks. Don't worry about the end, worry about the beginning. The rest falls in."
""
"It will be enough for you, too - if you let it be. "
""

If you work in business long enough, change will find you."
""
"Spoiler alert - every time change found me, it left me better. "
""
"In my 20 years in business:"
""
"- I’ve been a part of a a family business forced to change by a bank. "
""
"- I’ve been on the winning and losing side of elections (where my job was on the line)."
""
"- I’ve worked in a bank that was acquired, and I’ve worked as a bank executive as we acquired another bank."
""
"- I’ve led 8 acquisitions as a public company executive, and I’ve led the profitable carve-out and sale of two non-core business units."
""
"In every one of these transactions people were impacted. Many times it was me."
""
"In your lifetime this will happen to you. When it does, you’ll go through all the same questions and emotions that I’ve been through many times. "
""
"This is normal - totally normal."
""
"All of this experience has taught me a great lesson in the uncertainty of change: I get to choose to see any change as something negative happening to me, or as a great adventure and opportunity happening for me."
""
"Nobody makes that decision for me, I make it."
""
"In your lifetime, when change finds you, it will be your turn to decide if you’re a victim of change, or the benefactor of it. "
""
"You will choose, nobody else. Will you take the role of victim, or that of the owner.  "
""
"Those will be your only two choices. That’s it."
""
"How you answer this one question will have the single greatest impact on your path ahead. Choose well!"
""
"When change happens next for me, I’ll choose to own it. When it happens for you, if you’re not sure, drop me a note and I’d be happy to talk it through."
""

"#leadershipdevelopment "
"#change "

For 30 years, everything you see in this photo was part of a job. Through that lens, everything around me represented a fence to fix, a cow to chase in, hay to put up, rocks to pick or someplace that was hard to get to. Through that lens, this was a place to which I felt I was giving."
""
"Then, overnight, I changed my perspective. I spent 24 hours on this bluff and I got to know it for who it is."
""
"I chose to see it as an adventure that, by and large, has been uninterrupted for generations. I decided to listen to what it was saying as it was waking up. I decided to feel the dew on my feet in the morning sun. And I sat down to smell the wildflowers sitting low on the cool side of the hills. "
""
"Overnight, this became a place that gave to me; it gave me wonder. "
""
"This little bluff that overlooks the aptly named “Preacher’s Run” is on our family farm and ranch, and it may be a little slice of heaven. Ironically, it has been right in front of me this whole time."
""
"We all choose our lenses. Be sure you choose the right lens through which you see your life. Something like this may be sitting all around you, but you have to choose to see it. Don’t wait 30 years to figure it out. That is time you will never get back."
""
"Welcome to your Normal 40.  "
""
"I will post the sounds of the waking prairie in the comments.  It will be the best part of your day. "

"From 1914 to 1918, Flanders Fields, in the north of Belgium, was a major battle theatre on the Western Front during the First World War. ""
"We will not forget, and we will do our part to preserve our freedoms.

Coaching isn’t hard. It’s preparing someone to be coached that is the challenge."
""
"In the last year I’ve authored 38 pages of thoughts from my life’s journey. This morning, in a twist, I spent time ‘reading my mind’ from the pages of notes."
""
"Below is something I wrote about 10 months ago after a long conversation with a highly successful friend and eventual client. He is healthy, he has a loving family, a nice house and a big title. Yet, on the inside, he felt lonely, stalled out, and with a loss fulfillment. He felt an imposter of his own success, and he didn’t know how to change it. Here’s my summary of that day:"
""
"“We need to be better at being us, and feeling like ‘me.’"
"Not the me that you know, the me I used to know."
"We need to get back to who we were."
"We need to get back to how we felt."
"We long to feel like we did when we were on our way to where we used to be going."
"Bring me back, and bring me the courage to do it.”"
""
"At times, we all get down and all feel lonely. We all miss ourselves, and believe life used to feel better."
""
"But we all have the chance to take it back. But you won’t get there alone. "
""
"When you’re ready to go, read a book, call a friend or hire a coach."
""
"It starts with you."
""
"An investment in you always pays dividends."
""

"About a year ago I first posted to LinkedIn. I didn't start with the end in mind, but I started anyway."
" "
"Each week I have felt compelled to share an experience that was decent, mindful and heartfelt. In my perfect world, I also aspired to post about experiences that may be helpful to anyone who cared to read,including my kids."
""
"In the last year, the process has transformed into a journey, my journey. And I’m taking it."
""
"I've learned a lot along the way. I learned that vulurnably-sharing leaves people to wonder what you're doing, what happened or what you're hiding. But as I kept posting, I learned that something deep was connecting, and people started to “like” then comment and then ask for coffee."
""
"I also learned many of us show up here searching for something, but we don’t know what it is. We only know we used to have it, but no longer do. And I learned my posts helped inspire some to embrace a journey of their own."
""
"Lastly, I learned that most of us aren’t searching for more wealth or larger home, we simply want our joy back. The joy we once had."
""
"I have learned a lot, one post at a time. "
" "
"This is your journey; your pursuit of joy. What's your first step?"
" "
"Be brave enough to share."
